> Me : Back to the MHC 1 Gene (not MCHC-typo). I reread what I could find in the blog and am left wondering how directly we trade immune function for cognitive function? Dr. Kruse suggests strongly that our cognitive development is in many ways a byproduct of our immune development and that we have to survive at the immune level as an organism first. However, we have now evolved to where we can easily override this built in preference for physical survival and think/do ourselves to an early and uncomfortable death. Looks like it...Until now, I have seen this as a choice based on larger energy and metabolic considerations. Now, it looks more as though our cognitive abilities simply override our immune priorites. Does this mean we need to de-evolve the brain to where it listens to the immune system again?
>
> Dr. Kruse: you do not trade it.......the immune system evolved first......neurogenesis second. Following the energy equivalence of how things are powered in an atom the valence shell or last shell is most active chemically when energy is involved.......so it is congruent that neurogenesis or cognition and the immune system are the two systems that go awry first when energy is altered in humans.

Amazing.
Brain uses 20 w of power.
Memory can be put on CD, then my great grandson can have conversation with me while I am long gone.
Shove in CD and person with Alzheimer's gains access to the rest of his brain (that is still intact).
Shove in CD and you are expert on QED, Quantum electrodynamics.
Shove in another CD and you are black belt carate master.

Too bad that they currently are working on the mice only.

I have to buy this book.

Link to the full interview is below, but not sure how long the link will work.

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-02-27T19:36:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bumblebees-in-trouble-too.9947/post-112506>

> Honeybees have gotten a lot of sympathy for their mysterious declines. But a recent study has shown that the honey-makers are spreading their worst diseases to their cousins the bumblebees.
>
> [A study published online this week in _Nature_](http://www.nature.com/nature/journal/v506/n7488/full/nature12977.html) clearly revealed that two pathogens that infect honeybees have crossed over into populations of wild bumblebees. ...

This is really bad news for agriculture, of course:

> The loss of honeybees and other pollinators like wild bumblebees could devastate agriculture. [Pollinators are estimated to do nearly $200 billion in "work"](http://scholar.google.com/scholar_url?hl=en&q=http://www.researchgate.net/publication/41621021_Global_pollinator_declines_trends_impacts_and_drivers/file/32bfe5113880d88a8d.pdf&sa=X&scisig=AAGBfm14FbcqY1LMnZClTBFGqeQK24Iiww&oi=scholarr) around the world each year.

[http://news.nationalgeographic.com/...disease-declines-science-pollinators-disease/](http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2014/02/140227-bumblebee-honeybee-disease-declines-science-pollinators-disease/)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/podcast-on-the-eye.9948/post-112516>

Sounds like it may be interesting -- more from the point of view of intellectual history than for current thought, perhaps -- I've got it but I haven't heard it yet:

> The eye has been the subject of research for at least 2500 years. …
>
> In Renaissance Europe the work of thinkers including Kepler and Descartes revolutionised thinking about how the organ worked, but it took several hundred years for the eye to be thoroughly understood.
>
> Melvyn Bragg is joined by Patricia Fara, Senior Tutor of Clare College, University of Cambridge; William Ayliffe, Gresham Professor of Physic \[Physics presumably!\] at Gresham College and Robert Iliffe, Professor of Intellectual History and History of Science at the University of Sussex.

[http://www.bbc.co.uk/podcasts/series/iot](http://www.bbc.co.uk/podcasts/series/iot)

# Thread: Update - 3 Years and Still Searching for an Answer, maybe Dzugan is it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/update-3-years-and-still-searching-for-an-answer-maybe-dzugan-is-it.9950/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 40
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mafixit — 2014-02-27T22:23:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/update-3-years-and-still-searching-for-an-answer-maybe-dzugan-is-it.9950/post-112536>

Update on me -

Still suffering daily headaches and all over joint aches and stiffness. The headaches have gotten to be a pretty constant level 3-4 with peaks around level 8-9. Kept daily diaries - there are no patterns. Tried BHRT and supplements, more trigger point injections (no help), cervical epidural (never again - the steroid side affects were AWFUL), tried Indocin, more acupuncture, weekly massage, and now am in to see a chiro specialist for Atlas adjustment - Atlas Orthogonal. 4 treatments in and little help. Doc says to give it at least 4 weeks. I did notice that with this treatment (or for some other unknown reason), my headaches have switched/moved to pressure in the front - like a sinus headache, but I have no real sinus symptoms.

Right now I am on just Propranolol (just started 3 days ago) - no supplements, no BHRT, no other drugs, nothing. I feel just a shitty on or off any meds, so I am off all others for now. No gluten in my diet. Lots of spring water. No white sugars. Ice my neck almost every night.

Gained a ton of weight back - lost 35 lbs before this all starting in 2011. Gained it all back over the past year. It's hard to exercise with the pain.

I am seriously considering going a round with Dzugan. It's expensive, but I have already spent so much money with the Mayo Clinic and other docs. I also switched to the University of Arizona Integrative Health Center for my primary care doc. They will look at the whole me, not just give me an anti-depressant and send me on my way. Within this practice I also get to meet with a nutritionist, a heath coach, a mind-body practioner and take yoga, tai chi, nutrition and other classes. The center was started by Dr. Andrew Weil. They just opened here in Phoenix last year.

I also saw a new neurologist outside of Mayo (he was the one who put me on the Indocin). The Indocin gave me worse headaches, so we pulled that and he wants me on Inderol. Last week when I saw him he made me feel like we were out of options, with the exception of occipital nerve blocks, which my insurance does not cover.

I know my body has changed again because my hot flashes are minimal, but my menstrual cycle is a joke. 130 days , 19 days 40 days, 77 days, etc. My breasts are tender and I am sleeping like crap again. My last cycle my ovaries were screaming so bad I was ready to go to the Urgent Care. Even if I do sleep through the night, I never feel rested.

So I have run full circle and am back to the beginning. My husband wants me back and I want to be back. I know some of you are using Dzugan. It is worth the $$$$?

Karen from AZ.

### Lahelada — 2014-02-27T22:36:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/update-3-years-and-still-searching-for-an-answer-maybe-dzugan-is-it.9950/post-112537>

I wonder if iodine supplementation might be helpful. I am new to it but there is a monster thread on here. What makes me wonder is your comment about "my ovaries were screaming". I remember Dr K relating a story from his residency where a woman was supposed to be examined and every time her breast were touched she was in pain. An application of Lugol to the vulva sorted the problem.Iodine is critical for every cell but especially for the sexual organs.Whether the headaches fit in to the picture I cannot tell. Maybe read the Dr Brownstein book??

### Shijin13 — 2014-02-28T00:06:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/update-3-years-and-still-searching-for-an-answer-maybe-dzugan-is-it.9950/post-112546>

Karen

I've had such great success with DzLogic (Dzugan's new program) PM me if you want to talk to Linda Mchale - I've got her # and she can walk you through Dzugan's program.... Its taken me two years - but I'm 98% migraine Free! the 2% have been due to cross contamination of wheat/gluten when eating out. I will say it won't happen over night - but he will treat you holistically.

I do love AO chiropractic - they helped me to a point but never fully cleared my migraines.... unfortunately my AO chiro left the area and I had to find a new Chiro. fortunately I found one who practices the same techniques my 1st Chiro did, when I was seeing him at the Air Force Academy - he treated cadets for free b/c he was a '73 grad!

### Eugenia — 2014-02-28T01:15:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/update-3-years-and-still-searching-for-an-answer-maybe-dzugan-is-it.9950/post-112552>

I don't want to sound negative, but have you done an MRI to see if you have a tumor? If these headaches don't go away no matter what, either you're allergic to a lot of things (have you followed a non-ketogenic Paleo diet, that takes out all grains, processed foods, chemicals, sugar?), or something more sinister is going on. How about heavy metals?

\>>

If you have low:
cortisol AM
iodine
selenium
vit D

progesterone
vit A
DHEAs

high cortisol at midnight
high estradiol

correct all that first, then see where that puts you.

If you work in place full of electronics, change jobs or spend rest of the day in bathtub full of ice and cold water.

If you have a spare time and do not know what to do, go and take shower, use cold water only, longer better.

\----------------------------------
No high and medium GI carbohydrates.
No hydrogenated oils, or high fructose.
Weight animal meats that you eat, replace half or 3/4 with seafood.

### mafixit — 2014-03-03T23:24:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/update-3-years-and-still-searching-for-an-answer-maybe-dzugan-is-it.9950/post-113206>

> [Eugenia said:](/goto/post?id=112552)
>
> I don't want to sound negative, but have you done an MRI to see if you have a tumor? If these headaches don't go away no matter what, either you're allergic to a lot of things (have you followed a non-ketogenic Paleo diet, that takes out all grains, processed foods, chemicals, sugar?), or something more sinister is going on. How about heavy metals?

Yes, I have had 2 MRIs, and MRA/MRV, CT Scan of my sinuses, Vestibular Testing (twice), Xrays from my Chiro, Physical Therapy, Acupuncture, BHRT, Massage Therapy. Been tested for Heavy Metals. Removed all gluten. ALL tests have come out "normal". Had food intolerances and allergies, and cut all those out.

Going for my 6th round of Atlas Orthogonal. I feel "good" as soon as I leave there, after I am adjusted and iced up for 1/2 hour. Then after the ice wears off it all comes back.

My dizziness and headaches get worse after the day goes on. I generally wake up with 10% of my symptoms, then about an hour into my day, it starts to flare up.

Today I sit here with a low grade headache, dizziness/disequilibrium, numbness on the left side from my jaw line to my temple, fullness in my left ear and a neck ache on the left side.

I am on Day 2 of Propranolol 2x per day.

### mafixit — 2014-03-26T17:42:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/update-3-years-and-still-searching-for-an-answer-maybe-dzugan-is-it.9950/post-118067>

Update - had my labs done today for Dzugan. I will post them as soon as I have them.

Headache has been so bad that I went to Mayo for 3 days of outpatient IV treatment this past weekend. A nice cocktail of magnesium, toridal, reglan, ativan and benedryl. Worked fine while I was drugged. As soon as the drugs wore off and I woke up the headache came back. Felt like someone had whacked on the back of my head with a baseball bat while squeezing it in a vice.

Yesterday I went to my pain doctor. He gave me injections in the sub occipital nerves in the back of my head. Whoop! Pain went away while the Lidocaine was active. Still had pressure, but pain was 80% better. Headache came and went throughout the day yesterday after the Lidocaine wore off. Seems maybe a diagnosis of Occipital Neuralgia is correct? Shots did nothing for the pain in my trigger points on the back of my skull or the constant pain in the left side of my neck and shoulder, nor the almost constant pressure headache I have.

Now my question, what could cause this and what more can I do to fix it? I am seeing an Osteopath now once per week, and doing some stretching exercises. Neurologist has me trying Atenolol, which I started last night. Blood pressure dropped like a stone after one dose.

Eating clean, more tuna, salmon, veggies. No sugar, no artificial sweeteners, no gluten, processed foods. 64 ounces on spring water daily. Taking 400 mg of magnesium and 2000 IU of D3 daily. I have noticed since being on the D3 that I am not nearly as tired during the day.

Karen from Arizona

I rarely take medications, so I know these are not rebound headaches. OTC stuff doesn't work anyway, so I stay away from it. Once in a great while I go over the edge and need some help to bring the discomfort down to bearable. I have gone weeks without taking anything. And if I ever do take something, it's maybe one or two times per week.

I do use heat on one and and ice on another. Haven't tried the hot bath thing but I do have a hot tub. I will get in it halfway and use ice on the top to see if that helps. It's a great idea and I don't have to wait to fill up the tub.

Cindy,

I have never had an echocardiogram, but have had EKGs which are all normal. Not one of the numerous docs I have seen ever considered a heart issue.

I did start Atenolol and ordered a Cefaly device on the advice of my headache neurologist.

2 more weeks and I should be starting on the Dzugan plan.

Karen from Arizona

progesterone~14-15 pg/ml

You started menses 3 days after blood draw, one would then expect ideal
progesterone~5 pg/ml
Estradiol~(80-100)pg/mL

You got
progesterone~0.4 pg/ml
Estradiol~(105)pg/mL

Your estradiol is on the mark
your progesterone is super very low
you should newer ever have progesterone less than 1.5pg/mL

Your pregnenolone =13 should be closer to 188, but 200 is ok possibly more
use pregnenolone MLM as many pills as need,
that should improve

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-22T13:51:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/update-3-years-and-still-searching-for-an-answer-maybe-dzugan-is-it.9950/post-122989>

DHEA = IL 6 = sleep = autophagy efficiency..........when it is bad you are losing electrons in a big way...........when autophagy is bad mitochondria are swelling and you are using more apoptosis which is mitochondrial suicide and not renewal........autophagy is how mitochondria reorganize and renew.

When you lose one electron in mito =superoxide. This is OK because it is a signal of what to do
When your lose two electrons in mito = H2O2 can be OK or bad depending on context
When you lose three of more in mito - Fenton reaction or a build up of hydroxyl free radicals........always bad. This causes massive tissue damage = low DHEA

Read the blog about Fenton reactions. Quantum Autism has a real detailed area on this where I discuss this.

Normally the only place a normal hydroxyl free radical is used in biology is in macrophages that kill bacteria. This occurs within the confines of these cells and not in tissues.
When they occur in tissues constantly due to non native EMF collagen is destroyed and unzipped.
When Non native EMF interacts with "dislodged transition metal from un zipped collagen it cause the Fenton reaction to occur. This is why unzipping proteins is a big problem in our modern world. It frees the transition metal to interact with the higher powered non native EMF. What happens? the energy in the EMF is transferred to the electrons in the D shell of these metals to cause ionization of the metal or the remnant unzipped protein fragments......this changes their chemistry in you.........and it makes hydroxyl free radicals we can not clear well.

The hydroxyl radical can damage virtually all types of macromolecules by energizing their molecular structures using the quanta of energy to alter its structure and function: This is true of proteins, carbohydrates, nucleic acids (mutations), lipids (lipid peroxidation) and amino acids coded for by DNA and RNA.

The hydroxyl radical has a very short _in vivo_ half-life of approximately 10−9 seconds and a high reactivity. This makes it a very dangerous compound to any living organisms mitochondrial membranes or its cell membranes. It causes you to lose electrons in massive numbers.

Unlike superoxide, which can be detoxified by superoxide dismutase, the hydroxyl radical **cannot** be eliminated by an enzymatic reaction. Mechanisms for scavenging peroxyl radicals for the protection of cellular structures includes endogenous antioxidants such as melatonin and glutathione.

This is precisely how non native EMF destroys your redox potential everywhere.

Have you considered overactive yeast / candida?

BG 9 gives some clues. [http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-9-what-really-killed-michael-jackson/](http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-9-what-really-killed-michael-jackson/)

_But what are the symptoms of a candida infection and altered gut micro-biome? Symptoms vary wildly between people because their gut flora does as well, and this makes it a tough diagnosis for many physicians. Most MD’s are taught to look for the low hanging fruit symptoms, like a white patch on mucous membranes that can be scrapped off to show a red map-like covering of the surface. We see this is in the aero-digestive tract and in the genitourinary system often. The more “**zebra like**” symptoms can be chronic fatigue and loss of energy. They can be related to depression and progress to regular or migraine headaches, bloating, excessive gas as well_

> Supported market editors and fashion assistants across all product categories including Men’s and Women’s Ready-to-Wear

### Hope — 2014-02-28T02:49:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ppl-with-mold-issues-check-out-this-c4a-story.9952/post-112561>

Discovered this online...amazing. I bet the way better redox potential in this environment plays a huge role in this. How I wish I could do this too...

Here goes...oh and it's not me of course. But wow. Wish it were.

My c4a a little over a year ago was 21,000 and I was actually feeling better at that point than I had a year previously. I"m sure I was still dumping toxin at a pretty good rate. The last c4a was 451 at the beginning of this year, and I have also regained an immense amount of mental function in the last year. I attribute a lot of it to living in pure mountain air at 8000 feet last summer. Not so much to medications, but we are all different. I consider pure air at 8000 feet to be mold-safe housing, keeping the posts on topic for the group

It was done almost entirely with extreme avoidance. I wish more doctors would get on board with that; they continue to let people spend money on treatment without taking any action at all to help them with what they really need, which is mold-safe housing.

Moldies actually "emanate toxins" for a while after they get out of exposure, and they can actually contaminate their own clothing and bed clothing. If the toxins can be washed out (most can, but not all) that stuff can just be repeatedly washed, but the key is to not own anything you can't get in the washing machine.

### Lahelada — 2014-02-28T02:52:22+0200

### Hope — 2014-02-28T02:55:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ppl-with-mold-issues-check-out-this-c4a-story.9952/post-112563>

OH, if you had flooding, I'd get it checked out somehow. I can tell you, surviving mold.com has some great info on this, though. They just changed the site so i don't know where it is, but check it out...there are great ideas on how to see if you have mold. If anything, can you get a moisture meter? Do you smell anything? I am SO SO sensitive, I will smell anything. But there can be mold even if you don't smell it. Of course RP is everything..

### Lahelada — 2014-02-28T02:58:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ppl-with-mold-issues-check-out-this-c4a-story.9952/post-112565>

It happened in April last year and only recntly we have got rid of the smell withthe hot weather and aid of the air conditioning.We shall see what happens when the cooler weather comes in.If the smell returns...

### Hope — 2014-02-28T03:00:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ppl-with-mold-issues-check-out-this-c4a-story.9952/post-112568>

Lahe, wow. You are fortunate not to be affected. I got home tonight and bam, brain fog, stuffy nose...ugh. I wanna live outside now too. But some stuff I don't know how to have the energy for. Unless I am at the beach. See this:

Extreme avoidance is one way of living mold-safe. People who are severe reactors often end up practicing extreme avoidance. Tenting, cargo trailer, small travel trailer, even some living out of their cars. It means avoiding mold in an extreme sense, staying out of housing/buildings/situations that are not pristine. Most of us cannot find housing that is free enough of mold that it doesn't continue to make us sick. So in my case I bought the Camplite. I bought metal kitchen tools and new clothing and bedding. I do not use the cushions that came with it but put my own pads down on the benches, so I can replace them if they develop a problem. I have mostly new clothing. I have "inside" clothing and "outside" clothing, and once any clothing has been inside my car or inside a building, I don't wear it inside the trailer except to take it off and bag it prior to a shower. After having been in the car or in a building, when I get home, I remove all clothing, bag it for washing, and shower and wash hair. After that I put on clean "inside" clothing and proceed from there. If I forget something at the grocery, I will generally do without it rather than go through the whole routine again. I try to only do one shower a day, and sometimes that means I spend quite a few hours outside between appointments.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-28T14:21:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/february-2014-q-a.9953/post-112626>

Caroline life is what you make it. You're going to mess up sometimes, it's a universal truth. But the good part is you get to decide how you're going to mess it up. For example take people. Some will be your friends - they'll act like it anyway. Remember, some come, and some go. The ones that stay with you through everything - they're your true friends. Don't let go of them no matter what. The analogy for life? That is a Maxwell demon protein coded for on DNA. These proteins are so good to your zero entropy machine that your body has decided to conserve it in its code of life. The same is NOT true with all the other parts 3 legged stool. They are not conserved because they have always been around and been something life has counted on to organized around. These days, it can not count on two of the three. The key metric is to understand how we are constructed so that when things go awry in your wellness, you'll know why before your doc does. Moreever, you''ll know what to do this time. So keep your head high, keep your chin up, and most importantly, keep smiling, because life's a beautiful thing and there's so much to smile about.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-28T14:31:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/february-2014-q-a.9953/post-112628>

yewwei.tan TI's break supersymmetry in two ways. This is what makes them special. Stanford University is studying these effects right now with a certain man made TI made of transition metals. The material is called bismuth tellurochloride, or BiTeCl. It turns out that BiTeCl is a [topological insulator](http://phys.org/tags/topological+insulators/) that conduct electrical current with perfect efficiency on its surfaces, but not through their middles. They hope to exploit their unusual properties to create "spintronic" devices that use the electron's spin, rather than just its charge, to carry energy and information with 100 percent efficiency and at room temperature. See a semiconductor works best in cold.......but a TI can work with this efficency at room temp. This is where life finds itself. Physics has not even woken up to this realization yet. They will.

In conventional electronic chips, two types of semiconducting materials come together to form transistors and diodes. N-type semiconductors have an excess of electrons and are highly conductive. P-type materials are electron-depleted, leaving positively charged "holes" where the electrons used to be. I talked about this int he Q & A.
Where these two types of [materials](http://phys.org/tags/materials/) meet in a "p-n junction," electrons flow from the n to the player in a robust one-way current. (Rectified current: doping mechanism)
Most topological insulators operate as either a p-type or n-type material on both top and bottom surfaces. **But BiTeCl is asymmetric**: p-type on its top surface and n-type on its bottom.

This means the edges of the material could function as p-n junctions – or even many microscopic p-n junctions layered on top of each other. Even better, when the material is placed in a magnetic field, these p-n junctions develop unique edge channels that can conduct electricity with zero resistance. This gives them tremendous capability compared to a regular semiconductor.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-28T14:47:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/february-2014-q-a.9953/post-112629>

This unique arrangement of the TI can demonstrate many other phenomena. For instance, placing it in a static electric field (like Earth) can induce useful magnetic properties in the material, a phenomenon known as the topological magneto-electric effect. A TI also could allow us to use an electric charge to induce magnetic monopoles. Magnetic monopoles (think unipolar magnetic now) are "theorized magnets" that have just one pole, north or south, rather than the usual two. Why would you want that? This exotic magnetic state can do very practical work, such as storing information on a hard drive. Sounds a bit like how human memory works huh? So braking symmetry is huge. TI's allow it. To date magnetic monopoles have never been found as a fundamental particle in physics.

To make a real life magnetic monopole requires violating two fundamental symmetries: one which is known as inversion symmetry, and time reversal symmetry is the second. I spoke briefly about them last night. Time reversal symmetry is a process that should look the same going forward or backward in time. It is invariant with respect to time. Life does this already. Breaking both symmetries at once is difficult, but I think life's use of TI's does just that. So how did life do it? It requires applying a [magnetic field](http://phys.org/tags/magnetic+field/) perpendicularly but in opposite directions on the top and bottom of a thin film of a TI. I have a sense this is why the Earth magnetic field oscillates and is not constant. I mentioned this in Paul's question last night. Using BiTeCl, scientists at Stanford have already broken the first symmetry, namely inversion symmetry. Once you break one symmetry using leptons it will be straightforward to break the second one with experiments. Once they find the magnetic monopole they will see that life uses these principle in the brain in my humble opinion. All the parts to do this are in our heads. Physics is just using things they understand better now to show how these things work. we already have the most amazing memory in our heads that breaks both symmetries.......so we just have to get people to translate the science of one to the other.

### nicld — 2014-02-28T17:31:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/february-2014-q-a.9953/post-112661>

The big thing for me after listening to the webinar last night, I realized that I do need to take a step back a bit and work on certain things that might be just my n=1 and not think that everything that is talked about I need to do. I have done the general things that EVERYBODY should do like reducing EMF’s (but I could get better at that), tons of CT, blue blocker glasses, lots of oxy, working on the hormone issues and eating more epi-paleo.

When Jack was talking about Seahorse going to a tanning salon and seeing how that effected her, I thought I should try that but then I realized that is not my “big” issue that I need to work on right now. My “big” issue is my gut that has been crap for over 10 years now. With the help of Dr. Tim, I am hoping to get this fixed and work on methythalation issues too.

It is challenge to not try everything and anything to fix yourself NOW. It is a long journey with lots of ups and downs but I’ll get there.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-28T22:01:23+0200

This life is what you make it. No matter what, you're going to mess up sometimes, it's a universal truth. But the good part is you get to decide how you're going to mess it up. Girls will be your friends - they'll act like it anyway. But just remember, some come, some go. The ones that stay with you through everything - they're your true best friends. Don't let go of them. The same is true with the 3 legged stool. Also remember, sisters make the best friends in the world. As for lovers, well, they'll come and go too. And, I hate to say it, most of them - actually pretty much all of them are going to break your heart, but you can't give up because if you give up, you'll never find your soulmate. You'll never find that half who makes you whole and that goes for everything. Just because you fail once, doesn't mean you're gonna fail at everything. Keep trying, hold on, and always, always, always believe in yourself, because if you don't, then who will? So keep your head high, keep your chin up, and most importantly, keep smiling, because life's a beautiful thing and there's so much to smile about.

# Thread: ketosis, thirst
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-thirst.9956/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Julie5 — 2014-02-28T15:53:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-thirst.9956/post-112639>

I understand that I need to be in ketosis. I am eating the 50 g of protein in my BAB and maybe 30 or so g in my afternoon meal. When I test for ketones, the ketostix show that I have trace ketones. Is that being sufficiently ketotic, or do I need to further tweak my diet?

I do use a bit of hard cheese now and then and organic cream. I am not strictly tracking my carbs, but I do eat a serving of veggies every day: combos of onion, mushrooms, cauliflower, broccoli, cabbage,brussels sprouts. Not all at the same time, however. Also I eat coconut fat bombs, which have flaked unsweetened coconut and coconut cream, as well as coconut oil.

My other issue is thirst. I am drinking a LOT of water, but I am thirsty all the time. Not just a little, but very. I am controlling my EMF to the degree hubby will let me, which really only amounts to dimmer lighting at night, using goggles, no power or devices in our bedroom at night. So the question for me is, besides water, what more do I need to do to quench the thirst?

### nonchalant — 2014-02-28T16:10:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-thirst.9956/post-112640>

Hi Julie5, have you read the Redox Rx blogpost? EMF is a big issue for trying to get hydrated, unfortunately. But also you could eat lots of seafood, egg yolks, and gently cooked pork for cysteine. Cysteine is needed to make collagen, which is needed to hang onto water. Your sulfurous veggies also have some cysteine.

Ketosis and hydration are linked. Finding trace ketones is good, however, and I believe you are doing fine on your diet otherwise. Are you drinking RO water? The only other thing I can think of is potassium, which is needed to attach to proteins at the same time water attaches to the protein. You also need ATP, to unfold that protein in the first place. Sunlight would help the unfolding process by providing direct energy.

### nicld — 2014-02-28T16:59:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-thirst.9956/post-112653>

My N=1 is that I too eat a pretty ketogenic diet but I can hardly get the strips to read. I have found that it all has to do with how hydrated I am. The one day I did not drink that much water (I know, bad) my strips really showed good ketosis. I have realized that if I am drinking the massive amounts of water that one needs, the pee will be just so diluted that the strips may not show anything.

### Inger — 2014-02-28T20:18:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-thirst.9956/post-112696>

Do you measure blood ketones or urine? I guess the urine does not tell much.... from what I have read it could as well be you are just good in using them so you do not pee them out. Or you eat too much carbs / protein. But I would do the blood ketone test if any...

Is your water good quality?

I was much thirsty in the beginning of my ketotic diet many years ago... the increased thirst disappeared after a while. At least it did for me.. I just drank a lot! I now too feel it if I have forgot to drink - I get dryish mouth and feel dehydrated (especially if I am inside or get hot - I have a pretty high temp so I guess that is why I need much water too). So I guess it is good the body reminds us of drinking

Cooked meat/fish makes me way thirstier than raw - no idea why?!

Somewhere I read some people might be able to hold on water better if drinking a small glass of salt water in the morning followed by lots of fresh water. IDK tho if this is real You could experiment. I sometimes do this if I take Iodine (salt flush) Do not add salt to your water otherwise tho! Jack has said not a good idea.

You should count your carbs once in a while? At least in the beginning. Veggies can have quite a bit of carbs... at least certain veggies like cooked carrots etc. so if you have lots of them it could get above 20 grams / day easily...

### Julie5 — 2014-03-01T04:00:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-thirst.9956/post-112775>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=112696)
>
> Do you measure blood ketones or urine? I guess the urine does not tell much.... from what I have read it could as well be you are just good in using them so you do not pee them out. Or you eat too much carbs / protein. But I would do the blood ketone test if any...
>
> Is your water good quality?
>
> You should count your carbs once in a while? At least in the beginning. Veggies can have quite a bit of carbs... at least certain veggies like cooked carrots etc. so if you have lots of them it could get above 20 grams / day easily...

Inger, yes, our water quality is excellent. We have a country well AND an RO. No fluoride issues here. I know that most days I am doing well with the carbs. Other days I think I am pushing it, but haven't counted. It's interesting that drinking more water dilutes the ketones. I know I am eating lots of protein, but Jack told me not to worry about it converting to glucose. I get about 80 g. of protein a day.

### Linz — 2014-03-01T11:12:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-thirst.9956/post-112810>

Perhaps it is the structured water in the raw food that works.

Julie, do you drink the water cold. It has optimal structure at 4 degrees. I don't like the taste of room temp water any more but just a little icy water stops me feeling thirsty.

### Da-mo — 2014-03-01T21:02:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-thirst.9956/post-112877>

> [Linz said:](/goto/post?id=112810)
>
> It has optimal structure at 4 degrees.

Now that rings a bell . . . . I remember learning long ago that water reaches maximum density at 4degC. Above or below that density decreases.

### QiGuy1997 — 2014-03-02T03:47:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-thirst.9956/post-112993>

I never understood this. Doesn't the water quickly just warm-up in the stomach?

### Linz — 2014-03-04T11:12:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-thirst.9956/post-113308>

Qi, i think structured water = more energy. The water charge separates to form a molecular arrangement that can absorb more energy and actually excludes some toxins. Cold water, bone broth, fermented veg, raw are all ways to increase structured water I think, and perhaps it is stable enough to withstand warming.

### nonchalant — 2014-03-04T18:34:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketosis-thirst.9956/post-113377>

### sjoshua — 2014-02-28T23:02:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/theory-on-top-end-performance-and-the-ppp.9960/post-112726>

I've been browsing the interwebs and it seems the research surrounding "fat burning" individuals always tends to find that humans lose their "top end" sprint performance when they forgo carbs. It seems fairly universal among the n=1 testing and the scholarly findings.

The PPP's evolutionary intent was to function in cold climates. With that in mind, I believe that a "proper" test of fat adapted transitions would mandate environmental control.

My theory is a keto human with efficient redox _in the right environment_ (i.e. cold & ideally natural - such as artic circle adventures) would be able to match or beat their warm adapted/carb sprint performance hands down.

Anyone have some $$ around to sponsor a research study??

# Thread: A Little Perspective On How "New" This All Is or Is Not.....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-perspective-on-how-new-this-all-is-or-is-not.9962/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2014-02-28T23:58:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-little-perspective-on-how-new-this-all-is-or-is-not.9962/post-112735>

There was this doctor named Sun Si Miao born in 581 AD who lived 99 years....

He noted that thyroid/goiter was treated well with seaweeds and the thyroids of animals:

> Sun Simiao is credited with recognizing that goiter occurred in mountainous regions and could be cured by prescribing both seaweeds (which contain iodine) and thyroid glands (which contain thyroid hormone) from deer and sheep. He successfully treated night blindness with livers from oxen and sheep, which contain vitamin A, and treated beriberi (leg edema due to vitamin B1 deficiency) by using unpolished rice (the outer layer of rice and other grains are rich in B vitamins), based on Buddhist practices from India. Sun also contributed to the utilization of placenta for treatment of weakness as well as for regulating menstruation and relieving difficult labor. He presented 25 formulas for treatment of malaria, 17 of them containing _changshan_ (dichroa). Some Chinese authorities have suggested that Sun Simiao authored the ophthalmology classic _Yinhai Jingwei_, but the evidence supports a much later date for the work, at least during the Song Dynasty, perhaps afterward (7). Nonetheless, Sun devoted an entire volume of his work to ophthalmology and is credited with being the first to elaborate the causes and treatments for ophthalmic disorders.

[http://www.itmonline.org/arts/sunsimiao.htm](http://www.itmonline.org/arts/sunsimiao.htm)

He wrote a textbook on Medicine that included peventive health and wellness as well as Women prominently and noted that treating them was a bit more difficult...

> A few points are worth mentioning: First, one of the greatest innovations in Sun Simiao’s encyclopaedia was the eminent position accorded to gynaecology. Earlier texts like the Essentials from the Golden Coffer (Jin Gui Yao Lüe 金匱要略, composed in the Eastern Han period by Zhang Zhongjing 張仲景) or On the Origins and Symptoms of the Various Diseases (Zhu Bing Yuan Hou Lun 諸病源候論, composed in 610 by Chao Yuanfang 巢元方) contained brief sections on disorders and treatments specifically targeting the female body, but these were always placed, like an afterthought, at the very end of the text, to be followed only by paediatrics. Information on diagnosing or treating the female body in the other Han dynasty classics (Inner Classic of the Yellow Emperor \[Huang Di Nei Jing\], Classic of Difficult Issues \[Nan Jing\], Treatise on Cold Damage \[Shang Han Lun\]) was minimal and limited to fleeting comments. By contrast, Sun Simiao placed his section on _**‘Prescriptions for Women’ at the very beginning, after the introductory scroll. Moreover, he began his gynaecological section with an essay that emphasised the need for any ‘gentleman engaged in the art of nurturing life’ to devote particular attention to this topic because ‘women are ten times more difficult to treat than men’, and yet are the mainstay of the family because of their pivotal role in reproduction. As a consequence, he devoted a full three scrolls, or roughly ten percent of his Essential Prescriptions to this topic and eloquently argued for the need for ‘separate prescriptions’ and special care for women’s bodies and minds.**_ He thereby set the stage for the fully-fledged emergence of gynaecology as a respected medical speciality in the official, government-sponsored system of medical training, research and publications in the Song dynasty several centuries later.
> Related to this, I would argue that Sun Simiao radically altered the meaning of a key term in Chinese medicine, namely ‘nurturing life’ (yang sheng 養生). In the early history of Chinese medicine, the notion of health and longevity, achieved and maintained by personal cultivation, was the overarching goal, the pivot around which elite medical authors contemplated and composed medical literature, especially in the context of materia medica and theoretical literature. This concept included such practices as dietetics, alchemy, a reclusive lifestyle, gymnastic and breathing exercises to stimulate the circulation of qi throughout the body, sexual cultivation (which, while emphasising the female orgasm as an essential component of complete intercourse, aimed at the male practitioner’s health benefits) and visualisation meditation. By reinterpreting the meaning of ‘life’ in a social, moral and cosmological context, Sun Simiao was, to my knowledge, the first author to widen this circumscribed set of individual practices, centred on improving the individual practitioner’s health and prolonging life. He included the female body in the larger perspective of life beyond just the individual body, as well as the survival of the family for generations to come, and ultimately the altruistic ideal of benefiting society and the macrocosm at large.

[http://www.jcm.co.uk/product/catalo...ugs-transforming-bodies-and-cultivating-life/](http://www.jcm.co.uk/product/catalog/product/view/10293/nurturing-life-in-classical-chinese-medicine-sun-simiao-on-healing-without-drugs-transforming-bodies-and-cultivating-life/)

Just thought I would mention it....No EMF that we know of.....


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Folic acid supplementation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/folic-acid-supplementation.9964/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-01T01:29:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/folic-acid-supplementation.9964/post-112751>

Most docs know that folic acid (400mcgs) is given by OB gyn's to prevent caudal regression syndrome and spinal dysraphisms. You also may not know if the mother is a diabetic the child has a risk of having sacral agenesis. This linkage has been been behind the pushing of synthetic folic acid derivatives. What is not appreciated it what happens when the mother and child harbor SNP's to MTHFR while they are given these supplements. Here is a nice read to update things.

[http://www.uni.edu/desoto/FolicAcidASD.pdf](http://www.uni.edu/desoto/FolicAcidASD.pdf)

### SeaHorse — 2014-03-01T04:02:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/folic-acid-supplementation.9964/post-112776>

My daughter and I are living proof of that particular folly.

### fitness@home — 2014-03-01T04:02:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/folic-acid-supplementation.9964/post-112777>

I have discussed the importance of taking folinic acid with both of my daughters, rather than folic acid which is in most multis and prenatal vitamins. Have even went so far as to purchase multivitamins from Thorne Research for them with the correct form in it.

My generation wasn't aware of this or MTHFR. I'm making sure they are better prepared. Hope to have grand babies in the next few years

### Phil Möhrle — 2023-03-31T17:22:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/folic-acid-supplementation.9964/post-320348>

Hello,
could somebody comment my thoughts here. Folat: nature, synthetic in context of UV (QE 19)

If the deficiency of folat is so enormous, why does nature use this kind of folat (natural one). In context of UV light: If lights interact with folat in nature and therby destroys it? Should there be not more deficiencies or deseases around the equator if UV light turns of folat in food?

Is it that the synthetic one can just not be stwitched out by light and this can lead to troubles due to epigentic switches 24/7 365.
Germany, people eating cornflakes (synthetic) having to less, to weak sun...We activate things to a wrong time...
Conclusion: Folat is not that important? It just has to switched on and off at the right time?

# Thread: What is a "Soul Mate".....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-a-soul-mate.9966/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 35
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2014-03-01T10:33:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-a-soul-mate.9966/post-112801>

What is a "soul mate" exactly? What does that mean to you? Have you been lucky enough to have a soul mate?
Is it something that we all think we should have? something we desire? but is it elusive - just out of our grasp? Just a figment of our imagination?

Is this a girl thing? do guys think in these terms at all?

Is this something that we realise one day we have and then work to protect and nourish and appreciate .......

or is it a spontaneous event .... that hits us between the eyes like a thunderbolt .....

is it quantum entanglement that engulfs us from head to toe whether we are ready or not......

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-01T12:34:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-a-soul-mate.9966/post-112817>

I've heard of a "soul friend". The Irish is _anam cara_. These were a practice in the ancient Celtic Christianty.

[http://celtic-spirituality.net/what-is-christian-celtic-spirituality/](http://celtic-spirituality.net/what-is-christian-celtic-spirituality/)

As I understand it, you'd get advice and encouragement from the soul friend. This wasn't the same as your confessor. It was someone who "walks with you". (The Irish, by the way, invented the practice of private confession, a humane innovation for which Western civilisation should be grateful to them. In the Early Church confession was public. _Cripes!_ )

This guy, an Orthodox priest from New Mexico, but with an interest in the old Celtic culture and its practices, is quite interesting on all this:

[http://www.ancientfaith.com/podcasts/highdesert/celtic\_christianity\_part\_1](http://www.ancientfaith.com/podcasts/highdesert/celtic_christianity_part_1)

### prAna303 — 2014-03-01T16:23:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-a-soul-mate.9966/post-112827>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=112801)
>
> What is a "soul mate" exactly? What does that mean to you? Have you been lucky enough to have a soul mate?
> Is it something that we all think we should have? something we desire? but is it elusive - just out of our grasp? Just a figment of our imagination?
>
> Is this a girl thing? do guys think in these terms at all?
>
> Is this something that we realise one day we have and then work to protect and nourish and appreciate .......
>
> or is it a spontaneous event .... that hits us between the eyes like a thunderbolt .....
>
> is it quantum entanglement that engulfs us from head to toe whether we are ready or not......

Yes i have a soul mate, that one is always there no matter what. When i am happy we share those moments, when sad my mate can lift me and push me, when tired my mate gives me peace, the list goes on and on...

That special soul mate is: ME!

### sooperb — 2014-03-01T16:39:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-a-soul-mate.9966/post-112828>

A soul mate. I once saw and bought a fridge magnet that sums it up for me, just substitute the word soulmate for friend:

"A friend is someone who understands your past, believes in your future, and accepts you the way you are today."

Somewhere in there too is a depth of understanding that we don't get from passing strangers, an acceptance that we can and do fail but who don't berate us for it and a capacity to share that goes beyond the call of duty. Such liaisons are rare I think, most people are far too selfish to exchange a little bit of their soul with another, we are conditioned to protect our emotions and psyche from too much damage. When you find such a person you will know

### SeaHorse — 2014-03-01T16:51:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-a-soul-mate.9966/post-112830>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=112827)
>
> Yes i have a soul mate, that one is always there no matter what. When i am happy we share those moments, when sad my mate can lift me and push me, when tired my mate gives me peace, the list goes on and on...
>
> That special soul mate is: ME!

Amen to that!!

Once we become true partners with ourselves....then we can meet another traveller on the same road and recognize a soulmate. Many of us try to find a person to plug the holes we alone are responsible for filling. "I need you" is the kiss of death.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-01T17:55:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-a-soul-mate.9966/post-112845>

### Josh — 2014-03-02T02:05:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-a-soul-mate.9966/post-112964>

> by Kabir
> translated by [Robert Bly](http://www.poets.org/poet.php/prmPID/280)
> I talk to my inner lover, and I say, why such
> rush?
> We sense that there is some sort of spirit that loves
> birds and animals and the ants--
> perhaps the same one who gave a radiance to you in
> your mother's womb.
> Is it logical you would be walking around entirely
> orphaned now?
> The truth is you turned away yourself,
> and decided to go into the dark alone.
> Now you are tangled up in others, and have forgotten
> what you once knew,
> and that's why everything you do has some weird
> failure in it.
> \- See more at: [http://www.poets.org/viewmedia.php/prmMID/16756#sthash.ir8SMNRf.dpuf](http://www.poets.org/viewmedia.php/prmMID/16756#sthash.ir8SMNRf.dpuf)

### tellmisty — 2014-03-03T00:26:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-a-soul-mate.9966/post-113117>

At this stage of my life, I've extracted the concept of "soul mate" and "life partner" from each other ... I don't romanticize the notion of a life partner like I used to, as I have dated so many men who are great men, there may have been chemistry, we may even be in love, but a true partner they will never be. They are a grown-up child to take care of, incapable of giving or "partnering" in any kind of equal or reciprocal way without a lot of nagging or pleading or pestering. I adore them, but love does not equal partnership.

I am so lucky, though, to have several relationships that I feel are true soul mates ... people with whom you connect so deeply, who reflect back to you the holiness of your own soul and your journey, who honor and respect and love the person you are becoming, who are supportive and kind and just "get" you. My connection with these few are so deep and abiding, and frankly, holier and more enduring than anything I've ever experienced with a romantic partner.

To find partnership and soul mate in the same person would be BLISS. But it's also pressure. I prefer to allow life to unfold as it will ... I appreciate the gifts however they show up.

I have a small handful of friends who have a true soul mate who is also their life partner ... 1 + 1 = 3 ... and I think they are SO fortunate. They are the rarity, amongst my friends anyhow.

I have learned to be a really great partner to myself.  I've sought wholeness and completeness within, and I feel so grateful to have been given the gift of really learning to love myself. I will likely always have some work to do in this area, but in the South especially where women live their lives giving their best love away, I feel so lucky to have begun to learn how to give my best love to me. It definitely raises the bar for what kind of man I will allow to become the object of my affection ... he must make up for what I'm not giving to myself by being with him--and then some!

### caroline — 2014-03-03T00:43:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-a-soul-mate.9966/post-113118>

Wow Misty ........thankyou for sharing that ......you don't often speak.........

You are always here solving our problems ......and being a buffer .....

And I am sure you "take one for the team" more than we will ever know .....Thanks!

### sooperb — 2014-03-04T14:59:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-a-soul-mate.9966/post-113334>

Misty, such wise words  They should be issued to every girl who is reaching maturity and thinking about a life partner. Then they should be given to every mother of a son so that she plants the seed that women are equal beings, they are not put on earth to pander to men or as you say, to raise them once they move in! The societies where men are raised and valued and women are not should wake up and smell the coffee. How can women be so important they are trusted with child rearing, yet valued so little they are abused and not respected, such a contradiction.

### caroline — 2014-03-05T01:45:21+0200

### tellmisty — 2014-03-05T02:02:20+0200

I love men. I don't think the men I date "mean" to be dishonoring to women in any way. I think some probably are just not as tuned in to "being" a partner vs. "having" a partner. # Thread: Leptin as the topic for this week's homework on my university
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-as-the-topic-for-this-weeks-homework-on-my-university.9967/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Albert83BCN — 2014-03-01T17:18:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-as-the-topic-for-this-weeks-homework-on-my-university.9967/post-112836>

Wow, I couldn't be happier today when I saw that the topic for this week's homework is to write a short essay on leptin and it's role on obesity.

The assignment goes like this:

_In this forum we are going to do critical thinking and documented work on Related Topics with lessons 3, 4, 5 and 6.

Leptin And Obesity

Obesity has been declared by the WHO as "the epidemic of the XXI century". This pathology is associated with multiple factors, including the genetic ellos. One of the lines of research in the boarding of this disease focuses on leptin.

One. What is Obesity? ¿And leptin?

Two. leptin impact on metabolism.

Three. Hypothetical beneficial effects of treatment leptin on obese subjects. Reference at least two studies that manifest the Role of Leptin in Obesity.

4\. Your own conclusions on the subject._

Considering that this is an online,distance university that has just starting operating this year and that, until now, the provided material hasn't been top quality -to say the least... I'm pretty surprised to find such a hot topic on this subject. Well, I guess it depends on the teacher, some of them suck big time but as far as it seem, the biochemistry one is onto something. Maybe he follows this blog also? hehe

### caroline — 2014-03-02T06:07:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-as-the-topic-for-this-weeks-homework-on-my-university.9967/post-113013>

We will be expecting big things fom you ......

### Albert83BCN — 2014-03-02T09:00:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-as-the-topic-for-this-weeks-homework-on-my-university.9967/post-113023>

Well, remember I'm a software engineeer, I'm not actually sure what I'll make about this degree on Nutrition (I think it's RD on the USA) and Sports Science... when I enrolled I made it with the idea in mind of continuing living as a software developer and keep this as my biggest passion, just to know more and more (and in fact as a great way to understand better a lot of the thing I read here and everywhere about physiology, neuroscicence, etc)... but since I became unemployed and got so deep into my blog I really started considering changing professions... well I really don't know where I'm going, just living today and doing what I want without questioning too much but although it troubles me a little bit. The entropy in me is really high right now...

### caroline — 2014-03-02T10:21:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-as-the-topic-for-this-weeks-homework-on-my-university.9967/post-113027>

Albert - are you in Spain? not sure I remember that right.... I will go and read your blog!
So - that isn't so bad \[unemployed\]....you can get out of EMF hell?

### caroline — 2014-03-02T10:22:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-as-the-topic-for-this-weeks-homework-on-my-university.9967/post-113028>

nice blog ........I think........

### Albert83BCN — 2014-03-02T10:54:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-as-the-topic-for-this-weeks-homework-on-my-university.9967/post-113030>

Thanks caroline!

Yep I'm in Spain. No, I have quite a trouble getting out of the EMF hell and, in fact, this is the area where I still have lots of work to do, its my Achiles heel. This is my context regarding my environtment: I live in an 8th floor in Barcelona with a lot of WiFi signals as well as high electrification, light contamination and technology. I have computers, tablets and mobile devices all around, WiFI at home, almost no windows, poor sunlight exposure, high artificial light all day long and I'm hooked to my own device as I cannot scape the convenience of it on this world I live... etc so you guess, this is the EMF hell at the Nth level, and I'm pretty sure that if I have two problems that suck up my life energy and redox potential right now are a high messed environtment and psychological stress.

BTW, as my girlfriend went to the doc this week I went with her and took the chance to ask for some labs as it has been quite some time since I took the last ones. I asked for Vitamin D testing (really looking towards to it since long time) and guess what my doctor told me? That a deficiency would be strange on someone of my age range and that asking for it 'without a need' was unnecessary... I told him 'look I spend a lot of time indoors and I'm curious about it' (not planning to explain to him how it could be used as a proxy for hormonal status and redox potential, he might have looked to me like ?) but it made no difference at all. He only asked for a RBC with basic lipid profile testing and liver enzymes since he's worried because my cholesterol levels are high... Lots of things went through my mind in a second but finally the only thing I could say to not enter a discussion that would have been futile was "ehhr... ok".

This has been a little bit offtopic, maybe I should have used it as a good excuse to update [my optimal journal](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/alberts-story-and-journey-to-optimal.7216/) instead, hehe

### caroline — 2014-03-03T02:15:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-as-the-topic-for-this-weeks-homework-on-my-university.9967/post-113129>

Albert ..... please have a look at Peter's posts today .....all you young people drive me crazy! sorry to say......

You are young and delicious ......the world is your oyster.....why aren't you out travelling the world and improving your redox potential - the way mother nature intended? You could be sleeping under the stars, swimming and fishing in streams ...... getting to know people who have very little, but, in reality have everything.

Take your girlfriend and throw a clean tshirt and some shorts in a backpack and have the time of your life........

### Albert83BCN — 2014-03-03T10:24:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-as-the-topic-for-this-weeks-homework-on-my-university.9967/post-113147>

That's what we should be doing from a health standpoint, no doubt about it. The problem is that we're tied by the expectations that this crazy world has put on us, from our family, etc.. I'm working on my startup company while studying a double degree in Nutrition and Sports Science and trying to get my message to the world about this topics on my blog (a little bit ironical, isn't it?). These are 3 things that each of them would requiere that I dedicate the full day on them, yet I have to split the time and also find some more to exercise, meet ocasionally my friends (everytime less and less), search the net for information (and this site consumes a lot of time also, LOL), and a lot more things...

I also have to think about how will I make money from something of this, as I'm still at my parents home at 31 and unemployed... here the Barcelona live is so expensive that you either accept an 8h job that is a shit and team up with someone or you cannot be afford to rent a flat. There's almost no help to enterpreneurs and even the normal jobs are scarce with the current crisis and very bad paid. My girlfriend also has a shit of a job but she's not willing to leave it because, well, she needs the money to live.

The good thing at least is that the startup I'm working on, if it turns out well, will be an online tool that will not only allow you to track your diet and exercise but also all the biomarkers that you want. For example, you could create the biomarker 'redox potential' if it still does not exist, as well as many others and then compare them with graphics, share it here, etc. It will be some kind of a mix between WellnessFX with SuperTracker with social media capabilities. I think it will be very helpful to every one of us that love to do our N = 1's, that my hope at least if I get engulged by this crazy world, that I'll leave something good for the ones that come after, let it be this tool or my blog! That's why I cannot afford to leave just for my sake, I want to change this world in some way or another.

BTW, I'll have a look at that post, and you remind me that I must consider giving priority to plan some weekend breaks in nature to try to bring some calm to my world, thank you caroline

PD: Today I slept almost 8h and feel pretty refreshed, It seems that at least I could recharge batteries a little bit!

### caroline — 2014-03-03T10:43:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-as-the-topic-for-this-weeks-homework-on-my-university.9967/post-113148>

Albert - I hear what you are saying. of course.......but - you are letting the world put it's expectations on you.......I think that is the wrong way around.

What do you want out of your life?

Please do read Peter's posts - He is an amazing young man - who has figured out what is important to him .... and also take a look at Ricardo's posts on lipids and ketones.

Please let me know what you think.....

no regrets......

# Thread: Patrick: introducing myself
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patrick-introducing-myself.9975/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 72
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### patgrif@hotmail.com — 2014-03-02T02:11:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patrick-introducing-myself.9975/post-112968>

Hi everybody, I am very unsure how this Forum thing works but anyway. My name is Patrick, I was born in Ireland in 1952 (61 years old) came to LA in 1978 and have lived here since. I was diagnosed with diabetes Type 2 in 2003 but have 'conrolled' it fairly well I think with 'diet and exercise' I have never taken any medication for it. Having said that I am not sure how well I have controlled it.

I discovered Dr Kruse in late 2011 so have 'followed' him for quite a while, I became a Gold member sometime in 2012. I am very impressed by Dr Kruse as a 'thinker' his whole way of thinking really appeals to me. I have a degree from an Irish University in Philosophy and have always been interested in 'thinking' I suppose you could say. I have taken courses here in Biology, Physiology, and other 'sciency' type subjects (AC/DC currents etc) but anyway what I get from Dr Kruse is a very broad way of thinking. He is obviously very knowledgble about biology but I find him to be a 'philosopher' too and it is that combination I find so appealing and interesting.

I have also been involved in psycho-therapy specifically "Primal Therapy" which was pioneered by Dr Arthur Janov ("The Primal Scream") his first book came out in 1973 and it was that that motivated me to come to LA in 1978. I found the promise of primal therapy to be largeley unfulfilled for myself and also for most other people at least in my opinion. But even to this day I find the ideas in it very convincing and intriguing and in my fantasies sometimes imagine I could kind of 'merge' Dr Kruse's writings with the notions of primal therapy. As a short explanation it is in a way a standard sort of Freudian idea but the difference is not to 'just talk' about feelings but to actually feel them. And I do believe first of all a healthy body has a chance of doing that whereas an unhealthy one (most people I would say) cannot. The other side is I imagine somehow the idea of 'feeling' can maybe enhance Dr Kruse's protocols somehow too.

I am not married not do I have any children. I live by myself. I spent about 25 years building up and running a Moving and Storage company which was very stressful and took a lot out of me. Now I am retired and slowly kind of gaining my health back and actually enjoying life pretty much. I do CT quite a bit now in the Pacific Ocean here and I like it very much, I have decided to get a magnetico but I am wavering about the strenght. Dr Kruse said I might need 100 Gauss living in LA so I suppose I will spring for the 20 Gauss then. I pretty much follow the Epi-Paleo diet, I have recently also upped by spring water intake by a lot and all of these things I find helpful. Sometimes I have questions and I think the Forum here can help me. I will now send this off into the void and see what happens!

### caroline — 2014-03-02T02:19:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patrick-introducing-myself.9975/post-112971>

Welcome Patrick ..... Please start a journal in the journal section so Dr. kruse will have some context and we can get to know you too.
Go to the top of the page and click on forum - the journal section is in the first category.

Since you are retired ....... have you thought of moving out of your EMF hell? or at least spending large blocks of time in an area more conducive to optimal health ...... like the Gulf?

How is your redox potential?

### patgrif@hotmail.com — 2014-03-02T02:51:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patrick-introducing-myself.9975/post-112977>

Thanks Caroline, at least this seems to be working. BTW I am sorry I don't have a picture to put on here (technically challenged could use help with that). What does starting a journal mean? A history/biography type of thing? Labs? Not sure what it means.

I wasn't in the habit of thinking of LA as "EMF Hell" but maybe it is. I live pretty close to the Ocean on the ground floor of a cottage. I am as careful as I can be, no longer use a cell phone, no wi-fi, everything wired computer phone etc. I watched all Michael Nuert's stuff but you may well be correct I am sure living here I still have a lot of exposure. For that reason too I am going to get a magnetico.

Thanks again Caroline...............and I may need your help for a while

### caroline — 2014-03-02T03:11:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patrick-introducing-myself.9975/post-112981>

It is my absolute pleasure .... always, always please ask if you need help with something - nobody is more technically challenged than me - trust me on that one!

So - starting a journal ..... Just tell us a little about yourself - where you live and a little health history. Dr. Kruse can't practise medicine on the internet so he needs some context to point us in the right direction and tell us specific stuff to read.
He says it is like going to the garage and talking to your mechanic about a problem .... but leaving the car at home!

About a picture .... I just took one with my iphone and transferred it to my laptop and then to here. It was really easy. Honestly if I could do it - anyone can!

Since you are retired .... do you go camping? sleep under the stars? swim naked in a stream? catch fresh fish?
Have you ever thought of joining Erwan Lecorre and doing a movnat?

Sorry - I get so excited about everything here!
You will find lots and lots of help here ....this is a great community of folks working on their own masterpiece. You never know when someone will say something that rings a bell for you .... or you will have so much to offer others here ... something they hadn't thought about or overlooked. It happens to all of us.

Please keep reading and asking questions .....Dr.K. will keep answering questions till he is blue in the face - whatever it takes for us to get it!

Please tell me what else I can help you with .........

Do you have Jack's book? The Epi-Paleo RX

Have you seen his podcasts? they are on the home page.

I strongly suggest that you become a "Gold" member \[of course you don't have to\]
You will have access to all the webinars at any time that is convenient for you ... and there is a live Q&A every month where you can ask Jack pretty much anything - and they can go for hours!

I have heard Erwin La Corre and yes like that idea very much..............who knows maybe someday.

I AM a gold member and do listen to ALL the webinars etc, so I am not 'new' in that sense. I kind of 'devour' everything that comes from Dr Kruse, the blogs, webinars, Q&A's everything really EXCEPT this Forum so now I am trying to put my toe in the water there (with your help!)
