### ScottishEmma — 2017-08-03T09:03:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/root-canal-treatment.10003/post-220800>

I got one of my back molars removed years ago. There was a gap for a while then the other teeth moved (wisdom) into the space. Don't know if that helps but the only thing that worried me was chewing on just one side of my mouth for a while making my face squint...

### caroline — 2017-08-04T06:51:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/root-canal-treatment.10003/post-220882>

A few months ago I had a root canal problem - infection. I have had an infection there before ....and who nows - maybe always had a low grade infection over many years ...the root canal was done years go.

This problem was more complicated because that tooth was the anchor for a bridge. Without that tooth the bridge was no longer a possibility.
The dentist pulled the tooth and took out the bridge... and I have felt so much better.

The only thing I can do now is get implants - but it would be about $30,000.00. My dentist knew the funds were an issue so he said to leave it and see how I go. There has not been a problem at all.

### Inger — 2017-08-04T11:39:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/root-canal-treatment.10003/post-220890>

I have 2 teeth that needed root canal treatment... Dentist cleaned the tooth and put an temporary filling on it to make root canal treatment later.. but I just could not do it! I do not want any foreign stuff in my roots .. It is now like 7-8 year ago and I have zero issues with those empty dead teeth as long as I keep my diet clean (sans pasteurized dairy, and of course crap food.. that I do not eat..lol) When I do not - I get pain and infection.. but it clears up immediately when I clean up the diet again! Crazy huh.

### Sheddie — 2017-08-04T22:54:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/root-canal-treatment.10003/post-220940>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=220890)
>
> I have 2 teeth that needed root canal treatment... Dentist cleaned the tooth and put an temporary filling on it to make root canal treatment later.. but I just could not do it! I do not want any foreign stuff in my roots .. It is now like 7-8 year ago and I have zero issues with those empty dead teeth as long as I keep my diet clean (sans pasteurized dairy, and of course crap food.. that I do not eat..lol) When I do not - I get pain and infection.. but it clears up immediately when I clean up the diet again! Crazy huh.

Hulda Clark (hope she's not anathema here?) suggested to just keep replacing the temporary fillings with root canals...

I have a molar (#19) I've been trying to save from extraction for years; since 2011 I think. Radionics scans, where my holistic dentist sent me, keep pronouncing the nerve/tooth as still "alive" and no infection necrosis detected by that method, or on normal x-ray. The scans do indicate some parasitical activity. After some homeopathics and ozone injections, averaging once a year or so, the sensitivity abates and very slowly over time sneaks back and increases in 'volume.' That went along until a 3/4 gold crown (originally placed with Clifford Test for consultation selecting alloys or something?) popped off with flossing. I was prevented from getting it simply glued back on, while tending to cataracts in FL in February, and delayed months after that from sheer overload on my time. So, by July, teeth had shifted and the old crown didn't fit. (Punishment for my error in prioritizing.) I needed a full crown (Zirconium these days) and plan, hopefully, to continue with O3 to buy time... Of course, the dreaded eventuality may arrive I have to make decisions between extraction and implant so I am mighty thankful for posts around this topic... and, to Kovita, for mentioning it...

### smith5 — 2019-10-26T23:25:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/root-canal-treatment.10003/post-281298>

Can tooth implants produce undesired immune response or complications?
I've got a root removed due to infection and an indication for removing the adjacent tooth which has a vertical breakage and half of it removed.

I downloaded ~20> audios of interviews with Wim Hof, where he described how he's and +10 people trained by him got injected with (e.coli) endotoxin
and neutralized it in ~20 minutes by the Wim Hof method. When I told a dentist - a member of some odontologists' association - prescribing root removal what if I'd strengthen the immune system and neutralize this tooth infection, he said it sort of can't be done.
After taking antibiotic for 5 days I've got a piece of dried skin under a left eyebrow (the side of the infected tooth). Although it was summer and had moderate amount of sunlight and quite a bit of being in cold water, I think this piece of dried skin was resolved after a ~1 month of drinking the 10 mushroom blend from Four Sigmatic. I also commited to ~70% ketogenic diet which I found to remove fatique.

These infections feed on glucose, don't they? Maybe a more rigorous ketosis would help?

[Google](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2019-06-stem-cells-dentists-future-teeth.html) and [Pubmed](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/30051321) tell it is possible to regrow a new tooth from stem cells.
But given the rate of the development of such affordable treatments, it seems more lucrative to develop more means causing more suicides amongst doctors.

# Thread: A bread that isn't a bread
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-bread-that-isnt-a-bread.10006/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sooperb — 2014-03-04T15:24:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-bread-that-isnt-a-bread.10006/post-113337>

A recipe sent to me recently that is from a lady called Kelley Herring. It sounds amazing.

**Paleo Herb & Sun-Dried Tomato Focaccia Bread Recipe**
(low-carb, gluten-free, high fiber and healthy fats)

Servings: 12
Active Mixing Time: 10 minutes
Total Time: 50 minutes

Ingredients

• 5 pasture-raised eggs (or organic, omega-3 eggs)
• 1 pack [Let's Do Organic Creamed Coconut](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00113ZZ5U/ref=as_li_ss_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=390957&creativeASIN=B00113ZZ5U&linkCode=as2&tag=truthaboutabs-20) (key ingredient; \*details below about this)
• ¼ cup organic virgin coconut oil, melted (or extra virgin olive oil)
• ½ Tbsp. dried organic basil (an Italian herb blend and/or rosemary would be delicious too)
• 2 tsp. organic garlic powder (to taste)
• ¼ cup organic Parmesan cheese, divided
• 5 organic sun-dried tomatoes packed in olive oil, sliced (try Mediterranean Organic)
• ½ tsp. Sea Salt
• ¾ tsp. baking soda

\* For the "Let's Do Organic Creamed Coconut", this may not be in every grocery store, but I found it in Whole Foods, or you can get it at Amazon. This is VERY different than just coconut cream or milk, as this contains the fiber from coconuts too, which is key to making this recipe come out properly.

Preparation

1\. Soften creamed coconut by placing packet in a bowl of warm water. Work with your hands to create a pourable consistency.
2\. Preheat oven to 300 F and lightly grease a 9" round cake pan (preferably stoneware).
3\. Add eggs, melted coconut oil, creamed coconut, sea salt and baking soda to a blender. Blend until very smooth (no lumps!).
4\. Pour batter into a medium mixing bowl and fold in herbs and parm cheese (if using).
5\. Pour into greased pan. Top with sliced sun-dried tomatoes and a little extra parm cheese, if using.
6\. Transfer to oven and bake 40-45 minutes until golden brown and firm.
7\. Serve with a dipping oil made with high quality organic extra virgin olive oil, minced garlic and herbs, to taste.

Nutrition Information Per Serving:
192 calories, 18 grams healthy fats, 6 grams carbohydrate (mostly fiber), 3 grams protein

This is a perfect bread for diabetics since there's no blood sugar impact hardly at all, and also just good for anyone looking to control blood sugar and cravings for the fat loss and appetite control effects.

### Lahelada — 2014-03-08T01:54:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-bread-that-isnt-a-bread.10006/post-114067>

Soo,down here at the end of teh world I do not get most things . The coconut milk they are referring too sounds like coconut milk with rehydrated desiccated coconut to me. I make my own coconut milk quite often when fatty desiccated coconut is available and one thing you learn is that the coconut flour everyone buys is just the fibre leftover from making the milk.So if you whizz the fibre in a mill and dry it you have coconut flour. For you recipe you just ue the desiccated cc nut as is in conjunction with commercially available cc milk.

# Thread: Stella's Journey to Health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 156
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Stella — 2014-03-04T15:31:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-113339>

Hi All. What a great resource of information!! Thanks Dr Kruse for all your insight and time. I have signed up for the BioHacking and Optimal Reset. My head is exploding from all the info. I have figured out that I have major leaky gut issues and poor redox.

Progress to date: Been on the Leptin reset diet – very low carb, no night shades, no starches; stopped excersising; distressing and spending lots of time in my backyard on weekends - in sun and pool; lost about 5 lbs (BMI of 21); A1c dropped from 5.6 to 4.9; found dr that has prescribed bhrt, thyroid; some days I do feel increase in body temp.

Challenges: eating the amount of protein in am, adjusting clock and schedule to fit more natural circadian rhythm, suspect EMF sensitivity.

Main problem: Diagnosis of spondyloarthritis, presenting with several affected joints, esp . effusion in the right knee for about a year. Fluid in knee was removed only to return in a few days. Other symptoms in past history include recurrent uveitis and eczema. The joints started to hurt and swell just when I experience menopause. Non responsive to NSAIDS. Only when started on Enbrel did inflammation subside – although still left with fatigue, brain fog. Those symptoms of fatigue and brain fog have improved with change in bhrt and addition of natural thyroid.

My issues with inflammation: grew up in Cuban household – eating loads of white rice, beans, foods fried in vegetable oil, tropical fruits – all the time thinking something was wrong with me. I felt like crap all the time. Left home and changed diet somewhat and began to exercise – felt better. First run in with inflammation was in my late 20’s with several episodes of uveitis – checked out by rheum with nothing found in labs. Uveitis continued until mid 30’s. Late 30’s started to have skin issue. My hairline and skin around eyes would become red, inflamed and scaly, eczema. This has occurred every year during the hot summer months – May thru September. (Leading me to believe that I have leaky gut). I work in sales - my office is my car - so it gets really hot over the summer months in S Florida. I exercised very regularly until 3 years ago.

Questions: Is cardiac CRP the same as HS CRP? Would TNF blocker lower the HS CRP? Any other recommendations for a beginner.

### sooperb — 2014-03-04T15:43:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-113343>

Hello Stella, great to see you in here, the home of all the best brains for fixing yourself  I can't help with your issues as I'm just a learner myself but I can say "Ouch" they sound pretty painful. Someone will be along I'm sure to point you in the right directions.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-04T18:18:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-113371>

HS CRP = cardiac CRP TNF blocker can...........there are expensive new ones by IV route (Enbrel) but there are cheap ones too (Trental or nettle leaf extract). Trental is now way off patent and very very cheap. Few people know it works just like Enbrel does with a lot less side effects.
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11451976](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11451976)

Evidence has been accumulating for 15 yrs that the culprits are proinflammatory cytokines, together with the enzymes that generate inflammatory mediators and break down the collagen matrix. Cytokines are proteins that carry messages between cells and regulate immunity and inflammation. Two cytokines, tumor necrosis factor alpha (TNF-alpha) and interleukin one beta (IL-1 beta), play an essential role in the cartilage destruction and inflammation process. They have been found in elevated levels in the synovial membrane, the synovial fluid and the cartilage of osteoarthritis patients. In animal models, it was shown that inhibition of TNF-alpha results in decreased inflammation, while inhibition of IL-1 beta effectively prevents cartilage destruction. TNF-alpha has proven to be even more important in rheumatoid arthritis, where it is a key factor in promoting inflammation and damage to cartilage and bone.

Inflammatory Pathways in humans is one that can open the doors to heaven or hell. (Remember the COX −2 blocking drugs all caused heart attacks when taken to excess because of these pathways.
The body’s inflammatory pathway has two branches, COX (cyclooxygenase) and LOX (lipoxygenase). Aspirin and other non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), long the mainstays of **arthritis treatment**, block COX in both its forms, COX-1 and COX-2. COX-2 produces prostaglandins, which are powerful triggers of pain and inflammation. They also play a role in maintaining the proper redox potential of the cellular matrix in the heart and in blood vessels so chronic blockade will cause a problem. This is why they are no longer on the market. However, COX-1 is necessary for stomach lining protection, so interfering with its activity can cause gastric disturbances ranging from simple discomfort to bleeding ulcers. For this reason big pharma created COX-2 inhibitors, which block COX-2 with little effect on COX-1, and they rocketed in popularity with docs and people with arthritis because they really worked well for DJD. But when taken to too long they also killed you.........not so good.

The rub is that inhibition of COX-2 is thought to lead to an imbalance between two key inflammatory mediators, raising levels of thromboxane A2 relative to prostaglandin E2. This in turn increases production of the above mentioned proinflammatory signaling molecules, the cytokines TNF- alpha (tumor necrosis factor alpha) and IL-1b (interleukin-1 beta). The balance between these two systems is critical to maintain.

In addition, there is evidence that elevated thromboxane A2 raises levels of an important inflammatory mediator in the LOX (lipoxygenase) pathway, leukotriene B4.

Thus COX-2 inhibition worked by helping perpetuate the underlying degenerative process while just relieving its superficial symptoms. The research scientists who discovered these drugs said, “the short-term effects of (COX-2 inhibitors) on the pain and swelling of inflammation and arthritis may be achieved at the cost of an increased propensity to long-term tissue damage with which these cytokines have been associated.” His warning was not heeded and then all the bad news about the COX 2 drugs came out in after market data. TRental however is not a COX 2 drug. IT is forgotten by most people............for its mode of action on TNF.

Tumor necrosis factor (TNF) and interleukin-1 (IL-1) are considered to be master cytokines in chronic, destructive arthritis, even today

Their destructive roles overlap, however, in arthritic disease like you have mentioned above. In rheumatoid arthritis, TNF-alpha figures more prominently in triggering inflammation both locally and systemically, while IL-1 beta is more involved in destruction of cartilage and bone, and in impeding the repair of cartilage.

The research points up the importance of inhibiting TNF-alpha, IL-1 beta, and the LOX pathway when using a COX-2 inhibitor and how it can be great short term and not so great longer term for other organs.

Inflammatory joint diseases are characterized by breakdown of the extracellular matrix (ECM), which surrounds and supports cells. In arthritis, TNF-alpha and especially IL-1 beta stimulate enzymes known as matrix metalloproteinases (MMP’s) that break down the extracellular matrix. Experiments have measured MMP levels of chondrocytes (joint cells) exposed to IL-1 beta. Nettle leaf extract was specifically found to significantly inhibit all the matrix metalloproteinases tested (MMP-1, -3 and −9). Trental is effective in doing this as well. When COX 2 drugs were being taken off the market many people began to use Nettle Leaf extract to try to offset that bad effects of the COX 2 drugs........now today this is all an after effect since these drugs are no longer available anywhere. But they worked...........but you can not take them forever...........because they can kill you and they did kill many people with heart attacks.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-04T18:23:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-113372>

**Dual Inflammatory Pathway Blockade**
Stimulation of TNF-alpha and IL-1 beta is not the only problem associated with COX-2 inhibitors. The body has two inflammatory pathways, both branching from the inflammatory precursor arachidonic acid. Blocking one pathway while leaving the other wide open is as imbalancing as it is incomplete. Big Pharma understood this shortcoming, and are trying to develop drugs that block both branches of the inflammatory pathway.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

The problem with these drug candidates is that they block both forms of COX (COX-1 and COX-2) in addition to 5-LOX. Research has shown that indiscriminate COX inhibitors create a persistent “rebound effect” on proinflammatory signaling. This means pain and swelling increase!!! Even two weeks after discontinuation of daily aspirin or ibuprofen, cytokine-stimulated production of TNF-alpha and IL-1 beta was increased from 270% to 538% in studies.

The balanced approach of dealing with the good and bad in these pathways must be vetted. Super selective inhibition of COX-2 combined with inhibition of TNF-alpha and IL-1 beta—is, however, still incomplete even today. It is necessary to block the second inflammatory pathway, 5-lipoxygenase (5-LOX), which produces proinflammatory leukotrienes. Fortunately, a natural herbal inhibitor or good drug blocker of 5-LOX has not been discovered in my opinion yet. Boswellia serrata has been touted as a 5-LOX blocker but I have not seen great results with it.

I have never been a big eater, but most of my bad calories before came from wine and grains. Ive given up both - grains probably for good and well wine - I feel better without it. Ive got to have a party to consume the stash of wine that I have at the house!!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-02T05:42:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-124507>

**Study: Electromagnetic Fields Act Via Activation of Voltage-Gated Calcium Channels to Produce Beneficial or Adverse Effects**

**Electromagnetic fields act via activation of voltage-gated calcium channels to produce beneficial or adverse effects**

**_This message comes from Martin Pall, author of the study published online 26 June 2013 in the Journal of Cellular and Molecular Medicine : The study is available here in full_**:

[http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/jcmm.12088/pdf](http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/jcmm.12088/pdf)

One of the great puzzles about the action of electromagnetic fields is how can they influence the biology of our bodies? The reason that this is such a great puzzle is that these fields are comprised of low energy photons, with energies too low to influence the chemistry of our bodies. So how can they possibly influence our biology? Many have argued that the only thing that they can possibly do is to heat things, and yet it is very clear that levels of exposure that produce only the slightest heating have been repeatedly shown to produce substantial biological effects. Now this puzzle has been solved in a paper with the title of this email, published on line in the Journal of Cellular and Molecular Medicine, freely available on the publisher's web site:
[http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/jcmm.12088/pdf](http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/jcmm.12088/pdf)

That paper reviews 24 different studies in which EMF exposures produce biological effects that can be blocked by using calcium channel blockers, drugs that block the action of voltage-gated calcium channels (VGCCs). Most of these drug studies implicated L-type VGGCs, showing blockage by channel blockers specific for these L-type VGCCs; however threeother classes of the voltage gated calcium channels were also implicated in some of these studies. What these and other studies show, is that EMF exposures act by partially depolarizing the electrical charge across the plasma membrane of cells, activating the VGCCs and it is the increased intracellular calcium levels that are responsible for the reaction to EMF exposure.

These 24 studies implicate the VGCCs in responses to a variety of EMFs, including extremely low frequency EMFs such as 50 and 60 cycle fields produced by our alternating currents in our wiring, various microwave/radiofrequency EMFs and nanosecond electrical pulses.
Static electrical fields also act via VGCCs, not surprisingly because they also influence the electrical charge across plasma membranes.

Perhaps more surprisingly, static magnetic fields also act via VGCCs. This is a bit surprising because static magnetic fields do not produce electrical changes in static objects. However as pointed out in the paper, living cells in the body are rarely static, often moving rapidly in such phenomena as cellular ruffling.

Having resolved this long-standing puzzle, the paper goes on to consider how VGCC activation can produce two well-documented responses to EMF exposure: stimulating of bone growthand the production of single stranded DNA breaks in EMF-exposed cells. EMF exposures have repeatedly been shown to produce increases in nitric oxide levels, in some cases almost instantaneously. These nitric oxide increases are produced through calcium stimulation of the action of the two nitric oxide synthases in the cell, iNOS and eNOS, which are both calcium-dependent enzymes.

Nitric oxide in the cell, acts to produce most physiological effects, by stimulating the production of cycle GMP which stimulates, in turn the G-kinase (this is known as theNO/sGC/cGMP/G-kinase pathway).

Most pathophysiological responses to nitric oxide to through another pathway, where nitric oxide acts as a precursor of peroxynitrite, a potent oxidant and reactive free radical precursor.

The paper suggests that the EMF stimulation of bone growth, a very promising therapeutic response, goes through the first pathway.

It also suggests that induction of single strand breaks in cellular DNA goes through the second pathway.

It is possible that possible beneficial effects of EMFs go through the first pathway and adverse, pathophysiological effects go through the second pathway.

Clearly we will need a lot of study to test mechanisms of EMF action.

This paper may be viewed in a practical setting as being very important in two ways:

1\. There have been many claims that biological effects of EMF exposures cannot possibly exist because no plausible mechanism of action of such exposures could produce such effects. Clearly these claims are now defunct.

2\. In studies aimed at understanding the mechanisms of action of EMF exposures we now know where to look. Such studies need to look at roles of VGCCs, intracellular calcium, nitric oxide and possibly cycle GMP or peroxynitrite. It can be argued, therefore, that this paper is very much a game changer,
changing a situation where there has been substantial confusion, into one where, specific, targeted questions can be asked and answered experimentally.

Finally, this paper says nothing at all about EMF hypersensitivity (often abbreviated EHS), a condition where previous EMF exposure appears to induce high level sensitivity to some types of EMFs. EHS is similar to multiple chemical sensitivity (MCS), where previous chemical exposures produce high level chemical sensitivity. Chemicals act in MCS by indirectly activating the NMDA receptors and NMDA receptors have many similarities in their properties to those of the L-type VGCCs. You should expect, therefore, a future paper on a detailed proposed mechanism for EHS, with both many similarities and some apparent mechanism of MCS as well as some differences.

### Stella — 2014-05-17T19:46:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-127367>

My recent life as a Starfish

Over the past several years I have experienced somewhat of a moral crisis in terms of my day job. In the beginning I really did not care what I sold or how I made the sale, only that I made it and got paid. But after a while I started to see things were totally morally wrong. I began to care about the well being of the end users of the products I sold. Part of this stemmed from my curiosity and because I was experiencing health issues for which the medical community had few answers. As I look back I was starting to see the first symptoms of AI in my late 20's.

Fast forward to today. I left my first employer after 15 years because I couldn't live with what I was selling and because they only cared about returns to the shareholders. Fortunalely I left before the SHTF. But can't give any further details. I'm now working for my 3rd employer in this industry. Ive been around long enough to know there are ways to treat disease that make more sense than others.

Thus my current situation - what am I doing other than making a very good living? For most people making a good living is all they care about. For me that's not enough right now.

Because of my profession I interact with healthcare professionals on a more personal level and they see me as part of the medical industry. I am Certain that the vast majority of these very good, knowledgeable and caring professionals are disappointed to see the direction in which medicine has been taken by politicians and corporations. They are also frustrated by patients wanting a magic pill so they don't have to change their lives. Just the other day I overheard 2 physicians discussing business concerns. One says to the other, "every night when I lock the door I dream of not having to come back the next day." Something is seriously wrong with our medical system and it's getting worse.

So what to do about my current situation? Out of coincidence, or perhaps to challenge those who would listen, I've begun to ask different questions, that is after I discuss what I'm paid to promote. Questions like, "what effect do you think EMF and cellphone usage is having on your patients health?" "What are your thoughts about anti aging and BHRT?" "When you check for Vit D levels, what percentage of your patients have normal levels?" And finally, if I've set the foundation, "why do you think most of your patients with chronic diseases like type 2 diabetes are not improving, causing you to constantly intensify therapy?" Very thought provoking questions have led to interesting discussions.

I've begun to carry Jack's book in my bag. I've forwarded information on EMF to several physicians. Finally I know why I'm still out here after all these years. I'm a starfish!!!

### Stella — 2014-06-20T16:34:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-131528>

Haven't posted much on my journal lately but that doesn't mean I have not been busy making changes and improving in all areas of life. It's amazing to have a brain that is coming back to life. It's like going from analog to digital.

I'm pasting what I posted on the Oysters thread, so I don't lose this later on:

I totally agree with your thoughts. To understand how to fix a problem, you first have to understand how you got there. And lack of energy, electrons specifically, is at the foundation of all health issues. When your brain is starved for energy, how can the rest of the body function optimally? That means suboptimal mind, body and performance. When speaking specifically about sex, if you can't think right, how can you expect to act right? Details are important in life, and when your brain is starved, details fall by the wayside. You can't possibly grasp, retain and process information.

Before my brain became optimal, you could drive a truck in front of me and I could notice, but a small object or detail didn't have a chance of making it on my radar. Sex, as with anything in life, is best when you pay attention to details. Before optimal, I would not notice the things I notice now, the look of my husband across the room, a soft word, a tender detail. So that's why my engine is revved up and ready to go at a moments notice. It's because I'm paying attention to the subtle signs of life and I'm an active participant rather than just an observer.

As I've managed to bring my husband back to optimal, the same thing is now happening to him, as he is now paying attention and remembering the details that are important to me. Sex is no longer ordinary, but has improved because everything else in our lives has improved. I like the analogy of a masterpiece, and loving does require time, but it gets started way before anything physical happens. So in other words, when you move towards optimal, you became aware of details, colors, smells and beauty, thus taking the experience beyond the physical and into the sublime.

### Stella — 2014-08-01T23:07:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-136406>

It's been too long since I've been to my journal. I've been feeling so well and living life to the fullest that I haven't given time to document my successes.

First, a diet high in DHA totally rocks. Both my husband and I have noticed the difference. The positive cognitive effects are significant. I'm in a highly competitive sales profession that's highly regulated. As such I'm constantly evaluated, where my verbal messaging skills are critical. If I can't speak and communicate at a high level, then I'm basically toast. I've noticed dramatic improvements in my verbal communication. Not only organizing my thoughts but being able to verbalize them.

In the past couple of weeks I've worked with a field trainer and my manager. I've also led a conference call. I was congratulated on my mastery in presenting clinical studies, selling skills and business analysis. So things are becoming easier and I'm performing at a higher level without much effort.

I'm also noticing that if I don't jump in a cold tub at least once a week, I don't feel well and my sleep is affected. So I've jumping in a cold tub at least once a week. Unfortunately the pool is way too warm to cool me down.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-14T15:14:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-137818>

Nitric oxide in the cell, acts to produce most physiological effects, by stimulating the production of cycle GMP which stimulates, in turn the G-kinase (this is known as theNO/sGC/cGMP/G-kinase pathway).

Most pathophysiological responses to nitric oxide to through another pathway, where nitric oxide acts as a precursor of peroxynitrite, a potent oxidant and reactive free radical precursor.

The paper suggests that the EMF stimulation of bone growth, a very promising therapeutic response, goes through the first pathway.

Did you know that all oxides can be magnetized with light? Its true.

### Stella — 2014-08-17T23:32:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-138103>

I am just returning home after a 9 day vacation on Florida's beautiful beaches. Had great family time for a few days and then 6 days with my wonderful husband. Plenty of rest and time for reflection. It is a treat to be able to reflect on where I have been and also to be conscious of the progress that has taken place over the past short months. And people do take notice and ask about the change in my health and appearance. Friends that hadn't seen me in months, family that saw me deteriorate. They were so aware and delighted of the health and vibrations flowing from me.

For so long, I was a driving, highly successful person. Perhaps way too focused on work, to the exclusion of other areas of my life. Burn the candle at both ends. Obviously, this eventually took a toll on my health. Looking back, too much cortisol is not a good thing.

Also, too much work isolates you and keeps you from connecting with others. We cannot live in isolation from others. We are meant to be part of community, to share and to learn from the experiences of others. I now realize that these experiences and conversations provide the inspiration for our lives. They are seeds that we plant in each other's lives and occasionally water by our interactions. Consciousness is a big deal and can help us along the way if we know how to access it. But that implies that your brain has enough electrons.

Why did it take so long for me to learn these lessons? But I should be thankful that I did learn the lessons and that I can change my life to a more meaningful, worthwhile course. Could it be all those extra electrons that I am taking in and keeping?

I am really grateful that I have a great husband who loves and accepts me in spite of me. Who allows me to be me. Glad we have grown together after more than 35 years. Glad we are living and enjoying life more than ever. What a blessing. Grateful that life is working itself out. Certainly it is good living in a more relaxed manner. I still have the same stressors but I'm keeping everything in perspective, giving proper place to those things, people and experiences that belong in my life. And excluding those that have no place in my life. Not everything is good and we have to be surgeons, cutting out bad things. Life is way too good and precious to allow crap to ruin it.

I am grateful for all I've learned on this forum and from Jack's knowledge. I am grateful for all I took in while I was with Jack, Audrey, Josh and others in Orlando. This interaction put things into focus and gave me the extra kick in the ass. Like Audrey has said in her journal, that trip was a turning point and a catalyst for change. Quantum entanglements!!! The progress I've made in a few short months is remarkable. My mind is working again. What a gift to be able to see and interact with my environment, the way I did many years ago. I feel so much younger and healthier. Living the moment, enjoying and taking advantage of all the wonderful people and experiences that surround me. And creating life from my thoughts and imagination, all because my mind is working again! And by no means will I take all of this for granted, realizing the sick, unhealthy days I experienced.

So back to reality, back to being a starfish. Being able to balance work and rest, that will be my goal.

### Stella — 2014-08-19T22:31:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-138315>

Reflections on my vacation time - OR- The road to optimal is a two way street!!

What factors could have negatively affected my health in a few short days? 1) in tense exposure to wi-fi over a week; 2) much more alcohol than usual; 3) excessive blue light exposure - staying up late, reading from my iPad, esp. when combined with the alcohol; 4) not getting my morning sunlight exposure, which messed up my clock; 5) not taking my usual supplements- forgot my vitamins most days, left behind supplements to help with methylation; 6) taking my thyroid later in the day than normal; 7) too much heat and no cooling down; 8) not enough water to compensate for the dehydration caused be the heat, wi-fi and alcohol; 9) still cannot handle eating even a small amount of fruit; 10) living in am apartment is not a great idea, I was on the 8th floor; 11) doesn't matter how much DHA you consume, how much oxy you produce, it doesn't compensate for bad behavior and a bad environment.

So what was the result: 1) towards the end of the week, I was having trouble sleeping. I would get to sleep but would wake up several hours later feeling tired but wired. Obviously, cortisol up. Tired in the morning and having to sleep later. Cortisol up, T3 and vitamin A down; 2) my thinking is not as clear, ie., forgetting things, or decisions, trouble bringing up the right words when I speak, finding myself thinking "why did I say that"; 3) feeling slightly dizzy in waking and near bedtime; 4) vision is blurry esp in my right eye whet I have had a cataract for a long time - vitamin A alert!!; 5)gained a couple of pounds but feel heavier around the midsection. BMI creepinog up to 21.4. Not good given my AI condition; 6) aching joints, mostly due to nightshades, but also to increase in cortisol and it's effects on other hormones and factors.

If you can travel the road to optimal, you can also travel the same road towards ill health again. The disruption of my routine, esp lack of early morning sun exposure and over exposure to blue light, NN EMF, all of these head a very definite and rapid effect on my wellbeing. I suspect that only being on the road to optimal for about 7 months is not sufficient to correct physical insults to my health that took place over years. For months I was tuned into habits that quickly turned my health around. But in one short week, I feel the effects of cortisol rising at the wrong time, messing up other hormones in my body. Lesson to be learned from this:I still have quite a long time to go on my journey. It may take years to get new back to where I need to be, but at least I'm on the right road.

Goals: 1) reduce NN EMF; 2)get in a cold tub at least twice a week; 3) drop 7 lbs; 4) reset my clock, early morning sun exposure is a must; 5) limit exposure to blue light and technology in the evenings; 6) increase grounding and time outdoors.

Back to work!!!

### Stella — 2017-08-18T14:36:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-221876>

After a long hiatus from my optimal journal, I've decided to start updating my journal.

First, I realize that I can't fit 3 years of progress and change in a small post. Suffice to say that the last 2+ years have been planning and taking small action steps to get me and my family to where we are today. My inspiration has been the desire to see myself and my family is a better, healthier place. And I have to add, the love and support of my twin sis, Audrey!!!

So, today I'm counting down the days until I retire, 13 days to be exact. And about 3 to 4 weeks away from moving from the nnEMF nightmare called Miami, FL. We are trying to get out alive, and I'm being serious about that statement.

How did this all come about? The realization that my current lifestyle was not optimal, in fact it was sucking the life out of me and my husband and affecting my sons. Once I realized the serious nature of life in the crazy city, escape was really the only option. However, it does take a really long time to unwind careers, financial situations, etc.

So with knowledge gained mostly from Jack, the realization set in that we were screwed in our former lifestyle. Once the seriousness of a crappy living was realized, despite the low dopamine levels my husband was experiencing, I pushed to get us moving in a direction to free us from this hell. We listed our big house for sale, the whole process taking over 14 months to get us out. A month later my husband quit his job. He had been stuck looking a computer screen as much as 12 hours a day for his job. This went on for years. And over the years this caused serious weight gain. Now just 6 months later he is down 35 lbs and still losing weight. He is his old self again, happy and balanced.

Fast forward to today. I resigned my job and will be closing up shop in 2 weeks. I have been working for big pharma for 35+ years in several companies. SO, SO, SO glad that is ending. I'm leaving without a pension but fortunately medical coverage for me and my husband.

We have no idea where we are going to move. Fortunately we had purchased a small RV and we will be moving into this wonderful house on wheels in less than a month. Travel in the RV was what put us in remote places such as state and national parks. My husband and I were sleeping so much better, feeling better and living outdoors. Once we experienced this, we fully realized the damaged caused by living in a big city. This further drove our desire to get the hell out as soon as possible.

Don't know at this point where we will end up but hopefully somewhere in Florida.

### fitness@home — 2017-08-18T18:42:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-221888>

Stella - so excited for you. Congrats on retirement, especially while you can still enjoy it fully

You need to ship that RV over to Australia and toodle around with Audrey and her man! Oh, what fun you could have together...

We have just ordered a small RV and will be putting our property up for sale in a few weeks. Trying to juggle all the money is a bit scary ...but we are all trusting in the process!

I can't wait to sit under the stars at night - with a glass of wine around a fire pit ....and my dearest friend Stella and the man I adore!

Bring it on!

love you to the moon and back xo

### fitness@home — 2017-08-19T14:35:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-221935>

> Don't know at this point where we will end up but hopefully somewhere in Florida.

Stella, I adore the St George Island and Apalachicola, FL area. Far enough away from large cities, close enough when you need them.

Yes, there are cell towers but in this day and age, thete are very few places that don't have them.

### Stella — 2017-08-25T15:37:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-222227>

Hi Dawn, glad to see you're still around.

I did like that area around Apalachicola, my main concern are all the radar facilities because of the air base. We stayed at St Joe. Beautiful beach but just at the entrance to the cape there was a large radar, etc facility that belonged to the air force. So that just means I got to keep looking for a spot to land. I may have to drive all the way to Mexico to find a suitable spot.

One thing about being in a remote place, we slept so much better. My husband and I would dream all night long and remember the dreams in the morning. I'm talking about staying at primitive campsites with no hook ups, and very poor cell signal. Add a nice campfire at night, candle light with no TV at night and its the perfect recipe for a great nights sleep.

Another thing that we discovered we loved, soaking in the cool springs in central FL. There are just so many of them. We found that we felt so much better in that water. We would just find a spot in the sun and bask while soaking in the spring.

Intuitively, we are just gravitating to areas that feel good. It's not hard to see that you sleep better and dream all night. Its got to play a major role in health and recovery. Add to that sun exposure, living outside, eating according to my when I feel like eating. All of this moving in the right direction reinforces that we are doing what we need to do. Makes it easier to pull up the anchors that held us firmly in place. Just so many freaking anchors but the last ones are just coming up.

Hi JMO. Good to hear that you are thinking about hitting the road in an RV. It really does take a lot of effort to downsize and follow thru on your desires. The inertia of the status quo is very strong so your desire to live a different life has to be stronger. If it isn't you may find every excuse possible and not live your dream. Action is key.

I understand about health insurance, that is a big consideration. Fortunately I have retirement insurance benefits for me and my husband. It will probably last for about 4 to 5 years so we will worry about it when the time comes. There's also medical cost sharing plans, that is what I'm getting for my 2 sons. It's really affordable, $149 per person or $449 for a family. That's monthly cost.

next year it's OZ or bust!!!

### Stella — 2017-09-04T02:17:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-222821>

I've been making several changes, now that I have more time. The first has been taking long barefoot walks in a grassy field in the mornings. It's about 1 mile in total with half of the walk directly facing the early morning sun. So far it's been wonderful for my flat feet.

Oh and I've gotten rid of my work shoes for something a bit more sensible. My feet are feeling so much better already. Initially it was painful to walk barefoot but now it's uncomfortable to wear shoes. So I'm barefoot most of the day when I'm home.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-04T03:36:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-222825>

### Stella — 2017-09-04T06:21:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-222828>

One thing that changed as soon as I took a long trip in the RV, camping in some remote places, Also, sitting in many cool springs. I had to lower my thyroid medication by half. I also switched to a lower carb, sugar diet and most recently with intermittent fasting.

I do realize that lower carb diet could explain the need to cut the thyroid. It's amazing how a life lived outdoors changes health for the better.

So next month when we move into the RV full time, I will be living outdoors on an almost permanent basis. No smart meters, no home wiring, less nnEMF. Lots of fresh air, sun, hiking, biking swimming in springs. This might be the best decision of our lives.

I appreciate every second of the time we have known each other xo

wine and cigars soon!

After so many years of inflammation, my adrenals were working overtime and consequently knocked out my thyroid. Inflammatory lifestyle and its inevitable consequences. ### Stella — 2017-09-13T19:44:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-223569>

Interesting findings after having to leave Miami because of the incoming hurricane.

Ended up after several days on the road at a friends lake house outside of Charlotte NC. First thing I noticed after driving up to a remote area in central Florida, my sleep improves and my entire family is having and remembering vivid dreams.

Fast forward to NC. We are in a really nice area, many vacation lake homes here. Obviously much less population density vs back in S Florida. We were able to get a few days of sun so I've been sitting outside on the lawn grounding while getting morning sun.

I normally have a glass of water with 1 tsp of Celtic sea salt first thing after waking. I will usually take more salt at other times during the day. I find that after being here just a few days I only need 1/2 tsp of salt in the morning only. Also, I normally would get up twice during the night to go to the bathroom. Now I'm sleeping thru the night with no full bladder. I'm also noticing that even though I'm still drinking a good amount of water, I'm not having to run to the bathroom as often. I bet my adrenal function has improved in only a few days!

I may never go back to the hell of S Florida after this experience!

### caroline — 2017-09-14T11:04:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-223600>

So glad that you all are safe ....and in fact thriving! xo

### Stella — 2018-04-17T07:55:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stellas-journey-to-health.10007/post-239490>

Haven’t posted in a long while but think I should update my situation. Since being introduced to Jack and his blogs, one thing has been stressed constantly, the importance of ones zip code or location. It’s not something that most can easily change and many times you’re just exchanging a bad situation for something that isn’t entirely optimal.
In my case and I will include my husband in this as well, we no longer felt it was good for our health and well-being to continue to live in the south Florida area. To make a long story short, over the course of 2 years we sold our home, moved out my youngest son, bought a small motor home and quit our jobs. We moved into our RV in October and left Florida in December.
Our current living situation or where we park our RV is mostly free BLM or forest service land. It’s usually free which is great since we are no longer working. Plus there’s usually poor cell coverage in these areas. We no longer worry about WiFi, smart meters, 5G, house wiring etc. We spend most of our time in sparsely populated areas. And access to hiking trails is great in most places we choose, which means we are outdoors and exercising more than in our former lives. Our RV has solar panels but we only turn on the inverter when needed as most of our stuff runs on LP gas.
Most can’t just pick up and leave their lives as we have done. Just putting this out as an option. We bought our rig third hand on Craigslist as well as a car we tow.
I’m completely off my thyroid after first having to titrate dose down when we started camping. Being in remote places really does make a difference.
Also would like to add that there is a large nomadic community in this country that most would call homeless. They live in RVs, vans, trucks, school buses and cars. What is common to most of them is their love of being in the outdoors away from cities. They may not have all the information on the dangers of nnEMF but they’ve moved and made the best of things.
So when someone tells me it’s too difficult to change, we’re living proof that good change is possible.

# Thread: Sensation of hot water running down leg...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensation-of-hot-water-running-down-leg.10012/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2014-03-04T19:25:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensation-of-hot-water-running-down-leg.10012/post-113390>

What the fresh hell is this...
Feels like hot liquid running from right buttock down thigh to back of knee.

My poor pup that was on the couch behind me... I jumped up thinking she peed!

Googled it and got parasthesia...not much info to gather. It's happened probably 25-30 times since this morning.

What gives???

### kovita — 2014-03-04T19:32:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensation-of-hot-water-running-down-leg.10012/post-113392>

i have paresthesias regulary. Some weeks few some weeks more often. It is not very good sign, I think, it is connected with a whole bunch of chronic diseases. I have observed, that I get much more of it when I seem to be overhelmed by toxins. The clearing usually presents itself by moving deep electricity like feelings closer to the skin and it eventually clears as itchy feelings on the skin and than it disappears for some time. My "energetical "physio who works with me always connects this with relieving of deep tissue tensions and I can say that over the time I always emmerge out of this periods with a step forward in my subjective measurements of overall health. Very personal and very laic opinion here.

### cantweight — 2014-03-05T02:26:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensation-of-hot-water-running-down-leg.10012/post-113477>

You are totally right Kovita.... it is the toxins. The mold to be exact. Damn. I can not catch a break. Water has just started leaking from yet another light fixture in this house that I want to love so badly!! It is an amazing house and was not moldy when we moved here 18 months ago. Poor renovation work done has created a mess of water issues and we are paying the price. I was going to try to muscle through and deal with the problem as much as i could on my own but as my youngest has started stuttering again and developed nose bleeds I have no choice but to address this professionally now. ugh.

Oh well!!! At least it is something that can be addressed.....will count my blessings

### Dylan848 — 2016-07-20T06:32:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensation-of-hot-water-running-down-leg.10012/post-198653>

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=113390)
>
> What the fresh hell is this...
> Feels like hot liquid running from right buttock down thigh to back of knee.
>
> My poor pup that was on the couch behind me... I jumped up thinking she peed!
>
> Googled it and got parasthesia...not much info to gather. It's happened probably 25-30 times since this morning.
>
> What gives???

Hey was just wondering if your symptoms improved and if so what helped?

### cantweight — 2016-07-20T21:35:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensation-of-hot-water-running-down-leg.10012/post-198691>

> [Dylan848 said:](/goto/post?id=198653)
>
> Hey was just wondering if your symptoms improved and if so what helped?

I handled the situation very poorly if i am being honest. I thought I could focus on improving my redox, and muscle through the situation in our home...I regret that. Many wise people advised I have the house cleared of any issues and I did not heed their advice.

I pushed hard with a seafood based ketogenic diet, very very little carbohydrate, practiced CT and adhered to strict light blocking and sunning. I got worse....far worse. So did my family.

2 years since I had made this initial post I declined severely in energy, gained a lot of weight and was in much pain...nothing showed on my labs other than odd things like high C4a, slightly elevated hscrp. My skin (face) became covered in lesions that bled and pussed and burned relentlessly...no dermatologists could figure out what it was...many medications were prescribed, the last one being for freaking leprosy....nothing worked.

I broke down this past spring, told my husband I was fearful for what would come next as the rash began taking over my eyes. I told him to find a way to get me out of this house or fix it...fast.

We brought in experts and they found the house to be unsafe and told us any babies or elderly in the house must leave immediately...no babies or elderly just us for 3 fucking yrs in this mess. They found the HVAC in the attic was not hooked up properly and our air was full of fiberglass...one of the gentlemen that came said he'd seen someone with fiberglass in their air have the exact same facial rash i did. There were mold issues, mouse feces...every place we looked a new and even more expensive problem.

Our HVAC systems were repaired and UV light systems and high grade filters were installed on both, much drywall was replaced and repaired, windows that harbored a shocking amount of mold are being replaced...the house that I swore was trying to kill me is starting to feel like a home again. I actually have the desire to decorate and hang pictures where as previously I only had the desire to move again.

My whole family went back to our old doctor that I love...she is helping us improve our redox and as it improves helping us to slowly detoxify. I have been getting uv light IV treatments...they are amazing...after 2 treatments my skin was almost human again. My energy is increasing by the day. I respect that my body has a hierarchy at which it will address its states...I am simply supporting it now, not pushing too hard, and appreciating this rapid turn around.

Find what is in your environment driving your inflammatory symptoms...

### Dylan848 — 2016-07-21T01:07:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sensation-of-hot-water-running-down-leg.10012/post-198704>

Wow, well I'm glad to hear you're on your way to recovery, I'm sure you'll succeed.

I don't know how to go about evaluating and changing my home as I'm only 19 and still live with my parents :/

### Joeakim — 2016-07-27T20:18:31+0300

# Thread: Viruses and bacteria and the human race
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-and-bacteria-and-the-human-race.10013/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2014-03-04T21:03:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-and-bacteria-and-the-human-race.10013/post-113409>

I was already thinking about this post when Fitness@home posted yesterday about a new virus that has emerged and what other ones are laying dormant.

I am reading a book The Story of the Human Body (Evolution, Health and Disease) by Daniel E Lieberman on human evolution and started going back and reading the brain-gut series and a thought has popped into the head. The world is so obsessed with conserving life and not improving life are we ultimately hurting the human race by NOT letting bacteria and viruses force the genes to adapt for survival? In the book he talks about evolutionary mismatch and how the human genes are not able to keep up with our environment. In Brain gut 2, Jack talked about how viruses caused the body to make antibodies to survive and pass that trait on to the next generation. Those that do not make it do not pass that weak gene on. Maybe cancers and bacteria and viruses are around to strengthen the human genes and by us messing with it so much we are ultimately creating a weaker human.

I saw a documentary on the History channel back from 2005 call “The Plague” about the bubonic plague. It said that millions of people died from it but there were those who were unaffected even when in contact with the sick. These people then passed that gene on to the next generation. They started to look into why some people can be exposed to HIV and never develop AIDS and they found that their genetic history seems to point to the fact that their ancestors may have been ones that survived the plague and passed some genes down to them.

It is no longer survival of the fittest and natural selection but in some cases who has the most money. Don’t get me wrong, people dying early and of cancers and other diseases is horrible to see and so very painful for those that are left behind but is what we are doing now going to make this even worse for future generations (if we do not kill ourselves first from obesity and other mismatched diseases).

We are already seeing the effect with sicker children due to the over use of antibiotics and trying to keep everything sterile. Kids need to play in the dirt (how cow manure as I was told I did) and get their bodies exposed to all sorts of stuff to build up their immunities. I remember finding sand in our first daughters diaper and she had not been outside that day. She used to shovel spoonful of sand into her mouth.

Are we harming future generations by not allowing sickness and disease to make the human race stronger? Isn’t disease a way of population control? Just a thought.

### Josh — 2014-03-04T21:22:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-and-bacteria-and-the-human-race.10013/post-113412>

Watch this and look at the powerpoint:

[http://www.waterconf.org/participan.../Luc Montagnier Borovets October 23, 2013.ppt](http://www.waterconf.org/participants-materials/presentations/Luc%20Montagnier%20Borovets%20October%2023,%202013.ppt)

### Shijin13 — 2014-03-04T21:56:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-and-bacteria-and-the-human-race.10013/post-113418>

I'm immune to Norovirus. My cousin is working in Africa on AIDS - particularly the individuals who have developed immunity against the virus.... they're trying to figure out how it happened. I sent her the BG series... but when she broached the concept with the CDC folks the told her it doesn't happen that way.... She's still questioning their thought process and paradigms....

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-05T09:28:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/viruses-and-bacteria-and-the-human-race.10013/post-113528>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=113409)
>
> We are already seeing the effect with sicker children due to the over use of antibiotics and trying to keep everything sterile. Kids need to play in the dirt (how cow manure as I was told I did) and get their bodies exposed to all sorts of stuff to build up their immunities. I remember finding sand in our first daughters diaper and she had not been outside that day. She used to shovel spoonful of sand into her mouth.
>
> Are we harming future generations by not allowing sickness and disease to make the human race stronger? Isn’t disease a way of population control? Just a thought.

I think there are two different thoughts mixed up there, Nicole.

First, there's the thought that hyper-cleanliness is actually bad for us. (_That's a familiar thought now, and I'm sure it's true. Besides, the suggestion you make there's also the growing recognition that many bacteria are benign. Using some kind of powerful agent that kills everything -- a "scorched earth policy" -- can therefore be counter-productive. Completely harmless bacteria had been occupying space on your hands or in your mouth or gut or wherever. If you wipe them out along with the possibly harmful bugs, you can't know in advance what will expand to fill the space you've just made. It may be something harmful._)

Secondly, you kind of segue over to the find thought: "Isn’t disease a way of population control?"

This is coming out of the same general area -- thoughts about disease and how we cope with it, but it's not the _same_ thought. I think this one can take people into very dangerous areas. (I do think the relief of suffering is a duty we have.) It comes out of taking the notion of "the survival of the fittest" too seriously and too schematically. It's a hypothesis for explaining where we've come from; not a guide to action. It's comes from Darwinian biology (and the roots of it are in Malthus). Darwin himself tended to avoid the notion that natural selection could and ought to be applied socially. I say tended: sometimes people more eager to defend his reputation than well-informed say he always eschewed this; in fact, there are occasions when he didn't. But mostly he did. The person this is approach is strongly connected with is his cousin Francis Galton.

This is nowadays known as "Social Darwinism". Suffice to say, some 19th century gentleman were terribly worried that perhaps the poor, whom they assumed were less "fit", were reproducing too fast and should be discouraged. There were even people who thought that charitable relief should not be provided for the starving, this being supposedly an interference with "nature" (if not "the market").

These ideas get around. IIRC, as late as the 1970s Sweden had a pretty gung-ho policy of sterilising the "unfit" -- and who counted as unfit is something we'd best not go into here.

I can't go into it deeply here, but this whole notion contains a logical fallacy. Briefly, it's this: according to the theory, evolution by natural selection is what happens _always and everywhere_. There's something self-contradictory and ridiculous in saying: this is what always and everywhere holds … oh, and let's give it a helping hand. The Australian philosopher David Stove gives an in-depth discussion of this, and some other problems, that pop up in biological thinking here:

[http://www.amazon.com/Darwinian-Fairytales-Selfish-Heredity-Evolution/dp/1594032009/](http://www.amazon.com/Darwinian-Fairytales-Selfish-Heredity-Evolution/dp/1594032009/)

There has been a recent BBC podcast on the theme of Social Darwinism where Melvyn Bragg talks with three academics. I haven't heard it yet myself, but it's probably worth a listen for anyone who's interested in these themes:

[http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/b03vgq1q](http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/b03vgq1q)

It should be in iTunes or in the web in the BBC iPlayer.

# Thread: Newsworthy?  Do, tell....

### Tanya — 2014-03-04T21:51:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-113416>

I did not think I would end up starting a journal, but here I am.

I doubt if I will be writing in it all that often though, but at least, I am willing to give it a try.

It seems that those people here who achieved the most benefit from following Epi-Paleo Rx, all also have their journals. Even though, I can’t even bring myself to call it Tanya’s journal, I do need a quiet little corner of my own to sort things out, think out loud, invite others expertise and input.

So, you are all welcome in my corner!

### caroline — 2014-03-05T00:17:36+0200

We are all in each other's "corner" here.... how great is that....

### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-05T04:35:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-113494>

Why should you practice keeping your mind open to things and open to the world? It is a narrow mind which cannot look at a subject from various points of view. So if your mind is closed.......you'll never see the new and correct path. Those who cannot change their minds cannot change anything. And Intelligence is directly yoked to the ability to change. Change is a function of an open mindset. You mind functions just as a parachute does.......if it is closed your life ends faster than it might have.

### Tanya — 2014-03-05T18:57:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-113615>

Now, on the serious note....

I feel that in order for me to gain better clarity regarding my current efforts to get better, I need to describe how I happened to get to this point in the first place.

First of all, I’ve been sick for almost half of my life, but looking at me and what I was doing with myself at that time, you would never know that.

Surprisingly, I was a very healthy child growing back in the USSR. I say surprisingly, because now that “I know better” (JK), the living conditions were not supportive of that.

### Tanya — 2014-03-06T18:14:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-113794>

Cold Thermogenesis was not in my vocabulary until I discovered this site, but I was sort of doing it a little bit in my teen years, without even realizing at the time that that’s what it was.

As I keep going down the memory lane, our water supply back in the USSR was limited to only 2 hours each day - one in the morning (cold water only) and one with hot one in the evening. I loved my morning cold showers, even though they were brief and I could not even wash my hair while taking them - it was way too long and heavy and would not be dry in time for me to leave. For that we had our trips to banya - and I hated them with passion. While everyone else seemed to be so enjoying being there, I could not stand all that heat, steam, noise, and complete lack of any privacy.

Moving on to the drinking water. Come to think of it, I was not drinking that much plain water, at all. Instead, we always had plenty of store bought tomato juice, kvass, soda, and black tea.

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-06T20:16:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-113811>

Perhaps steam baths aren't a bad idea in themselves, though. They seem to be an established method of bathing for many hunter-gatherers. Best to finish with a cold plunge or a roll in the snow, though!

And I recall from a podcast that Jack himself takes infra-red saunas.

The history of it seems to be that the practice tended to retreat with the spread of Christianity. The early Christians could be very ascetic and, believe it or not, keeping yourself clean was actually frowned on as a form of luxury. This is perhaps a surprise to people who think of New England Puritans who, far from that, seem to have believed (equally extraordinarily) that "cleanliness is next to godliness". It's a fact, though. Possibly it was also felt that naked people might be getting up to who-knows-what in the steam bath.

The sauna, of course, comes from Finland which was evangelised very late, not till the late Middle Ages. (There are still traces of pagan beliefs in the the Finnish national epic, the _Kalevala_.) I've no idea why steam baths kept going in Russia, but the above seems to be the historical reason for their disappearance in most of what was Christendom.

Kvass doesn't sound too bad to me either. It tends to be recommended in those sectors of the Ancestral Health Movement that have actually heard of it. It's a lactic ferment and hence tends to help the digestion and so on, in the same way that sauerkraut and similar vegetable dishes do. But I guess a shop-bought kvass might not be authentic.

Apparently, the authentic version is making a comeback:

I've made the "beet kvass" before -- it didn't work very well.

### Tanya — 2014-03-06T21:26:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-113824>

Mike, back then steam bath and garlic were indisputable solutions for all kinds of health problems. I just did not feel that well while being exposed to all that heat and felt so much better taking cold showers.

Even though, kvass is a very healthy drink for some people, I am not so sure if I was one of them. It was made out of bread and I already was consuming way- way too much of it as it was.... I did love the taste of kvass though and was drinking a lot of it as well.

Now we can buy locally carrot or beet kvass, but of course it is not even close.....

### Tanya — 2014-03-12T17:56:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-115027>

Fast-forward a few years - walking on a street in Vienna and suddenly feeling a strange pain in my right ankle with no reason for it to be there.... Tried to ignore it for 2 days, until one morning the foot got swollen to the point that I could not even zip up my boots.

Luckily, I was able to use the old stand-by treatment from my ballet days and by the time we left Austria, I was fine again.

After that, we were so fortunate to spend 7 months living in Rome, Italy. Being born and lived all my life behind the “iron curtain”, it was my dream come true for me... There was so much food for my soul.....As far as bodily nourishment - we mostly relied on all kinds of pasta, pizzas and ice-cream - of course, all my favorites at that time.

We were on a crowded bus when something happened again. I started feeling very hot and somewhat dizzy and the next thing that I remembered I was outside and surrounded by all these strange people. The ambulance arrived and took me to the hospital. They treated me by giving me some injections - I have no idea what they were and what they were for.

### Tanya — 2014-03-24T18:48:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-117619>

Time to continue filling in a few more blanks....

So, when I finally got sick, I felt a huge sense of disbelief - it could only happen to the others around me, and never to me...

But not only it did so, but it happened when it should have been the best years of my life. It came in a form of a flu-like symptoms during the night spent in a high-rise hotel.

After a week or so, some of those symptoms were gone, but I kept feeling such tremendous fatigue and muscle pain. I tried to ignore it and kept thinking that what II need is to get more exercise. I was getting even more exhausted and the pain was spreading. I finally went to see a doctor who began a series of different tests in order to find out what was wrong.

First blood test seemed to be normal, except for two things: I was ANA positive, and my sedimentation rate of 110 was extremely high I was told. More blood tests followed as well as many X-rays, CT-scans, MRI. Still, no answers....except that all my symptoms were pointing into Dx of FA and CFS - and that what was given to me. I kept it to myself, telling everyone that they are still testing me - it just did not make any sense to me, and I went from one doctor to another trying to find out what was really wrong.

It was a demanding process - both time wise and financially, but each visit and new test gave me a new hope. After awhile, even that was gone. I kept getting worse....

### Tanya — 2014-03-27T22:24:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-118367>

For someone who has never been sick before, my medical history, in addition to CFS and FM at that point included so much more

As the time went by, I even learned how to cope with all of my symptoms until another crisis arrived in a form of a severe RA.

### sooperb — 2014-03-28T11:49:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-118452>

RA is the most pernicious of ailments, I sympathise, life must have been a real struggle.

### Tanya — 2014-03-28T17:11:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-118484>

Yes, Sue - it hit me pretty hard. I was really messed up by it, but in the beginning did not even realize that it was RA because I was tested for it just a few months prior to that happening. I attributed it (just like everything else) to the CFS and FM and did not seek immediate help for it until it spiraled out of control....

### Tanya — 2014-03-30T22:57:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-118857>

RA started in my upper body, but it did not even occur to me that that's what it was - they completely ruled it out after a recent test. So, I kept on going, as I usually do...

When pain in my shoulders became even stronger, I finally went to see a doctor, something I had difficult time forgiving myself for later because during that visit I ended up with torn rotator cuff and damaged scapula. Meanwhile, my RA was spreading to my elbows and hands., especially my hands. They were painful, swollen and red - folded into fists and kind of resembled red fighting gloves.

I went to see another rheumatologist and a new test confirmed that it was RA. I was put on massive doses of cortisone and Plaquenil, but soon the RA was also in my lower body - my hips, my ankles and my knees. It was a very aggressive kind - it took away my ability to move and to use my hands.

### caroline — 2014-03-31T07:24:31+0300

I also love ..."if our Hands and heart are open - we will receive gifts.
I am living proof of this every single day ....and so are you.

I think I am not destined to speak chinese only to eat it so this may be as far as it goes for me but who knows what tomorrow brings.
Take heart !

### Inger — 2014-04-03T19:54:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-119638>

> [Tanya said:](/goto/post?id=119471)
>
> I don’t even recall now how I happened to learn about Jack Kruse and his site. I do remember, however, thinking how different it was from everything that I have come across in my search so far. I began to read the blog, but very quickly felt completely overwhelmed by it, with a huge amount of all the material for Epi-paleo Rx and especially all the science behind it. I still felt that way even after reading Jack’s book and decided then that I would not be able to go on with it. My brain fog was (still is) too severe for that and reading this blog while experiencing this terrible cognitive skills decline is the most challenging thing for me right now. It is so incredibly difficult to even comprehend the material, never mind try to retain it, and it does not matter how many times I go over it. Reading other people’s discussions and analyses of it just underscores to me even more how much of my brain function is lost. It frustrates me so much and makes me feel so inadequate and very sad...
>
> I am hanging on though, and fortunately, even with only some of the changes that I was able to make so far, I can tell already how much it is helping me physically. So, I keep trying to convince myself that with even more of these changes to come, I might be able to restore some of my brain function as well.

Tanya... just follow your heart, live through it when your brain is still not well- do the good stuff, and your brain will heal. I am 100 % certain about that. Mother Nature is one amazing doctor
I can tell you I am not smart either! But I know in my heart what will make me heal, and I do that. It is all I need. You too  I cannot even tell how glad I am that it is so easy... almost too easy to believe it could work. Go out barefoot in the sun.... eat some raw fish.. go for a swim in the river... drink clean water...  Also... when we get silent and listen, we will hear what our body needs.
I believe our heart always win over the brain, our courage, our hopes and our thoughts, what we believe in. When the heart is on fire it does not matter if the brain is not optimal yet

### Tanya — 2014-04-09T18:19:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-120632>

Earlier this morning I had a chance to watch an interview with Jeff Bauman and his fiancé.

He lost both of his legs the Boston marathon bombing and now he is telling his story of survival and tremendous courage in his new book "Stronger" - definitely on my " must read" list. Just listening to him today helped to enrich my mind and to fill my heart ....

There is also a new Russian movie coming out about one of my favourite artists - Marc Chagall. What an incredibly talented and at the same time a truly humble man he was. Another must see for me.

### caroline — 2014-04-11T01:40:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-120889>

I got burns because I didn't have anything underneath! Ice vests are awesome ...and yes of course they have Dr. K.'s approval. Anyway you can do C/T - go for it. In the easy start guide he talks bout putting ice packs on your body.

There are earlier hilarious posts about where folks put icepacks!!! that is about the time that Eric developed the ice vest - same thing - only better. around the neck is super important!

if you read the comments in Jack's new blog - he told me that Jeremy does C/T on the mountain.

I never get ice burns when I used my own ice packs and I put them right on bare skin .... but the cooling, commercial packs are a different story.
