### Tanya — 2014-04-11T16:49:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-120989>

Thanks Audrey. I am pretty sure there are some hormonal issues..... Have not done any recent testing though....
What makes me think that my problem stems from my lack of CT are these thoughts that I have:
1\. I have never ever had a weight problem before, still don't, except for the boobs which mostly consist of the fat tissue. So, I hugely increased the amount of fat in my diet, but did not do enough CT to offset it...

As far as avoiding a compression shirt, I am just being cautions, perhaps overcautious here? I'd rather avoid compressing my breasts. I use here the same logic that I did when I stopped having mammograms done because of this other risk involved in addition to the radiation exposure

> **The Compression Reason**
>
> Another raging debate is over _how_ mammograms are administered, which is a very important issue but rarely talked about. In order to get a clear picture of inner breast tissue, a technician must position the patient’s breast in a vise-like device, then squeeze the breast to an almost inhuman degree. Make no mistake, the physical compression is severe. The breast is first compressed in one direction, then for the second angle, it is compressed in another direction. The main concern over this is that some medical experts believe the compression force may rupture any existing cancer cells that are contained in a localized mass, and cause some malignant cells to be released into the woman’s bloodstream, thereby promoting metastatic cancer. For any woman this might happen to, it would be a disaster. A localized tumor that grows over time to where a woman can detect it through self-examination, but stays contained, is much more easily cured than a mass caught earlier but caused to spread throughout the body due to compression.

### sooperb — 2014-04-12T17:02:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-121124>

I don't think mammograms have a particularly high success rate either. Routine screening for anything has come under fire lately as being ineffective not to mention costly.

I have thought a few times about the Fournier effect La, I considered that I could also combine it with an Epsom salt bath, the crystals probably dissolve better in warm water. Which bits, apart from the abdomen, can you rest the ice on? Jack always talks in weight, use 20lbs for example and reduce skin temperature to around 50 deg. I used just some ice packs that some frozen food was delivered in and they took my skin down to 42 deg. There certainly wasn't anything like 20 lb of them. I'll have another read.

### Tanya — 2014-04-16T23:12:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-121989>

Had a great day today. Went downstairs into my gym to do my Pilates routine. When I slided the door open into the yard and saw that the snow from last night has melted I just stepped out on a grass as I was - barefoot and in my shorts and short-sleeve top. The music inside was loud enough for me to hear and I just felt like dancing. So, I did my version of Zingarella. Here is one done by Gina Lollobrigida - love it.

### Tanya — 2014-04-17T18:46:57+0300

Yes, we do, Audrey and I strongly recommend it!!!  Putting aside all the joy that I felt during that time, and simply looking at that based on Jack’s teachings - I must have collected lots of free electrons by doing that, so many of the elements were there: drinking my coherent water prior to that, the sunshine, cold air, wet grass....

Maybe, it was even the most meaningful test that I so needed for myself - even though it was far from being as good as I would have danced it in the past.... ### Tanya — 2014-04-18T22:39:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-122423>

I’ve been completely without Armour thyroid for an entire week now.  I know that it is too early to make any conclusions, but so far, I have not seen any negative effects as a result of that. My temperature, which was low anyway while I was taking it, still remains the same; skin, hair, nails - all normal. My ability to tolerate cold is still the same, actually I think it is even better - I felt warm sleeping at 62F. I also noticed that I am beginning to perspire during my exercise - where before it was not happening. I hope, that it is indeed a sign of improvement.

Changes in my supplements: I no longer give myself B-12 shots and replaced them with sublingual one instead.

In addition to that, after many years of non-stop taking various probiotics, I now stopped taking them altogether. That was a huge step for me to take and I was really concerned, but I don’t seem to have any problem with doing that - Bristol score #4 every day.

I’ve been testing brasil nots - taking them every day and not reacting to them either. When I’ll finish my selenium bottle, I guess I will not be reordering it.

Today, I increased my cocoa to 2 tsp/day - I have a long way to go there....

### Tanya — 2014-04-23T18:25:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-123207>

There are still no changes with me not being on Armour Thyroid anymore....
I continue with my goal to significantly limit the amount of all the supplements that I've been taking....while being guided in this process by only how it makes me feel - still no lab testing at this time....

I've been slowly reducing the amount of the digestive enzymes (I was taking them and probiotics for many years) - went from taking 2 X 3/day to just one /day now. I plan to stop it completely by the end of this week. I guess, I will have to wait to retest my reaction to the egg yolks until I am sure about the enzymes.

I started wondering if it is it possible that my leaky gut is finally healed, even without me taking any specific supplements for that (e.i. glutamine, aloe vera)?! Hmmm, I don't know, but whatever it is, I hope that it would stay that way....As Dr. Dyer said: "My goal is not to be better then anyone else, but to be better than I used to be"

### Tanya — 2014-04-27T22:27:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-123596>

They say “ a little knowledge is a dangerous thing”. I am definitely experiencing something like that. As I go deeper into trying to limit my EMF exposure, I don’t know what to do about all the metal that is just about everywhere around me. I am especially concerned about my home GYM. Some of the equipment in there is now unplugged and is no longer being used, but I am still surrounded by all that metal while I am doing my workout. I am now trying to limit my time being there where before it was one of my favorite places to be.
Am I overthinking it now? It seemed so clear and straightforward in the beginning what needed to be done with all the tech stuff in order to limit the emf exposure, but with the transition metals I feel as if whatever I will do, it would still be just a drop in the bucket.

### Tanya — 2014-05-06T02:54:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-125385>

I had to take it easy for a while because of my swollen ankles and knees - it happened because I attempted to do a full split thinking that I was ready. I am not going to revisit that anytime soon. For now, though, I need to decide what to do with my inversion table. My husband thinks that I should get rid of it because my ankles and knees will not allow me to, use it anymore either. I still hope that in time it will be possible.... I used to love it so much - feeling so energized afterwards...

I am really tolerating cold so well now via air, ice and water that I seem to feel even too warm at times. I hope that somehow that will settle down before the weather gets really hot around here...

My appetite is changing also and I am not sure what it means yet - I no longer feel the need to eat my lunch. Maybe, I should now give it up altogether? I am not trying to loose any weight, so I need to figure that out better....

### Tanya — 2014-05-07T17:16:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-125643>

Looks like it is going to be a gorgeous day today! I took a short walk this morning before breakfast - just to get that sunlight in. Came across some early birds - people getting ready to drive off who were scraping off their cars windshields. We exchanged pleasantries, but they were staring at me so much, it was almost funny - I think it is because was in a short-sleeves top. I guess, from now on, that is all that I probably need to have in my wardrobe - very light, summer-like things. I have not even touched any of my cashmere sweaters this winter. I have no idea what I am going to do with all of that winter clothing that I have....

I would have loved to have my inversion table moved outside today. Last night I tried it again after not being able to do it for a long time at all. I only did it at the 90 degrees angle, but it is a start..... When I attempted to move it to 45 - I felt it in my ankles, but not in my knees. I hope, it will be better today.

I had some steamed mussels for yesterday for dinner and again this morning. Taste vise, I definitely like scallops and even steamed oysters much better, but I wanted to try mussels also. I hope that they did me some good, anyway.

Looking forward to dinner already...

### Tanya — 2014-05-21T21:50:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-127866>

Hi Dawn! It is one of the easiest tests to do at home....but I don't know how reliable it is though. I use a product called "zinc status" (you can get it from the Amazon). You swish some of it in your mouth. If you can taste it, and especially if you feel that it tastes unpleasant to you - you probably don't have any zinc deficiency. I've passed it every single time I took it, but I was supplementing with zinc at that time. Will see if it will continue that way now that I am off the supplement.

I continue with my rather bold experiment with my Armour thyroid. It's been over a month now that I don't take any and my temperature has been slowly, very slowly going up.

### Tanya — 2014-05-22T22:30:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-127997>

This is from a book I am reading, and so I am just parking this information here for now about the VIVAXIS. It is described in detail in Judy Jacka's book "the Vivaxis connection".

The idea is that energy continues to flow between us and our place of origin (our birthplace) throughout our life, like a two-way umbilical cord of magnetic waves - it develops within us as a fetus and forever links us to our place of birth.

For the healing purposes, you need to pick up your vivaxis and anchor it someplace else in your new life - then allow the energy of this new site to cleanse and clear your body....

Another thing that I learned today is about lamb. Ours is mostly from New Zealand or Australia, but this is what they said about the one raised in France or Britain, especially Brittany and Normandy in France, such as by Mount-Saint-Michel: "The sheep advance and retreat with the tides, eating the grasses and other salt-tolerant plants of the coastal marshes. The marine taste is often said to include iodine - it is completely different taste from other lambs".

### sooperb — 2014-05-22T23:40:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-128006>

Salt marsh lamb is prized for its taste although I honestly can't tell the difference. I like lamb, cooked properly it is a lovely meat, cooked badly and it is chewy and tough. We use an excellent butcher who knows exactly where his meat is sourced and knows most of the farmers who raise it. He's expensive but worth it.

### Hope — 2014-05-23T00:20:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-128012>

> [Tanya said:](/goto/post?id=127997)
>
> This is from a book I am reading, and so I am just parking this information here for now about the VIVAXIS. It is described in detail in Judy Jacka's book "the Vivaxis connection".
>
> The idea is that energy continues to flow between us and our place of origin (our birthplace) throughout our life, like a two-way umbilical cord of magnetic waves - it develops within us as a fetus and forever links us to our place of birth.
>
> For the healing purposes, you need to pick up your vivaxis and anchor it someplace else in your new life - then allow the energy of this new site to cleanse and clear your body....
>
> Another thing that I learned today is about lamb. Ours is mostly from New Zealand or Australia, but this is what they said about the one raised in France or Britain, especially Brittany and Normandy in France, such as by Mount-Saint-Michel: "The sheep advance and retreat with the tides, eating the grasses and other salt-tolerant plants of the coastal marshes. The marine taste is often said to include iodine - it is completely different taste from other lambs".

Wow. I didn't know that about the birth place. How does it say to anchor your new place? Glad you can move it bc I was born in the B\*g Ap\*le....

And the lamb.......no surprise. Lots of French food just seems....better.

I bet when you get optimal you can pick up your trips again

### Tanya — 2014-05-26T19:43:48+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-11T01:09:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-137507>

Today's PSA is about probability neglect — our inability to properly grasp a proper sense of peril and risk — which often leads us to overstate the risks of relatively harmless activities, while forcing us to overrate more dangerous ones. Biology makes this error often and does not realize it. Why am I sharing the picture? A thought is absurd by what it seeks but is great by what it is. A thought is an endocrine secretion formed and containing electrons from your brain. Those electrons contain energy and information for you to share with others. Biology can not measure thoughts so they do not count on them to alter our DNA or epigenome. They do.

### Tanya — 2014-08-11T19:47:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-137567>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=137507)
>
> Today's PSA is about probability neglect — our inability to properly grasp a proper sense of peril and risk — which often leads us to overstate the risks of relatively harmless activities, while forcing us to overrate more dangerous ones. Biology makes this error often and does not realize it. Why am I sharing the picture? A thought is absurd by what it seeks but is great by what it is. A thought is an endocrine secretion formed and containing electrons from your brain. Those electrons contain energy and information for you to share with others. Biology can not measure thoughts so they do not count on them to alter our DNA or epigenome. They do.

Jack, I appreciate this very thought provoking PSA about probability neglect.

I must admit that I have never given that much thought before to this entire process, but I imagine that there are several factors at play when people form their decisions, such as other people’s influence in their lives and what media encourages people to think and believe. Unless that someone has the ability to think for himself, of course…. More and more rare occurrence these days. Made me raise my eyebrows so many times, especially lately - that alone should qualify as my entire exercise routine. lol

Interesting still, as I learned today from you how a thought is formed from a quantum prospective.  I always equated it with a tiny voice in my head that was telling me what to do or not to do. Kind of like what Vincent Van Gogh once said: If you hear a voice within you say “you cannot paint” then by all mean paint, and the voice will be silenced.

Thank you again for sharing this.

### Tanya — 2014-09-14T23:53:46+0300

### notsoperfick — 2014-09-16T16:02:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newsworthy-do-tell.10014/post-141154>

For those who don't have 6 minutes to watch the video:

**Abraham Lincoln’s Letter to His Son’s Teacher**

He will have to learn, I know, that all men are not just, all men are not true.
But teach him also that for every scoundrel there is a hero;
that for every selfish Politician, there is a dedicated leader…
Teach him for every enemy there is a friend,

Steer him away from envy, if you can, teach him the secret of quiet laughter.

Let him learn early that the bullies are the easiest to lick… Teach him, if you can,
the wonder of books… But also give him quiet time to ponder the eternal mystery of birds in the sky, bees in the sun, and the flowers on a green hillside.

In the school teach him it is far more honourable to fail than to cheat…
Teach him to have faith in his own ideas, even if everyone tells him they are wrong…
Teach him to be gentle with gentle people, and tough with the tough.

Try to give my son the strength not to follow the crowd when everyone is getting on the band wagon…Teach him to listen to all men…but teach him also to filter all he hears on a screen of truth, and take only the good that comes through.

Teach him if you can, how to laugh when he is sad…Teach him there is no shame in tears,
Teach him to scoff at cynics and to beware of too much sweetness…
Teach him to sell his brawn and brain to the highest bidders but never to put a price-tag
on his heart and soul.

Teach him to close his ears to a howling mob and to stand and fight if he thinks he’s right.
Treat him gently, but do not cuddle him, because only the test of fire makes fine steel.

Let him have the courage to be impatient…let him have the patience to be brave.
Teach him always to have sublime faith in himself, because then he will have
sublime faith in mankind.

This is a big order, but see what you can do…He is such a fine fellow,
my son!

Such a lot of wisdom, thank you for linking.

### Shijin13 — 2014-03-05T01:22:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/would-anyone-else.10015/post-113462>

Hawaii is 5hrs behind California. Germany is 7hrs ahead. Diego Garcia is 10hrs ahead.

Hawaii is 2 hours earlier than California this time of year. 5 hours earlier than East Coast.

**Labs- Feb. 20, 2014 (old labs in green)

Symptoms as of 3/04/2014:**
Up ten pounds this winter 135 to 145 lbs. mostly in stomach area.
Ringing in ears
Performed 1- mile VO2 max test ; scored low average, need to repeat - artifact?
Fatigue increase from a few months ago
Sleep 9.5 to 10.5 hours rather soundly, feel refreshed slightly upon rising, gone in less than two hours.
Dreaming is good, neither unpleasant nor anxious
Nails brittle and split somewhat, hair thin as ever
Back in the g\*\*\*\*amn diapers again, worse than before
Struggling mentally and really kinda bummed out, but I take solace in knowing why, so my cheerfulness really shines through.

### Shijin13 — 2014-03-05T00:59:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-redox-potential-labs.10016/post-113452>

Martin

I love your lipid panel! your potassium seems to be solid, and your Bun/Creatine ratio has creeped up a bit. DHEA-S is up! IGF-1: my n=1 shows my IGF-1 gets worse as my SHBG goes up, and it gets worse in winter..... and IIRC Tim Jackson said something about IGF-1 decreasing when SHBG goes up due to excess estrogens.... VitD - last year I biohacked not taking D during the winter and I tanked as well. I know I need it to help me counter balance winter. I wonder if your redox isn't as shot as you think it might be....

Things I've noticed this winter. I've been more tired. I'm also feeling inflamed what with the whole sudden onset peripheral nuropathy, yet my hscrp is .o2! and I'm not showing any elevated antibodies or any other infections - yet I'm having inflammation symptoms. why? I think this year - I've become more sensitive to the sun, due to paying attention to the circadian cycles. I've noticed an uptick in seeking/needing time in it and even just a short while recharges me. add to all this and my sudden thyroid craziness (hello 30lbs of weight gain boo) and monstrous aversion to cold... things haven't been as good as last year. Sex drive is in the toilet, but it has started to pick up with the increased light during the day.... my Redox is in the tank but fully recoverable... I just need sunlight and lots of it... I'm just considering this a bump along my journey...

### Martin — 2014-03-05T17:37:35+0200

When I saw the SHGB result I did some research and decided to keep trying to balance through what I'm learning here and test again. I'm hesitant to supplement, but I sure did enjoy shooting up T last year! He may have a more informed opinion these days, but I pulled this from JanSz posts on allthingsmale 1.5 years ago:

_Nobody, nobody I know of, have a practical way of addressing SHBG.

Definitely do not use Danazol or Stanozolol or anything that just directly kills SHBG.

You have to create environment that will tel your liver to slow down with SHBG production.

One way is to load up on external testosterone (must be shots, it is lots of T).

That is not really elegant solution, but only way I know and it actually work if you decide to use it, and able to use (iron).

\------------
So the amount of produced SHBG is outcome of a sum total of everything else and nothing singularly specific.

Assuming relatively healthy person without unusual genetic or hereditary problems,
In my observation most guys could benefit from
changing life style to the way that we are designed
eat food that is compatible with our design
monitor frequently the outcome:
micronutrients, fatty acids, phospholipids
on top
of the usual
Lipid, Steroid, thyroid Panels_

### Josh — 2014-03-05T22:06:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/martins-redox-potential-labs.10016/post-113645>

> Thyroid hormone deficiency affects all tissues of the body, including multiple endocrine
> changes that alter growth hormone, corticotropin, glucocorticoids, and gonadal function.
> Primary hypothyroidism is associated with hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, which is
> reversible with thyroid hormone replacement therapy. In male children, folliclestimulating
> hormone (FSH) is elevated and associated with testicular enlargement
> without virilization. Men with primary hypothyroidism have subnormal responses of
> luteinizing hormone (LH) to gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) administration and
> normal response to human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG). Free testosterone
> concentrations are reduced in men with primary hypothyroidism and thyroid hormone
> replacement normalizes free testosterone concentrations. In men with primary
> hypothyroidism, prolactin is not consistently elevated (except in men and children with
> longstanding severe primary hypothyroidism), but prolactin declines following thyroid
> hormone replacement therapy. Thyroid hormone is known to affect sex hormone-binding
> hormonal globulin (SHBG) concentrations. Men with hyperthyroidism have elevated
> concentrations of testosterone and SHBG. Thyroid hormone therapy in normal men may
> also duplicate this elevation. In addition, estradiol elevations are observed in men with
> hyperthyroidism, and gynecomastia is common in them as well. In contrast to patients
> with primary hypothyroidism, men with hyperthyroidism exhibit hyperresponsiveness of
> LH to GnRH administration and subnormal responses to hCG. Radioactive iodine
> therapy (RAI) of men treated for thyroid cancer produces a dose-dependent impairment
> of spermatogenesis and elevation of FSH up to approximately 2 years. Permanent
> testicular germ cell damage may occur in men treated with high doses of RAI. RAI
> commonly increases serum concentrations of FSH and LH while reducing inhibin B
> levels without affecting serum concentrations of testosterone. Thus, radioiodine therapy
> transiently impairs both germinal and Leydig cell function that usually recover by 18
> months post therapy.108
> Although hypothyroidism is not common in men, it does occur and it typically leads to
> severe alterations in male hormone profiles. The impacts of thyroid hormones on male
> androgenic pathways are so diverse that potentially any abnormal pattern could be
> secondary to thyroid dysfunction. These patterns become difficult to evaluate as a
> thyroid problem since the male's dysfunctional androgen disorders are the presenting
> symptomology. Thyroid hormones can directly cause abnormal male hormone
> imbalances. On the other hand, when Hashimoto's is identified in a male, it is common
> to see antibodies against the testicular cells, testosterone, and/or sperm. Therefore, the
> connection with abnormal male hormone patterns and thyroid can be directly related to
> abnormal thyroid hormone influences, or from a polyendocrine autoimmune disorder
> that is disrupting both thyroid and testicular function.

Seminar Notes

# Thread: shivering and mitochondria
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shivering-and-mitochondria.10017/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Julie5 — 2014-03-05T00:59:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shivering-and-mitochondria.10017/post-113451>

I just read in Ask Jack that shivering after CT is a sign of poor mito. So do I just stay the course: continue CT iin 55 deg water for 45 min until it gets better? Or cut back? I shiver in the water some, but the shivering afterward is worse and lasts longer than the 45 min I was in the water.

### Shijin13 — 2014-03-05T01:05:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shivering-and-mitochondria.10017/post-113454>

Everyone shivers. I've been having such a hard time with CT this winter - I've found I shiver just in my house (we live in an old 1950s post war construction w/ single pane windows and the warmest part of the house may be in the 60's - the rest of the house is in the 50s!) hang in there... you can try backing the temp off, or go for a shorter period. When I'm in my CT groove I prefer colder temps. Though right now I'm fighting some really temp challenges. We all take a few steps forward, and a few steps back....

### nonchalant — 2014-03-05T01:07:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shivering-and-mitochondria.10017/post-113456>

Maybe the Mitochondrial Rx will help? I shiver also, for a lot longer than I was in the tub, unless I'm outside basking in the sun afterwards.

### nicld — 2014-03-05T03:47:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shivering-and-mitochondria.10017/post-113485>

> [Julie5 said:](/goto/post?id=113451)
>
> I just read in Ask Jack that shivering after CT is a sign of poor mito. So do I just stay the course: continue CT iin 55 deg water for 45 min until it gets better? Or cut back? I shiver in the water some, but the shivering afterward is worse and lasts longer than the 45 min I was in the water.

I think I asked that question or at least something like it. I used to get full body shakes when I got out but I no longer shiver like I used to and now can do deep CT. It will get better most of the time.

Gretchen, when did you start on the Lithium Orotate? I have been finding not dealing with the cold well the past couple of days and I just started it.

### Don — 2014-03-05T14:13:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shivering-and-mitochondria.10017/post-113569>

Stay the course ... When I started CT, 5-10 minutes in 55 degree water would leave me shivering uncontrollably for 2-3 hours. It stayed that way for a long time. About a year or so into CT I felt the heater turn-on. Now the shivering, after a much longer CT at cooler temps, is almost nonexistent. As your body adapts to what Jack is teaching us, I have found playing with water and fat intake helps. It apparently takes a while for our bodies to adapt and be able to uncouple the electron transport chain to get the heater to kick in. There are no small details on this path, and all of them need to be tested on your n=1.

# Thread: Fake Hormone primer:  Nuvaring
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fake-hormone-primer-nuvaring.10018/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-05T03:54:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fake-hormone-primer-nuvaring.10018/post-113487>

Dear Dr. Dach: Thank you for the post. My 29-year-old daughter died while she was on NuvaRing from blood clots in her lungs last year. She had been married for just 5 weeks when she became became so ill she had to go to the ER. They took the NuvaRing out, but nothing the doctors did could help and she died within 24 hours of being in the hospital.

The warnings on these prescription inserts and packages to call your doctor or go the hospital are not enough.

The FDA has accepted studies that show the least risk to women, instead of the studies that show there is a higher risk. Why they choose to ignore the higher risks and to allow Merck to minimize the true risk is beyond me. I do not trust the FDA as they are not protecting women.

I also do not understand why the medical community and others continue to compare blood clots for women who are pregnant against women who are trying not to get pregnant. One would think that people could understand that women who use birth control are trying not to get pregnant. So why not inform women about the safest birth control?

Nothing will ever replace the loss of my daughter, and I hope other young women will pay attention to the fact that all hormonal birth control has risk and that third and fourth generation progestins have a higher risk.

Thank you again for writing about this important issue.

Sincerely, D

to read the original article: Bad News for the NuvaRing .......[http://jeffreydachmd.com/bad-news-nuvaring-jeffrey-dach-md/](http://jeffreydachmd.com/bad-news-nuvaring-jeffrey-dach-md/)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fake-hormone-primer-nuvaring.10018/post-113606>

How right you are Audrey. Their is so much clouded perception around the medical arena I despair. Example. FDA announced a couple of years ago that they were withdrawing an anti-hypertensive because it was problematic in some cases. It hit the news in the UK and lo, OH was taking it. I told him to contact his GP straight away. He didn't but he asked the next time he was there for a check up of his diabetes. There's no problem with it he was told, just keep taking it. Less than six months later, urgent call from the surgery, don't take any more of it, come in and get a prescription for a different one.

I swear I don't know why my husband has such a mental block about these things, just like his statins! He's not a stupid man but I don't think he has the same enquiring mind around medical stuff as I do. Given that most people have complete faith and utter acceptance that their GP couldn't possibly be wrong, it's easy to see how big pharma make so much money. When there is an issue, most of the people slavishly taking their products will not be in line for compensation if something goes wrong, the GP won't be in trouble for prescribing it because NICE said it was ok to do so, so ultimately no one carries the can. No matter that someone lost a life, a limb or their sanity because they took it.

# Thread: Sharon's Epi Paleo/Sugar Detox Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharons-epi-paleo-sugar-detox-journal.10019/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### shuvelle2@yahoo.com — 2014-03-05T04:04:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharons-epi-paleo-sugar-detox-journal.10019/post-113490>

After digging jelly beans out of the trash so that I could indeed finish the bag and eating sugary stuff every night for the past 2 weeks, I've had it. I formally start a 21-day sugar detox tomorrow.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-05T04:21:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharons-epi-paleo-sugar-detox-journal.10019/post-113491>

welcome........get a pic up. It is a new rule. This will be the last comment I make in this thread until the picture is up.

And instead of waiting for tomorrow start now. When you convince yourself the answer is not now, the result is never achieved.
You don't have to see the whole staircase, just take the first step. You are what you do, and not what you say you'll do tomorrow.
Do you "mine" your potential constantly or do you let is lie as an under appreciating asset? Those who leave their potential untapped don't add value to life and they can't get the best from life. Those who maximize their potential live full and die empty. We often underestimate our capacity for change. There is never a right time to do a difficult thing. Our job in life is to help ourselves create a vision to unleash our potential to help others. Our main task in life is to give birth to ourself, to become what we potentially might be. Discovering our potential removes us from false realities, and brings us back to the truth of our core. Our only limits are the ones we place upon us. Realize today, that mining your potential is on you. Excel at learning who you are. Practice being the ultimate you. Rise up today and mine that gold.

We humans can and do lie to ourselves to soothe our emotions often. But what also is true that humans also implicit know when things are not right with them. Optimal health is a lot like pornography......you know it when you see and feel it simultaneously. That sensation should guide us........and the better we take of our brain the more I believe it does. This is why adversity is critically important in re creating wellness. Adversity cause some to break and others to break records. Who you choose to be is your choice. When you have to make a choice and don't make it, that in itself is a choice. So you still want to waste your time and try tomorrow something that should happen right now?

### caroline — 2014-03-05T04:30:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharons-epi-paleo-sugar-detox-journal.10019/post-113493>

welcome shuvelle! What can we help you with to get started?

We have all been there.......you are definitely not alone!

### nonchalant — 2014-03-05T04:42:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharons-epi-paleo-sugar-detox-journal.10019/post-113497>

Welcome, Sharon! You can do it!

### caroline — 2014-03-05T04:47:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharons-epi-paleo-sugar-detox-journal.10019/post-113498>

I was a binge eater most of my life .... I can relate to picking something out of the garbage! been there - done that!

### sooperb — 2014-03-05T17:35:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharons-epi-paleo-sugar-detox-journal.10019/post-113608>

Oh the shame, well I was shamed, now I know I'm not the only one to have done that I feel so much better lol. I've never sunk as low as to eat cooking chocolate though, it's so awful I would rather go without. I think the easiest way to stop with sugar is to just stop. Full stop. If you don't buy it, you can't eat it. That means none in the house, not for your husband or your children or your friend when she visits, none. I have a sister who has spent a lifetime not eating this and not eating that. Whenever she visited, her first words through the door were, "Have you got any biscuits, sweets, chocolate etc." Eventually she just bypassed the request and went straight to the cupboards.

One day she came round to collect me as we were going off for a walk. On the table was a packet of chocolate biscuits, new, unopened (the bad old days you understand). In ten minutes, she had eaten half a packet and some chocolate. Ask her though and she will tell you, she never eats them. More accurately, she never eats her own because she never buys biscuits lol. Her other trick was to ignore the biscuits if they were out when we were having tea or coffee. Then she would take one and break it in half, "See me, I'm good, I'm restricting myself to only half a biscuit". The other half of the biscuit would sit there until her resolve broke and she ate it. Then she would break another one in half, repeat the cycle. This went on until she had eaten at least two or three biscuits. She would then declare she couldn't possibly eat any more because she was full. Self delusion at it's best!

### sooperb — 2014-03-05T17:36:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sharons-epi-paleo-sugar-detox-journal.10019/post-113609>

Oops, welcome Sharon, to this life changing forum full of supportive members all willing to help you achieve.

# Thread: Acupressure mat, WOW!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acupressure-mat-wow.10021/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-03-05T08:42:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/acupressure-mat-wow.10021/post-113525>

hey,
So, after telling caroline i wouldnt use one cos i didnt think they would do anything for me (there was a thread started by caroline earlier, because dave asprey is now manufacturing 'acupressure mats') but i had a look on the inet, and found them available in NZ - the brand is 'shanti'. $40 a piece, what do i have to lose!

Ok, im the first to admit im not using it every nite, but also, its not nearly as painful as it looks - or feels. touching it definitely hurts more than lying on it!

What really blew me away though, was taking it away to my last race and the boys all saw it at some point in the first couple of days (I had 8-9 riders for these races). So they were intrigued when I gave a speel, and all tried it. typical boys - shirts off and straight into it. And they all LOVED it! Bloody hell, every day my mat was disappearing so they could do sessions on it, or id find someone passed out on it on my floor.. lol .. (i do the massages in my room ,so its kinda an open door policy!)
But the token insomniac is the one that blew me away - it was like he had been drugged, within minutes of getting on it. this was early afternoon - he went and slept for 1.5 hours afterwards, he was struggling to hold a conversation. then, that nite, he crashed out from 9pm to 6am - one of his best sleeps ever! WTF! thats awesome aye! they pretty much all want to buy one now cos they found it so nice.

SO, i will perserve and get better at using it! it is definitely relaxing! I would recommend them, if youre thinking about it..

MG

# Thread: Is your mind open to new things?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-mind-open-to-new-things.10022/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2014-03-05T09:20:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-mind-open-to-new-things.10022/post-113527>

"Those who cannot change their mind - cannot change anything!" JK

Do we have to practise to have an open mind?

The last number of weeks I have been thinking about all the changes in me.....
At first I thought it was the leptin RX and eating tons of shrimp and sardines and raw salmon and raw heart and who can forget the MHS .. and then I added in a bathtub full of ice, and then Jack told me to throw my ipad in the garbage! and then I had to hug a tree or two, and then I had to sleep naked ....
and Then Jack said if I live close to the ocean and didn't go in everyday I needed my butt kicked....
and then, of course, Jack said no lites after sunset and rise with the sun and eat half a cow while you are at it! and lets not forget drinking imported water from the Northern Hemisphere!!
did I forget ....having unlimited oxy - around the clock if possible

The last few weeks .....I was sure it was that my redox potential had improved .... my battery was actually maybe holding a charge and, wonder of wonders, maybe I am actually hydrated with all that expensive, imported bottled water. We also have learned to never, ever ask another water question ......

Today, after reading a Post of Jack's ..... maybe it is something else entirely .....

I have expanded my mind ..... I have taken all of the above on board and thrived....... and that might just be the most important thing that has happened to me

Some might think it is like a lamb being led to slaughter .... she can't think for herself ....where is the koolaid?

How could I ever have imagined that I would love to read QED .... like really give it a go...

Is this why I am happier and healthier and having the absolute ride of a lifetime??? \[and I can wear a mini skirt!\]

Jack said " Intelligence is directly yoked to the ability to change"

Is this what is really happening to us???

I think maybe Dr. Jack kruse snuck this one in on us......... whaddayathink??

### kovita — 2014-03-05T10:15:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-mind-open-to-new-things.10022/post-113533>

Caroline, I think you expressed perfectly the most important aspect of all susscesfull healing approaches - layering. you need to support yourself on many different layers which "normal" people sometimes even do not connect as part of the same entity. The western medicine and lifestyle taught us subconsciously concept of isolation. I see eround me this is the most single obstacle that is lreventing us from major progress. It is happening in the school, at the medical office, in the science and in our daily lifes. As you do, i am as well discovering, that implementing support on each and every level. i discovered in my life, health and environment I am finally starting to progress. I have no guarantee I will ever come back to my former health but my progress on psychological and philosophical level is so striking that I struggle to keep the people around me "on board". I feel the profound split from nature our society achived so quickly extremaly disturbing and it takes a lot of thinking to work up the feelings of "internal anger and fight" with the system and set of laws I was born to. I remember, when I was younger, I was thinking some alternative people really got mad. Even my mother, when she turned to God and alternative practices (such as keeping drinking water in red crystal bottles on sunshine in the garden!), I took her for lost charlatan. I became this charlatan myself now and I wonder why it is so difficult to push through people's rectangle minds simple concepts and simple solutions intuitively known for millenia by our specie. Living through this all I am sure now it is the split from nature induced and supported by the system we have to live in. We should organize sometimes some real life getting together, if this would be somehow organizable!

### caroline — 2014-03-05T10:58:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-mind-open-to-new-things.10022/post-113537>

I love what you wrote ^^^^^ Thankyou

Dr. Kruse and Misty have tried to organise a meet and great a couple of times .......but a few people said some really stupid things and Dr. K. pulled the plug.

Jack has all the patience in the world and will keep answering our questions - until we finally get it - or whatever it takes .....but if you question his motives - he is done.......and rightly so!

### caroline — 2014-03-05T11:26:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-mind-open-to-new-things.10022/post-113539>

I have spent a lot of time today thinking about negative thoughts.......... It seems to me if we spend most of our time concentrating on all the negative things in our life we are doomed to ill health and unhappiness - no matter what protocols we follow, no matter how much money we spend on supplements.......

All of us have plenty of things in our life that we wish could be different or better or just plain gone.....
but, when all is said and done, isn't it happiness and joy and wonderment in everyday simple pleasures that turns it all around?

When we can see all the wonderful things in our life - no matter how simple - and just be darn glad to be so very alive. Just to watch your child playing in the dirt or to hold a loved one's hand or hug someone you treasure or to just smile at an elderly person and check that they are okay or see a sunrise or a beautiful sunset or enjoy the rain and thunderstorms like i have this afternoon.

Is this way, way too simplistic?

Yesterday when I was waking to the shops - I stopped to ask an elderly lady if she was okay - she had stopped and sat on a ledge. You would have thought I had given her a million dollars.......to think someone actually cared if she was okay ......we chatted for awhile about all the stupid hills and the weather and whatever else ..... and we both had a big smile .... win win for both of us!

### kovita — 2014-03-05T12:03:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-mind-open-to-new-things.10022/post-113545>

Caroline, this is great! I can tell you I know that I may have lyme and million other ugly bugs living in my body, but I do not differ in this sense from most of other people and from my husband for example. I know I got sick during the time I was a living zombie. My second child was born and I was living in a place I did not like, I was living a life I was not sure about and than my younger child got seriously sick and have spent more than year in my living nightmare thinking only about one damn thing - will my child survive, will he recover, will there be life lasting consequences for him, will any other terrible kick come from any side any time any soon? I lost sence of humour, I lost interest in life, I lost interest in my children I lost interest in my husband, i lost interest in myself, I was deeply and utterly unhappy denying all love I was being given. I know lyme played the opportunistic role in my disease. I think JK privided me with a way and support which has exact meaning and base and the real healing force is now starting to come slowly from my own recovering soul. I actually recently started to feel happy at least sometimes and I can connect again with my family and I started to forgive things I thought I would never forgive. I am yet to forgive myself, but I am on it! Some people are blessed by stable internal drive to choose the good over the bad, I was alaways the anxious type and it does not come easy to me. I spent one great year in Tanzanian game reserve. It is really difficult to express this powerfull healing experience. i felt i am at home and I am sure I was. I do admire in this sense my husband. He just goes thrupugh the life and finds joys everywhere. I know I get angry on him because his life seems so terribly light. He embraces evrything in its vaste size and I get terrified by the vaste size of everything. I would never be thankfull enough to JK for the transalation of philosophy into exact science, this was exactly what my brain needed. I know I have everything i need to get well around me, it is there waiting for my to learn how to access it and make a use of it.
here is a photo of my son, he is growing into smart, happy and strong boy!

### caroline — 2014-03-05T12:15:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-mind-open-to-new-things.10022/post-113547>

OMG Kovita ..... what a beautiful child you have! please show us more pictures - everyday when you post - share your treasures with us please. I also need to see a picture of this wonderful husband of yours - this man that finds joy everywhere! what treasures you have......
My Dad was born outside of Vienna.
You probably know this ... Dr. Tim Jackson is a hormone expert and Lyme.
are you a gold member?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-05T15:33:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-mind-open-to-new-things.10022/post-113579>

A mind that is open to new things will be stretched and will not return to its original dimensions.

### Lahelada — 2014-03-05T15:38:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-mind-open-to-new-things.10022/post-113582>

Absolutely. Historically we sent tradesmen to walk about Europe to learn new things.The adage travel broadens the horizon has its place there too.
More recently travel has come to us and seems to have an interesting effect.
[http://www.dailymail.co.uk/sciencet...ally-diverse-areas-makes-people-tolerant.html](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/sciencetech/article-2572281/Living-ethnically-diverse-areas-makes-people-tolerant.html)

### sooperb — 2014-03-05T17:03:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-mind-open-to-new-things.10022/post-113596>

Audrey, you are a fully fledged Jack Kruse groupie  I mean that in the nicest, possible way.

I like to think I have a wide open mind. I have embraced many new things in my life, some positive for me, others not. Along the way you meet many doubters, those who can't look into the future and see better things might be possible. Insecure people who want to deny you the joy you are finding. They haven't just closed their minds, they have closed their hearts, I am sad for them. I think as a species we are composed of many variables, thinkers, doers, self-destructers, innovatists, inventors, challengers and more. Weaving your way across the path of many you may find yourself, picking up a little knowledge here, a little knowledge there until you have constructed your whole, if you ever do, I feel it's a never ending journey. We who open our minds continue to evolve and are ecstatic that we do. We find joy and happiness but also meet sadness and despair. If your mind is open you can draw on your experiences to help with slaying dragons and climbing your own personal mountain. I wouldn't change my journey, there has been hard times, good times, much joy and many tears, for others and for myself. Go back to white bread and margarine when I can have butter and and pure food? Not me.

If anyone wants a helping hand, mine is extended to them, but please don't take it and then slap me hard because you couldn't make it happen for you. Let it go graciously and allow me move on to someone whose life I may be able to make better.

### caroline — 2014-03-06T01:17:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-mind-open-to-new-things.10022/post-113667>

Beautifully said Soo....your post made me realise why I get so bored around old friends.
They haven't moved on , they haven't move forward - they are perfectly content to sit and eat cake and wash it down with a milkshake.
They look at me with my bottled water ....and feel sorry for me....that is the saddest thing of all. I am sitting there glowing with life and I can't contain myself .......

My friends are all much fatter than they were a year ago and sicker .....one has prostrate cancer.

I think I should be helping some how - but they don't want my kind of help....

OH NO ....a Jack kruse groupie .......I could try to change back I guess ...... to a sad, overweight and unhappy person who couldn't care less about QED and not really living. BUT - Do I have to give away my mini skirts and skinny jeans tho ?

Nope .... not going to happen........

A "groupie" has really horrible/sad connotations.......

but it is what it is ..... my apologies to everyone.....

### caroline — 2014-03-06T08:06:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-mind-open-to-new-things.10022/post-113727>

Just to be clear ..... I can't be a groupie ....sorry Jack ... but in my defence - I do know the real deal when I see it.

I have to set a few things straight ..... Jack told me to throw my ipad in the garbage ..... I did not

and I don't do deep C/T - in fact lately - I only do the ocean.... so sorry

I don't eat oysters nearly enough .... they make me throw up every once in a while.

I do walk around naked a little to often ..... and I do hate underwear...... but I will never run thru the forest naked like Peter and sleep under a tree ....but who knows - that could happen I guess

I will probably never chop a hole in the ice to do C/T like Sarah and Inger - but I guess who knows about that as well.

It seems ..... I am just me .....and now - I am really, really okay with that and that is what I have learned from Jack!

This took a long time coming ...... and maybe wasn't what JK intended ......that might not be in the quilt?

### Albert83BCN — 2014-03-06T09:26:47+0200

### sooperb — 2014-03-06T14:19:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-mind-open-to-new-things.10022/post-113758>

"I will never run thru the forest naked like Peter and sleep under a tree"

We all have our limitations, I couldn't stand up and sing in public but I love singing and wish I could. Perhaps groupie was the wrong word Audrey, it's easy to see that Jack has had a profound effect on you and many others too. I admire his guts for sticking his conventially trained medical neck on the block while he pours forth all he's learnt that stands nutrition and health care on its head, changing the world one person at a time. I'm glad I found my way here, some things are meant. I've travelled many miles across the internet looking for answers and this site is the closest I've come. I try to spread the message but it's surprising the resistance you encounter. I wrote a message on a fitday forum, this morning I received notification of a response. The poster has used my id with "not" tagged on the front! He/she has tried to rubbish everything I posted, taking issue with my saying that exercise alone cannot burn off weight. Hey ho, when he started talking about BMI I lost interest in his response. Not one link to any support for what he said, I suspect a personal trainer or a government trained nutritionist who just can't let go of the dogma that was their training.

I was even more galled this morning, on the magazine programme that has become the BBC News, they had some woman, I didn't catch her name. She spoke about the perils of sugar (hurray) and then said ............. "Of course if you're not eating sugar you could be eating more fat, that would be even worse. What you should be doing is eating more wholegrains, pasta and brown rice, and replace the sugar with fruit and vegetables". National TV, peak time and someone still promoting nonsense. Thank God for Jack is what I say.

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-06T15:15:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-mind-open-to-new-things.10022/post-113762>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=113758)
>
> He/she has tried to rubbish everything I posted, taking issue with my saying that exercise alone cannot burn off weight.

Anyone who does that simply doesn't know what he or she is talking about. In the past, the people who actually do know something even if they have recommended that have added "that's what we think, but admittedly there's no evidence to show that".

But this study should have finally knocked this superstition on the head:

> In this study, we used the doubly-labeled water method to measure total daily energy expenditure (kCal/day) in Hadza hunter-gatherers to test whether foragers expend more energy each day than their Western counterparts. As expected, physical activity level, PAL, was greater among Hadza foragers than among Westerners. **Nonetheless, average daily energy expenditure of traditional Hadza foragers was no different than that of Westerners after controlling for body size**.

[http://www.plosone.org/article/info:doi/10.1371/journal.pone.0040503](http://www.plosone.org/article/info%3Adoi%2F10.1371%2Fjournal.pone.0040503)

That's July 2012 -- a year and a half ago. But they don't know about it.

This is the problem: people have these fixed ideas that are literally immune to evidence.

### Martin — 2014-03-08T17:27:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-your-mind-open-to-new-things.10022/post-114158>

As a dyed-in-the-wool science geek from an early age, I looked to the Newtonian model of reality as THE way of explaining things. That is, until I ran up against unexplainable occurrences like how our nervous system transmits signals, and that science couldn't explain biochemically why we don't feel the signals instantly. And what about intuition and prediction? And homeopathic remedies, acupuncture, etc.? Woo stuff to the max?

Perhaps not.

Now that I see how QED theories seem to be explaining these mysterious things, I feel that for the first time in my inquisitive life, there are answers to the big questions of Life. I'm more relaxed, more inquisitive, less judgmental, and more willing to stand before someone and speak to the questions that come up about Life, all without thinking I have to bash them over the head with what I have seen. The Truth is bigger than my ego, and being able to stand among the naysayers with the knowledge, and, more importantly, the _experience_ of how Nature is running the show, has me in a place that is far beyond the dark ages of my past.

You can have a lengthy conversation about swimming, complete with charts, graphs, and discourse, but until you jump in the water, you don't know Jack.

NSH

# Thread: (Book) Decoding the Human Body-Field: The New Science of Information as Medicine
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/book-decoding-the-human-body-field-the-new-science-of-information-as-medicine.10023/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Albert83BCN — 2014-03-05T10:58:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/book-decoding-the-human-body-field-the-new-science-of-information-as-medicine.10023/post-113536>

Today I found out this book on Google Books while looking for some info on cell metabolism and stuff like that. I've been quite impressed to find out that it talks a lot about some of the hot topics that we cover here, and the author seems to realize the role of phyisics on the human body and mentions a lot quantum physics. It also presents the data in very undestandable way (as far as I have read). Here's an small excerpt:

_"The human body-field is a self-organizing, self-directed, intelli-
gent system that directs the information flow of the body, information
that is crucial to the genetic, chemical, and physiological processes of
the body. To understand the NES model of the body-field you have
to unlearn biology, in a sense. You have to put aside your focus on the
diagnosis of disease based on symptomology. Symptoms are a conse-
quence of the breakdown of biochemistry. However, chemistry is driven
by the interaction of subatomic particles and waves. The root cause of
disease, as most complementary practitioners will tell you, is at the level
of the energy and information of your body."
_​

I see a lot of comments on JK podcasts and blog entries about the work from people like Robert O. Becker or Pollack about water, but can't recall having heard about this book. I wonder how much of his thinking might be influnced by Frasier & Massier and some of these people that call this the nutri-energetics system (NES)? as it is massively tied.

​

### Albert83BCN — 2014-03-05T11:01:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/book-decoding-the-human-body-field-the-new-science-of-information-as-medicine.10023/post-113538>

Oh great, here's the full book:
[http://www.hekli.se/wp-content/uploads/2012/11/Decoding-the-HBF.pdf](http://www.hekli.se/wp-content/uploads/2012/11/Decoding-the-HBF.pdf)

# Thread: can detox present itself as paresthesia?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-detox-present-itself-as-paresthesia.10026/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kovita — 2014-03-05T15:09:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-detox-present-itself-as-paresthesia.10026/post-113576>

guys, I am neverending source of questions! I happen to get one of my old lyme bothers more often now. Better said I had a lot of paresthesias in feets and hands and big no blood feelings in my hands during the sleep, the worst during my initial doxycyclin tratment. This eventually got much better over the time. Now after lets say 2 months of consistent CT I seem to be unevitable going to be one of the dominant problems again, it is more is is affecting the whole right leg, that is new. It came back slowly and it is definitely intensifying. Any thoughts? Should I get back to neurologist asking for brain/spine MRI ( a year back it was completely clean)? Should I just stay calm and assume it is part of recovery or cleaning? Anyone with similar experience?

### Lahelada — 2014-03-05T15:31:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-detox-present-itself-as-paresthesia.10026/post-113578>

How about similar but different? I used to wake up with numb hands , found things difficult to grip . I suspected night shades ,arthritis you name it. Nomoney to throw at the problem so I just did what i could namely a very high fat diet. I have s avery faint stiffness in the joints of the hands now but the symptoms disappeared as soon as ketosis took hold. If you are not living ketogenically it might be worth looking into. Fibro at least from what I remember responds well to it.
If you have not read these yet
[http://jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-4-metabolic-syndrome/](http://jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-4-metabolic-syndrome/)
[http://proactivemed.org/881/center-...kruse-md-readers-summary1-why-is-lyme-misund/](http://proactivemed.org/881/center-for-proactive-medicine-facebook-wall/amazing-read-written-by-jack-kruse-md-readers-summary1-why-is-lyme-misund/)

### kovita — 2014-03-05T15:34:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-detox-present-itself-as-paresthesia.10026/post-113580>

I am in and out of ketosis for a few months now. It seems like it is not a big player for me :-( I starteda. diary to make some sense of it and even with some proper analysis, ketosis is not implied in my paresthesias. Thanks for your thoughts!

# Thread: Meat and exercise are "the key to aging well"
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meat-and-exercise-are-the-key-to-aging-well.10027/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-03-05T15:37:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meat-and-exercise-are-the-key-to-aging-well.10027/post-113581>

> Protein and muscle-building exercise could be the recipe for staying spritely in old age, according to a new study.
>
> The protein and exercise combination may reduce the risk of falls and improve mental function, say Deakin University researchers.
>
> A first phase of their study shows marked muscle-building benefits for women aged 60 to 90 who ate a diet high in lean red meat and did strength training.
>
> Those on a high-protein meat diet achieved an 18 per cent greater increase in muscle strength than a control group who ate normally. ...

[http://health.msn.co.nz/healthnews/8806547/ageing-meat-eaters-pack-on-muscle-study](http://health.msn.co.nz/healthnews/8806547/ageing-meat-eaters-pack-on-muscle-study)

Rather begs the question of what's meant by "normal". Anyway, good to see some sense in this debate.

### Tanya — 2014-03-05T21:25:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meat-and-exercise-are-the-key-to-aging-well.10027/post-113637>

another study: [http://www.washingtonpost.com/natio...f0603e-a3b5-11e3-8466-d34c451760b9\_story.html](http://www.washingtonpost.com/national/health-science/too-much-protein-could-lead-to-early-death-study-says/2014/03/04/0af0603e-a3b5-11e3-8466-d34c451760b9_story.html)

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-05T21:54:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meat-and-exercise-are-the-key-to-aging-well.10027/post-113642>

> [Tanya said:](/goto/post?id=113637)
>
> another study: [http://www.washingtonpost.com/natio...f0603e-a3b5-11e3-8466-d34c451760b9\_story.html](http://www.washingtonpost.com/national/health-science/too-much-protein-could-lead-to-early-death-study-says/2014/03/04/0af0603e-a3b5-11e3-8466-d34c451760b9_story.html)

Yeah. That one's doing the rounds now.

Usual caveats apply. It's a questionnaire for a start. Andreas Eenfeldt has some pertinent criticisms. Someone also remarks wittily in his comments that:

> Just the average of _"1,823 calories"_ a day for the USA, ought to start cautionary bells ringing about how reliable this data could possibly be...

[http://www.dietdoctor.com/dangerous-eat-meat-age-65](http://www.dietdoctor.com/dangerous-eat-meat-age-65)

Mind you, I do wonder if the occasional low-protein day (or day of total fasting) could be a healthy practice. As I understand it, it should encourage autophagy. Established practice in Orthodox countries, like Russia, of course, isn't it? In Western Europe we kind of gave up fasting gradually -- back in the Middle Ages they said fish counted as fasting, then that various birds and whatnot counted as fish, then dropped out most of the seasons for fasting, and finally dispensed with it altogether after the Reformation in Protestant countries. Maybe not a good idea ...

### Tanya — 2014-03-05T22:27:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meat-and-exercise-are-the-key-to-aging-well.10027/post-113648>

Being strict vegetarian was one of the diets that I tried at one time...I just pretty much ignore all these studies these days. So liberating to follow the diet I am on now and not to have any cravings

### caroline — 2014-03-06T02:30:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meat-and-exercise-are-the-key-to-aging-well.10027/post-113688>

Having no cravings and no hunger beats the crap out of anything else ..... in my opinion of course!

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-06T11:39:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meat-and-exercise-are-the-key-to-aging-well.10027/post-113744>

> [Tanya said:](/goto/post?id=113648)
>
> Being strict vegetarian was one of the diets that I tried at one time...I just pretty much ignore all these studies these days. So liberating to follow the diet I am on now and not to have any cravings

Nor hunger as Audrey says.

As Taubes points out, it's an apparent paradox that you get hungrier eating carbs than eating nothing. I can't remember when I was last hungry. It wouldn't bother me if I did miss a meal -- I don't think I'd even notice -- but I enjoy Epi-paleo food so much I usually don't.

As you imply, one can't run around like a headless chicken every time one of these "studies" comes out. It's just an observational study, anyway. I wish the media would put these things in the bin when they're sent them. It was a questionnaire for goodness sake! As someone points out at Andreas's blog this (surely mythical) average consumption of 1,823 kcals/day worked out on average to 51% of carbohydrate and 16% of protein. So, he says, they blame the protein! They know what they want to to "find" before they start is what it amounts to. They also added in some data on mice. As an Epi-paleoist I'd prefer data on Great White Sharks, but never mind ...

I did the arithmetic.

1,826 x 16% = 292 kcal

There are 4 kcal per gram of protein

292 / 4 = 73 grams

So 73 g of protein a day are going to give me cancer? I'm in trouble then: I should think I regularly eat 100 to 110g a day and sometimes more. Except I'm not.

It's balderdash! We know that medical missionaries in the nineteenth century -- people like Schweitzer -- said that there was virtually no cancer among native peoples eating their traditional foods. It came in with what Weston Price called "the displacing foods of modern commerce".

They're just putting out rubbish science. Apart from the fact that you can drive a bus through the massive holes in their assumptions, what they say can be immediately refuted by looking at other sources of evidence. I think people in the biological sciences are in severe danger of becoming regarded as clowns by real scientists. I haven't forgotten the physicicst who remarked to Robert O. Becker, when a biologist had had an experiment demonstrated to him and wouldn't believe it because it went against his preconceptions, "Biologists are the worst."

# Thread: THE MTHFR TEST FOR YOU TO CARRY
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mthfr-test-for-you-to-carry.10028/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-05T18:59:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mthfr-test-for-you-to-carry.10028/post-113616>

HOW DO I KNOW IF MY DOCTOR CAN TREAT MTHFR WELL?

Dear All: I have had several recent requests for a doctor knowledgeable about MTHFR in or near a certain town. As I have done for all my beloved patients and friends in the past....here's the 411!:

MTHFR or Methylation has become an industry buzz word....and everyone wants on the bandwagon! Often, patents are tested only for MTHFR (C677T and/or A1298C) and uninformed, inexperienced physicians are prescribing Deplin, 5MTHF, or the like with no knowledge or consideration of the other pathways. Frequently, patients react negatively to the aforementioned and I get the above stated request.

Treating MTHFR without investigation of the other single nucleotide polymorphisms (snps) is a "crap shoot" at best. At a minimum investigation of the Detox pathways, methylation/transufuration pathways, Kreb's/ATP pathways, as well as COMT, MAO etc. together with a good history and consideration of other lab tests is necessary to properly perform nutrigenomics (the optimization of biochemical pathways with nutrients). I think you get the idea....so, let me make this light and amusing with your kind indulgence my friends! Presenting the Top Ten (five, really) reasons to not treat with a health care provider who claims to "do MTHFR/Methylation":

5\. Keeps calling MTHFR the "Mother Father" gene or worse.
4.. Cannot explain what MTHFR does and how it leads to certain symptoms
3\. Has a bewildered look on his face when you show him the pathways that you copied from Dr. Yasko
2\. Conversationally mentions other pathways but breaks sweat when you ask him which of your snps are involved (since he hasn't tested them)
1\. You produce a list of snps and mention a few and watch him turn different colors when you ask him what they mean (FYI ladies, there is nothing more frightening to a doctor than a woman with a list---Tip: position yourself between him and the door. If his office is higher than the 2nd floor....he's trapped!)

DRUMROLL.........The quickest phrase that will set you screaming into the night and out of his office....

"There is no scientific evidence that these other snps have anything to do with you health, dear. Besides, it's all in your head"

### Shijin13 — 2014-03-05T19:27:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-mthfr-test-for-you-to-carry.10028/post-113619>

HAHAHAHA so true about the List things. the VA Docs really hated that when I came in with those, or current Journal articles.....

### caroline — 2014-03-06T02:36:46+0200

The cardiologist said if we wanted him to treat Doug we had to toss the supps. he said it was him or them......

we had waited 12 weeks for that appointment....

# Thread: New member with Hep-C liver damage question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-with-hep-c-liver-damage-question.10029/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### russell graves — 2014-03-05T20:44:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-with-hep-c-liver-damage-question.10029/post-113635>

Hi to Jack and to all of you,

I was diagnosed with Hep-C in 2001 and went through two long rounds of interferon - ribavirin therapy. The virus is now undetectable and my liver function tests have been in the normal range for 10 years, but I am left with stage 3 liver fibrosis. I became interested in a ketogenic type diet for many reasons but recently read in the Hep-C Primer that the extra work load on the liver required to metabolize the high fat and protein could be severely dangerous to my health. Could anyone here advise me on my best course of action? Thank you all in advance for your time and attention.

Russell

### sooperb — 2014-03-05T21:35:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-with-hep-c-liver-damage-question.10029/post-113639>

Welcome Russell. I can't help but I'm confident someone will be along who can. There's a lot of knowledge between these "walls".

### caroline — 2014-03-06T01:45:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-with-hep-c-liver-damage-question.10029/post-113675>

Hi Russell ... and welcome!

Please start a journal in the journal section - go to the top left of this page and click on forum - the journal section is in the first categorie - 4th down I think.

You will see "start a new thread" give your journal a name like .....Russell's journal or something more fancy!

and then tell us a little bit about yourself. Dr. Kruse needs context in order to offer some suggestions.

Do you have Dr. K's Book? The Epi-Paleo RX ...from amazon in kindle or paper - great overview of all this and Dr. Kruse

Have you listened to Jack's podcasts? on the home page .... a quick and easy way to get a handle on all this!

Have you joined the free 90 day optimal reset? it has been fantastic...Dr. K. is giving us a huge amount of his time.

what else can I tell you? what can I help you with?

Keep reading and asking questions ... Dr. K. doesn't know what we don't know unless we ask!

Consider becoming a "gold" member .... you get way more bang for your buck! You certainly don't have to tho. there are lots of great people here to help you on this journey

### nonchalant — 2014-03-06T02:33:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-with-hep-c-liver-damage-question.10029/post-113689>

Welcome Russell, glad to have you with us.
Jack has posted something somewhere about Hep C, I'll see if I can find it. I know that years ago, when I first went on a low carb high animal fat diet, yeah, my digestion suffered because of the big change in diet. It took me a couple months for things to settle down. Perhaps that is what the primer is referring to. Or perhaps the primer is figuring that you would be eating lots of peanut butter, mayo, corn oil, cheese, etc.

My liver/gall bladder/small intestine has zero problems now. Perhaps someone with Hep C would have to take it slowly, but I would think in the long run that a ketogenic diet would be much easier on the liver, especially if you got into raw meats.

### caroline — 2014-03-06T02:40:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-member-with-hep-c-liver-damage-question.10029/post-113691>

So - that means eating high fat would be easier on the liver..

Have a look at Ricardo's posts on lipids and ketones ....
