# Thread: Newbie in PA (US) - Jack, youve turned my life around :-)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-in-pa-us-jack-youve-turned-my-life-around.10031/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Steph Corby — 2014-03-05T22:27:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-in-pa-us-jack-youve-turned-my-life-around.10031/post-113647>

Hi Everyone!

Ive been doing Leptin Reset strictly for almost 2 weeks and I am wondering why I didnt do it sooner. Actually I know why- though I have eaten organic for years, I was afraid of myself - afraid there was NO WAY I could give up all grains (including rice!) AND snacking forever.... Amazingly, I havent cheated once or even wanted to - this is first time in my life Ive been through PMS without the help of chocolate  ! Im so glad I didnt bet money against myself - someone would have hit the jackpot on that bet!

A little background: I have been seeing a MD (practicing holistically) for my thyroid condition for about 3 years. Though I was raised to "let the body heal itself" when my condition started in 2009 with a thyroid storm, I thought I was going to die and ended up at an endocrinologist. I kept asking him what I could eat/not eat to help my condition. He swore foods had no effect. After a year of that bs, I knew there was something else I could do. That said, I knew it was time to move along...

I landed at the Holistic MD in 2011 (I went about a year untreated, with mild success using kelp and diet/exercize to support my thyroid - but I had no way to check my T-levels) who put me on Cytomel and sent me for 43 blood tests! Those with concerns get checked every 3-6 months depending on my progress. Supplements include (am) adrenal support, pregnenalone, (mid-day)multi-vit, Res-Q1250, D3, A, B-12 and (pm) phosphadityl serine & cortisol manager.

He kept telling me "first we will focus on getting you healthy, then we can focus on losing weight". Well, I am a bit impatient and have always taken initiative... After reading "Mastering Leptin" ('prescribed' by dr) I asked dr more about it. He handed me a 1-page photocopy with a list of foods to eat and not eat, but no 'rules'!! I tried to eat/avoid what was on the list, but of course it wasnt enough. I think he just assumed being the Google-Queen that I am Id figure the rest out.

Then one day it happened. I had been steadily gaining and getting desparate. I felt like the harder I tried the worse I failed. And every time I THOUGHT ABOUT giving up my gluten-free cookies and dark chocolate almonds, I ate MORE of them !

So I started on another Google adventure. I found that photocopy and at the top it said "Epi-Paleo something else that was cut off" I Googled "epi-paleo' and my life changed from there. I found Jack Kruse's Optimal Health site. I read and read and read about the science/logic of the Leptin Reset. That night I told my family I was "pracitcing" for it because I didnt think I could do it.

2 weeks later I am down the 6 pounds I had gained since Thanksgiving and feeling awesome . Honestly, at first I was to tired I thought I was going to fall asleep at work a few times, but then I added some of the recommended supplements (magnesium malate, iodine, selenium, K2, PQQ and CoQ10) Im thinking of cutting back a bit on the multi (dr has me @ 6/day) and getting the b-Complex.

One question I do have about the supplements is timing- I figured out magensium at night, but not 100% sure on the rest. Also, will I still need the phosphadityl & cortisol manager?

Anyway, this rave is basically to say Im excited to be part of this group and THANK YOU for your work, philosophies, ethics, and transparency with information  Much appreciated, to say the least.

### Shijin13 — 2014-03-05T22:39:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-in-pa-us-jack-youve-turned-my-life-around.10031/post-113649>

WElcome!!!!! great successes so far!!! Ask questions and keep searching for more!

### nonchalant — 2014-03-06T04:09:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-in-pa-us-jack-youve-turned-my-life-around.10031/post-113706>

Steph, what is the cortisol manager? If it is adrenal support, I think Jack would not approve. If we mind the light cycles, seeing morning sun and have low light after sunset, our cortisol will adjust without "support". Jack has said several times that the adrenals are just fine, it is the brain signals to the adrenals that are messed up, and you can't fix that with supplements.

My drs theory is that it helps relax you when you need to settle down and then the pregnenalone in am helps you wake up until your body starts doing it naturally. I have always preferred "light in the daytime and dark at night" so that is not much of a change for me just something to be more conscious of (as I sit in the sunroom this sunny morning typing this)

### kovita — 2014-03-06T15:20:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-in-pa-us-jack-youve-turned-my-life-around.10031/post-113764>

I think i managed my cortisol by accepting the light cycles into my life and using orange gogles when necessary (out of home in the evening etc.). i have no labs to confirm, but i feel it pretty well. pregnenolone was not good in my case, it only started to elevate my BP. When I took a look on the picture of the steroid hormone cascade after i noticed the BP change, i realized my problem is most likely favouring synthesis of cortisol on the price of other hormones on the cascade and pregnenolone was just feeding more on the beginning making production of even more cortisol posssible. yhat was my experience! I tried before eleutherococcus which probably helped me with the fatigue, but I took it only for a short time and in quite low dose...than i stopped and realized I do not need it to keep awake and fresh thorough the day.

### Steph Corby — 2014-03-06T16:20:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-in-pa-us-jack-youve-turned-my-life-around.10031/post-113772>

oh! also wanted to mention that the above was prescribed based on labs, not just theory . Thats the only reason I hesitate to stop it. Though in listening to the podcast, i think I can at least cut back while I work harder on eliminating as much blue light as possible.

After listening to the podcast, I have some changes to make ! Lol- starting with removing the 2 wireless routers in my bedroom! I do have the f.lux program on my computer for awhile now, and I'll be making sure (with 5 kids) its on every computer in the house! I work in IT for a publisher of health and wellness books and magazines, so those kind of things (along with ergo/stand-up monitor lifts) are a standard here. I know, rare stuff in the US, but this company is special. Also, our cafeteria is 100% organic. That goes a long way.

### caroline — 2014-03-07T02:01:15+0200

### Steph Corby — 2014-03-07T03:14:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-in-pa-us-jack-youve-turned-my-life-around.10031/post-113885>

@[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) Well, the routers were in my office, but we werent getting signal through the whole house, so I moved it. But I am going to move it into the sunroom and start turning it off at night. My one daughter is in cyberschool so she needs access and I need it for work sometimes, but if its close we could hard-wire to it.

Other than that, I do just have a bed and dresser in the bedroom (and a papasan chair and a chameleon). My hubby has been bugging me to get the black-out curtains and he loves to sleep in the cold. Of course he has the metabolism of a 15 year old and weighs 145lbs. Maybe thats why LOL! Guess Im going to give in and make him happy . One caveat about the curtains though. As I said, we live in the woods. There are no streetlights and very few cars go down the road. I liked having no curtains because the sun woke me up in the am. Its dark at night naturally (other than the moon) bc there are no light sources outside. Does that count for anything?

As for the meter, I live in the woods so we dont usually have access to the latest and greatest. (I had to Google it and Ive never even seen one of those things!) We do have an energy guard and run on solar during the day. The solar app tells us how much we use because it shows how much we have stored and then when its gone, but nothing about EMFs. :-( One good things is we do not have close neighbors and thus no wifi signal to be found other than my own. The microwave is barely used - mainly to heat up coffee. Ive always hated microwaves - they destroy most food - so I could likely convince the fam. that we can toss it. Wow. So much to think about!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-07T05:53:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-in-pa-us-jack-youve-turned-my-life-around.10031/post-113914>

If we are listening to our thoughts clearly, we all harbor one fiercely held aspiration for our countries health. It should aspire to keep us healthy. Think about that today. Without our health, does anything else really matter?

I have learned people's definition of health frequently is being pain free thanks to the magic pill the insurance company bought...not my definition of individual responsibility. And the former definition may be the very thing that decides future elections. Its time for you and the world to change.

We are more often scared and frightened than damaged from our current beliefs about our circumstance; and yet, we suffer more from the lack of our imagination than from reality of our current circumstance. Life is a canvas furnished by nature laws and embroidered by our own imagination.....In the world of our imagination, all things exist and belong.

Everyone sees the world differently. Your brain is bombarded by 10 million bits of information per second, you only process 40 bits. Your brain filters the other millions of bits through your beliefs. You see what you believe. You and only you control what you believe.........and remember that people who resist change tend to buy beliefs over benefits.

### Steph Corby — 2014-03-08T22:22:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-in-pa-us-jack-youve-turned-my-life-around.10031/post-114200>

I do have a stream with a spring in the back (lucky me!) I actually had to dip in last weekend to get some river rock to fill a hole in the driveway (all the other rock is under 2ft of snow!) It was COLD and I got the pink skin on my hands so I tried dipping my face in. WOW. That wasnt happening for 10 minutes but it was quite refreshing and I could feel my body warming up afterward.

Its just so cold up here this winter that its hard to warm up after CT . Thought I would wait til spring to brave it but the stream is a good idea, it stays pretty cold until summer and even then its way colder than the air. Ive been trying to go out early (7-20 degrees out in the am lately) to let the chickens out without a coat on, wondering if that makes any kind of difference.

Also when I cleaned 2ft of snow and ice off the trampoline (that was a trick, let me tell you!) I got soaked to the skin and my skin was dark pink all over. That was about 20 mins x twice bc my husband made me come in at one point. Does that count?

### jib — 2014-03-08T22:39:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-in-pa-us-jack-youve-turned-my-life-around.10031/post-114202>

It's all adds up, water has better conduction than air, but even one degree less than your temp has a benefit, at least that's what I recall being said here..

### caroline — 2014-03-08T22:40:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-in-pa-us-jack-youve-turned-my-life-around.10031/post-114204>

Your hubby made you come in? it does count - especially if it was for oxy!!!

You are doing a lot of great stuff for yourself - keep it up ..... it all makes a difference. I love how you just jump in and do things! Cleaning snow off a trampoline would be interesting!

You are so lucky with that stream .....

Great attitude!

### nonchalant — 2014-03-10T23:33:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-in-pa-us-jack-youve-turned-my-life-around.10031/post-114610>

> [Steph Corby said:](/goto/post?id=114200)
>
> I do have a stream with a spring in the back (lucky me!) I actually had to dip in last weekend to get some river rock to fill a hole in the driveway (all the other rock is under 2ft of snow!) It was COLD and I got the pink skin on my hands so I tried dipping my face in. WOW. That wasnt happening for 10 minutes but it was quite refreshing and I could feel my body warming up afterward.
>
> Its just so cold up here this winter that its hard to warm up after CT . Thought I would wait til spring to brave it but the stream is a good idea, it stays pretty cold until summer and even then its way colder than the air. Ive been trying to go out early (7-20 degrees out in the am lately) to let the chickens out without a coat on, wondering if that makes any kind of difference.
>
> Also when I cleaned 2ft of snow and ice off the trampoline (that was a trick, let me tell you!) I got soaked to the skin and my skin was dark pink all over. That was about 20 mins x twice bc my husband made me come in at one point. Does that count?

This sounds great, Steph! Most of us have such a fear of being cold. It takes a while, but you can learn to be confident of your body's ability to stay warm. As Jack said, our skin is the thermostat for the body. When your skin gets cold, the body realizes that more heat would be a good thing... Unfortunately with all the warm, attractive winter clothing we all have, only small amounts of our skin (mostly on the head) ever feels the cold. I think for the face you need to get it in very cold water, but at first, for the rest of your skin, going without a coat is a great introduction to CT. If it is sunny, you might be pleasantly surprised at how warm your body can get with bare arms. Much warmer than with a thin long-sleeved shirt.

### Steph Corby — 2014-03-05T23:39:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-in-the-life-of-a-contemporary-cavegirl.10032/post-113651>

Hello out there.

Im Steph. Ive been reading up on this fun stuff for a few weeks and decided to give it a go almost 2 weeks ago. Leptin Reset, that is . Hardcore. After all, Im desparate.

Honestly. I didnt think I could do it. In fact, I was _sure_ I couldnt. I mean seriously, HOW am I supposed to give up chocolate?? Chinese food? No way! Surely my cravings will overtake my feeble mind and i will cave (no pun intended)

But I havent. Amazingly. I havent even WANTED to. My motivation? Aside from the FACT that this stuff _works,_ Ive lost 6 pounds already. No, seriously, you dont understand... I tried (what I thought was) EVERYTHING. But every time I thought about never having my gluten-free cookies again, I ATE them, lol !

But it seriously works. And that is why I believe in it and will continue through the reset verbatim.

### Steph Corby — 2014-03-12T17:27:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-in-the-life-of-a-contemporary-cavegirl.10032/post-115020>

Day 18 on LR - I gained a pound back :-(.

So my first thoughts are "What!!? how did that happen? Ive been doing everything right!"

Am I not eating enough - Im hitting the BAB protein @ 50g ?? (getting about 1500 cals/day - only know bc I use FitDay to track protein & carbs and it gives you calories too)

Am I eating too much at lunch & dinner? (not a crumb after 7pm!)

Are my supplements right? Should I be exercizing yet? CT or no CT this early in? (I do try occasionally)

Too many EMFs (I am unplugging the modem/router at night - and getting better sleep) ?"

Then I think, "ok, relax Steph. Its one pound. Stressing it will not help."

Plus - I FEEL better. Still a tiny bit of fatigue (partially bc I need to get to bed earlier) but I generally feel better.

And one ODD thing. Prior to starting LR, my hips had been popping out of place (sacroilyac) DAILY - pain so bad I could barely get dressed - EVERY day for at least 3 months. And its gone. I just noticed it last week that it has been gone since about day 5.

Not sure if there is a correlation - and what that would be - whether its the diet or the supplements or both or even the lower EMF, but I like it

### sooperb — 2014-03-12T17:37:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-in-the-life-of-a-contemporary-cavegirl.10032/post-115024>

### Steph Corby — 2014-03-12T21:34:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-in-the-life-of-a-contemporary-cavegirl.10032/post-115052>

Well, case solved. It was the sweet potatoes. I pulled the data from FitDay and did an analysis on what I had been consuming versus when the weight gain occurred.

It was the sweet potatoes. Apparently I ate them 2 days in a row, and then gained the pound. Didnt realize until I looked at the data how many CARBS they contain!

Even though my protein also went up, its apparent that on the days that I had higher carbs (i.e. sweet potato), I had a gain the following day. I know its common sense, but Im glad there is logic to it.

this makes me wonder more : how much protien/fat/carbs should I have in a given day/week? I know the protein s/b 50g for breakfast, but what about the rest of the day? Any guidelines?

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-13T08:21:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-in-the-life-of-a-contemporary-cavegirl.10032/post-115132>

On what Audrey said -- here's an old thread that just got resurrected. See post #7 from Jack in it:

> Protein is a gateway drug for a sugar burner to becoming a full fledge PPP mammal..............you are all learning about the magic of the Quilt.

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/big-fat-bab.8189/#post-95475](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/big-fat-bab.8189/#post-95475)

So I guess if you're no longer much of a sugar-burner, you don't need so much of the "gateway drug" and are OK with less protein and more fat if that's comfortable for you.

I'd not worry about number-crunching too much. I don't know that you know for sure that the sweet potatoes were responsible -- weight can fluctuate a bit anyway -- but if they were then there's your answer. What I mean is: you don't need to spend a lot of your time with FitDay trying to hit a specific number of carbs, knowing which foods to go easy on will get you close enough.

I'd suppose that this time of year something like 50 to 60 grams of carbs would be appropriate for a day's intake. But you're not going to get above that if you tend to stick to above-ground vegetables like leafy greens and salads and avocados. (That's not to say that a small serving of sweet potato or small piece of fruit now and then is going to put you over.) It's the food choices that count, and measuring gets to be a pain.

As for protein for "the rest of the day", which you also asked, again you can get there without precise counting of macronutrients in FitDay. Many people eating low carb would take something like a piece of meat the size of a deck of cards for each meal; with fish you'd go a little larger. (That'd work out to something like 20 to 30 g of protein.)

But if you have quite a lot of protein-rich foods with breakfast, then perhaps take a somewhat smaller piece with lunch (if you eat lunch) but a little more in the evening. Jack himself, from what I gather, tends to only eat in the morning and evening and will have either nothing or something more like a snack midday.

### Steph Corby — 2014-03-18T18:49:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-in-the-life-of-a-contemporary-cavegirl.10032/post-116221>

Well, Im in week 4 and havent lost additional weignt, but havent gained either (I re-lost the pound I gained) though Im not steppng on the scale as much. However, still feeling great and gaining more and more ENERGY every day.

Started Official BPC (using Upgraded coffee) and that eally keeps me going all day - I was slumping around 2pm each day but now sustaining.

Also doing some "Tier 1" CT - not ready to try bathing in ice til the weather gets warmer, but doing face and occasional dips in the stream - also much less afraid of cold weather in general (thats why CT has been a fear for me, I HATE being cold in any way - probably bc my thyroid has made me feel cold most of my life. I always thought of myself as more like a lizard- preferring sunning on a hot rock over a walk in the cold! )

Anyway - keeping up with the LR rules but I have had some urges (not exactly cravings and DIDNT give in) to eat something sinful. But I know it was stress-related. Prior to LR, I had a bad habit of munching on a Kind bar or dark chocolate almonds while figuring out a complex issue at work. The crunchier the better, like I was on a mission, lol! Had an urge to do that a couple days this week, but didnt give in - I hope I dont cave!!

### Steph Corby — 2014-04-04T16:02:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-in-the-life-of-a-contemporary-cavegirl.10032/post-119773>

Well, at some point in week 5, I hit 10 pounds lost. I rarely weigh myself now, just doesnt seem as important as feeling good .

Now mid-week 6, I have the family more conscious of EMFs, grounding and they are all coconut oil freaks. Two of my daugters do oil-pulling, I havent tried it yet, and my youngest (17) decided that the way to like healthy foods is to eat them until the gets accustomed to them and convinces herself she likes them. Pretty smart kid. She sat at the table for an hour with an avocado last night, refuses to add anything to it to make it taste better - says she wants to like the avocado itself. (personally I could eat them right out of the shell all day) Her migraines are less frequent due to her change in habits and that is motivating her to try harder.

As for the earthing, I had purchased a set of mats for the car, mainly because they were the least expensive option. Not sure if I am deriving the benefits Id expected, but wanted to give it a whirl. I suspect that sleeping grounded fares much better. Guess at some point I will fit that into the budget. In the meantime, I have a French Drain to redig across about 2 acres of property. Ive been doing it by hand (I mean literally by hand) for a couple of reasons - one for the earth-touching and two because the old guy who built it had a good system with small rocks in the middle and large ones on the outside, and I just have to pull the big rocks out along with globs of leaves, twigs and muddy compost, and the small rocks are still there. Its actually amazing to watch it come back to life and fill with water as I unclog it . My husband is funny bc he bitches about how swampy the property is and at the same time how I spend an hour or so every day unclogging that drain. When I am done the land will not only dry up, but our pond (that it drains to) will come back to life as well!

Im curiou to see how the poison ivy will affect me this year. I never got it my whole life until around the same time as thyroid issues...then I would get it I swear by walking past it or petting the dogs. I know I am digging up the hairy roots working on the french drain. I slather my hands in coconut oil when I come in, and so far, no itching...

### Inger — 2014-04-08T15:12:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-in-the-life-of-a-contemporary-cavegirl.10032/post-120376>

> [Steph Corby said:](/goto/post?id=119773)
>
> Well, at some point in week 5, I hit 10 pounds lost. I rarely weigh myself now, just doesnt seem as important as feeling good .
>
> Now mid-week 6, I have the family more conscious of EMFs, grounding and they are all coconut oil freaks. Two of my daugters do oil-pulling, I havent tried it yet, and my youngest (17) decided that the way to like healthy foods is to eat them until the gets accustomed to them and convinces herself she likes them. Pretty smart kid. She sat at the table for an hour with an avocado last night, refuses to add anything to it to make it taste better - says she wants to like the avocado itself. (personally I could eat them right out of the shell all day) Her migraines are less frequent due to her change in habits and that is motivating her to try harder.
>
> As for the earthing, I had purchased a set of mats for the car, mainly because they were the least expensive option. Not sure if I am deriving the benefits Id expected, but wanted to give it a whirl. I suspect that sleeping grounded fares much better. Guess at some point I will fit that into the budget. In the meantime, I have a French Drain to redig across about 2 acres of property. Ive been doing it by hand (I mean literally by hand) for a couple of reasons - one for the earth-touching and two because the old guy who built it had a good system with small rocks in the middle and large ones on the outside, and I just have to pull the big rocks out along with globs of leaves, twigs and muddy compost, and the small rocks are still there. Its actually amazing to watch it come back to life and fill with water as I unclog it . My husband is funny bc he bitches about how swampy the property is and at the same time how I spend an hour or so every day unclogging that drain. When I am done the land will not only dry up, but our pond (that it drains to) will come back to life as well!
>
> Im curiou to see how the poison ivy will affect me this year. I never got it my whole life until around the same time as thyroid issues...then I would get it I swear by walking past it or petting the dogs. I know I am digging up the hairy roots working on the french drain. I slather my hands in coconut oil when I come in, and so far, no itching...

I really loved this, Steph
digging the pond sounds like a funny workout and super earthing!
I just love your daughters.... pretty amazing kids! Must be the mom

### Steph Corby — 2014-04-08T19:50:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-in-the-life-of-a-contemporary-cavegirl.10032/post-120402>

Side note on the poison ivy - I was ripping up roots by the armfuls this weekend - a bit daring but figured Id go for the "acid test". My arms were pretty messed up from the wild roses and general mucking around. When I came in, I washed off and applied coconut oil mainly because the mud had totally dried out my skin.

Not one itch. All of the bumps went down, and other than a little residual dirt in my fingernails you'd never know I was wrestling poison ivy all weekend

### Steph Corby — 2014-05-21T03:37:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/day-in-the-life-of-a-contemporary-cavegirl.10032/post-127746>

Just ans update since I havent been on for awhile... been busy in the garden! Lost some more pounds, almost 20 in all. Still keeping with the program, get to do a little CT each day washing off in the stream after gardening. "Weightlifting" consists of 5-gallon buckets of water from stream to garden and back again & again, since the garden is a couple acres away from any other water supply. Also moving 30-40lb rocks across the property for the flower gardens in the front yard.

I added Bee Pollen/Propolis/Royal Jelly (all in one capsule) from Durham Farms - it seems to give me energy and for the first year in my life, no seasonal allergies!

# Thread: Male, 62 yo with 30 year history of IBS and chronic lower right quad pain
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 40
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rick — 2014-03-06T05:29:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-113710>

Dx = hypogonadism, adrenal fatigue, IBS, internal hemarrhoids and diverticular disease. The latest diverticular flare was in August of 2013, Rx 10 days of Cipro and Flagyl plus one overnight in the ER on IV Cipro and Flagyl.

This cleared the diverticulitis but oddly a new symptom appeared shortly thereafter in September 2013; the chronic lower right quad pain (my view) was gone and I developed and still have chronic lower left quad pain (my view) about 45 degrees to the left and halfway between the lower rib and the iliac crest. There is no sharp pain, distension or fever and at the same time I developed severe constipation requiring daily doses of magnesium citrate, sulfate or a mixture of magnesium, potassium and calcium ascorbates to keep this condition at bay.

I have successfully stopped the colon hydrating minerals and supplement with artichoke leaf extract and oxbile before high protein and fat meals. Every week I eat bacon, eggs, egg salad, chicken livers or leftover dinners for breakfast, a salad for lunch with MCT oil, Bubbies pickle juice, greens, seedless peeled cukes, skinned and seeded tomatoes and jicama or radish with a daily avocado. Dinners are beef, pork, lamb with salmon, shrimp, scallops, oysters or mussels 2 to 3 times along with cooked greens and other non-starchy veg. No dairy except Kerrygold butter, butternut squash once or twice per week and a little 90% Lindt chocolate. No alcohol of any kind and I have just started cold adapting again. I wear wrap-around blublockers at night and in the early morning before sunrise. No wifi at night and grounding outside as much as possible now but more of that in the spring and summer of course with copious amounts of sunbathing from April to November.

Cooking and coffee water is distilled and I drink Pellegrino and Aquafina during the day. No coffee or other beverages made from municipal water at home or at work. I have seen a new gastroenterologist who suggested Miralax for the constipation (which I flatly refused to do) and he thought that the constipation was the reason behind the lower left quad pain but I explained that symptom is not relieved by a b.m.

I had also seen a doctor from the Paleo physicians network in Manhattan and she prescribed b-vitamins, fish oil, adrenal support vitamins, 10 mg sublingual DHEA, 1000mg vitamin D and Bioimmersion probiotics with beta glucans. She also placed me on 50 mg Testim gel daily. The beta glucans with probiotics did nothing for the constipation at the time and the Testim simply increased the hypogonadism symptoms after two months with no improvement to libido or erectile function. Obviously I have left her care and have scheduled a consult with Dr. Tim on March 11 but I do have lab results that I ordered on my own and blood and urine testing done through her office, please see the attached file.

### Rick — 2014-03-07T16:21:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-113996>

Hello Caroline, f@h and nonchalant and thank you all for your rapid responses. Please allow me to elaborate so everyone can understand my context a little better:

I am not new to the JK forum, I have been here pretty much from the beginning, before the first takedown and rebuild of JK forum and came here from the Robb Wolf forum. I have read everyone of Jack's blogs new and old incl the RP Rx. I have his book on Kindle, read it twice and refer to it often and constantly search the forum threads, and yes I'm in the optimal reset and am following most of his guest podcasts on other sites etc.

I am friends with Curves64 here and she and I attended U of Western States as cont ed students in 2012 to take Anat & Phys and Intro to Biochem because we both had decided to open our own health coaching businesses. She has already started her biz but I am worried about possible regulations in NJ although right now there are no licensing or credentialing requirements. To cover my ass I enrolled in 2013 at Hawthorn Univ for their MS in holistic nutrition program. The master's degree (I have a BS in Sound Recording Technology from SUNY Fredonia 1980) will allow me to sit for the board certification exam in holistic nutrition given by NANP who the school is affiliated with and then I should have no problem talking to clients openly about diets. This will not happen until 2017 or 2018 at which point I will be ready to retire (I'll be 65-66) from Consumer Reports in Yonkers NY and go full time on my own practice (I will never retire per se but I will and desperately want/need to retire from all corporate employment and commuting).

I think I do understand the EMF situation quite well but I am not an RF engineer; I was a pro audio engineer for 15 years in NYC and gravitated from recording and production quickly into electronic repair, design and fabrication. My partner and I built and installed some high end audio systems based on my proprietary amplifier designs - I won't bore you with the details, but I know my analog and digital electronics pretty well...so the question is what can I do about this issue and I'm looking for detailed electrical engineering type explanations because that is what groks with my technical background and training unfortunately. I did attend Michael's segment on the Opt Reset last week and am thinking of not spending the 200 on the EMF bootcamp but perhaps investing in a consult with him one on one sometime down the road.

So I had spoken with Jack about what he wrote in the RP Rx re: Apple's iPhone modulating the device's RF power in relation to a hidden body temp sensor in the phone. The plan was for me to get Consumer Reports to send me to the EMF bootcamp and then I would write a blog for CR on EMF protection products. Sadly my manager gave notice and is leaving this week so difficult for a 6T like myself to pitch this idea on my own in a union shop where the guild members are already embroiled in a long and bitter battle with management...so that's why I say no bootcamp and invest that money with Michael directly to clarify the EMF questions I want answered...

I was also a personal/private chef (self-taught) for many years in Bergen county NJ and was able to design the menu, cook and serve for 14 guests at a 7-course wine pairing event with Bruce Cakebread of Cakebread Cellars and Rene Schlatter of Merryvale Vineyards. I was dba Chef for the Night and my Paleo/Primal dinner party service was called The Onion Tree Cafe. Those sites are about to go down and I have already opened Evolution for Life, LLC.

So from the first day I found JK's blogs online, I decided I wanted to be a self-styled Paleo nutritionist and that I would have to go back to college. When I read his first blog on osteoporosis, I knew immediately that would be the topic of my master's thesis.

But here's the thing, while I have "hacked" my way into electrical engineering and professional cheffing with little formal training, I'm having a hard time applying the principles Jack talks about and biohacking my own n=1.

This does not bode well for my future business; if I can't gain the skills to biohack myself how will I be able to help clients? My biggest fear is that my MS program will not prepare me to be a really great holistic practitioner. No matter how cutting edge these programs are they do not recognize nor do they teach quantum biology so I look at JK's writings as my graduate level training.

I picked Hawthorn as opposed to Bauman College or the Institute for Integrative Nutrition because they were at least Paleo-friendly, but even they have a lot of CW and other nutritional dogma to sort through. So I truly appreciate what Jack and everyone is doing on this forum and the blogs but the informational format is tough for me to grasp; not so much scientifically but because of my ADD and non-verbal learning disorder - its hard for me to follow what is being said if there are any typos and trying to sort through forum threads to discover the gold nuggets, not so easy for me.

So, I've never done this journal thing before and I never had labs to post before so that's why I'm back here now to get help biohacking and to ask more questions to get the details I am missing. I thank everyone in advance because I will pester you all with dozens of questions and I will track and share my progress with you here, firstly my consult with Dr. Tim on March 11 next week...

Hello Caroline, yes EMF hell, hundreds of cell phones and wifi routers at work, at home we turn it off when not in use but 20 other routers in our neighborhood - yes I need a magnetico most assuredly. $ for that will need to be accrued over time this year but $ for Gold membership, no way, not yet...my goal is that I can join as a healthcare practitioner in 5 years or so. In the car I personally have no EMF locally; no headsets, bluetooth etc but I spend anywhere from 1-3 hours driving un-grounded to and from work depending on the traffic over the damn TappanZee bridge...

What else can you help me with on the forum? I will elaborate ABOVE in my other answer because I got kicked off the internet and didn't finish editing to fully explain my problems, but here is one example from the RP Rx, quoting JK:

"For a low redox potential, we want a low electrical resistance in cells and tissues. Semiconductors are designed to have low resistance by their very nature. A high redox potential robs them of that ability."

I don't understand these statements because in my mind I thought for sure he was saying a high redox potential was good and desirable and here it is now the opposite? So what is this measurement of redox potential (RP)? Is it our cellular charge contained within our aqueous battery (the water EZ, right?) so you want that potential difference to be as high (more voltage) as possible? Or does he mean the redox potential is the actual barrier potential described in biochem re: enzymatic reactions and we really want that barrier potential to be as small (low voltage) as possible so the reaction pathways can occur with the least amount of additional energy expenditure??

This is one example of where I read and reread the blogs and just get more and more confused and go around in circles ad infinitum...maybe it's just me because other folks seem to catch on to what Jack means and are able to apply this knowledge successfully or maybe these are just typos in the blogs...but being very literal myself, it's confusing as hell and uber-frustrating.

So if anyone can help translate this passage, that would be great! In essence here's what I'm asking - is the RP a measure of charge or potential difference (voltage) that indicates how well our water batteries are charged up and therefore the higher the better....or is the RP a barrier potential or threshold that needs to be overcome and therefore we want it to be as low as possible as he seems to be indicating in the quoted passage?

Thank you Caroline!

### Rick — 2014-03-08T21:22:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-114188>

> [fitness@home said:](/goto/post?id=113747)
>
> Rick, could the Cipro be the culprit for the new symptoms? I've updated my "avoid" list at all doctors to include fluoroquinolones. Not taking the chance of being floxed.

Hello fitness@home! Not sure if you mean the idiopathic left quad pain or the constipation but it never occurred to me to blame the Cipro or the Flagyl because I have had about 7-8 outpatient occurrences of diverticulitis over the last 10-11 yrs and the standard of allopathic care is always these two antibiotics to wipe out aerobic and anaerobic bacterium; never before have I experienced this pain on the left or constipation after a round of these admittedly very nasty antibiotics - I don't like 'em anymore than u do!

So you really think these drugs could be related to either symptom? Always for 30 years before I have described my issues as IBS-d with lower right quad pain, in fact the antibiotics usually caused BSS to stay around a 4 while on the meds which was actually a pleasant change and then after the scripts were done the diarrhea would make its regular appearance...So then this begs the question if fluoroquinones are responsible for or associated with these kinds of symptoms, what the hell has changed with the last diverticular flare that made this occur this time and not all the others - I would point the finger at increased EMF exposure except I've been at the same workplace for 8 yrs now so this is very curious?

Hi Rick,
My RF career ended in 1995 not long after public TV lost federal funding. At that time I was an engineer for NH public television, prior to that I was a microwave systems technician in the Air Force. I guess it was a blessing in disguise when I lost my job. I work in IT now so I can’t escape all EMF, especially Wi-Fi. I started to plan my escape, but I haven’t figured out what I can do that satisfies my techie-geek side without exposing me to massive EMF… I am open to suggestions…
You are right about it being a floating ground, floating in reference to earths ground. But as long as it is the only ground up there and there isn’t a second ground source in reference to the first ground to cause a ground loop it is ok. You’re definitely not going to receive the same benefits as grounding bare foot to the earth but you will benefit from being at zero reference to the on board electronics.
No, I don’t fly the big planes, sailplanes and ultralights are what peg my fun meter… seat of the pants flying. When I do fly it is only for a short duration of time, usually under an hour and also it is not too far up from earth.
Bruce

Hey nonchalant! Yes I concur that these drugs are reprehensible but faced with the choice of a diverticulum becoming abscessed and leading to life-threatening sepsis, I never know what else to do when this occurs, except of course to go on a liquid diet, then gradually change to soft smooth foods and then solid food with lots of probiotics.

Anyone know of a protocol to stave off these infections with Lugol's? My understanding is that iodine is a powerful and adaptogenic antimicrobial that could wipe out the bad bugs while preserving the beneficial flora but I think I have not the knowledge or skill to implement this therapy on my own; thus the reliance on very bad antibiotics! I certainly don't plan on this happening ever again (I am completely OCD about avoiding seeds, nuts, skins and peels or anything that could get stuck) and yes the previous gastroenterologist and my primary internist all suggested I do a bowel resection surgery to eliminate the pockets in the descending colon. I even saw a surgeon to discuss the possibility and then one morning I got a call from Valley Hosp saying I was scheduled for this procedure without my confirmation! So I got a second opinion from the chief of surgery at VH in Ridgewood NJ and I followed his advice to hold off on the surgery. Sadly the worst culprit in my experience, the one that landed me in the hospital in Teaneck NJ for five days about 14 yrs ago was a piece of gristle from a strip steak that I accidentally swallowed...newer info online now about diverticular disease suggests it has nothing to do with hard crunchy foods getting stuck and other practitioners claim they can cure or prevent an attack with heavy doses of probiotics, not sure about that...The standard recommendation is a high fiber diet but I disagree and try to stay moderate to low fiber now.

> "For a low redox potential, we want a low electrical resistance in cells and tissues. Semiconductors are designed to have low resistance by their very nature. A high redox potential robs them of that ability."

So what is this measurement of redox potential (RP)? Is it our cellular charge contained within our aqueous battery (the water EZ, right?) so you want that potential difference to be as high (more voltage) as possible? Or does he mean the redox potential is the actual barrier potential described in biochem re: enzymatic reactions and we really want that barrier potential to be as small (low voltage) as possible so the reaction pathways can occur with the least amount of additional energy expenditure??
>
> This is one example of where I read and reread the blogs and just get more and more confused and go around in circles ad infinitum...maybe it's just me because other folks seem to catch on to what Jack means and are able to apply this knowledge successfully or maybe these are just typos in the blogs...but being very literal myself, it's confusing as hell and uber-frustrating.
>
> So if anyone can help translate this passage, that would be great! >
> Thank you Caroline!

You are absolutely right, Rick! I missed that. The passage should read something like this:
For a high redox potential, we want a low electrical resistance in cells and tissues. Semiconductors are designed to have low resistance by their very nature. A low redox potential robs them of that ability. (Or perhaps -- A high resistance robs them of that ability.)

I have sent Jack a correction.

### JanSz — 2015-01-20T17:06:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-153438>

\============================== =====================
**Hemachromotosis Genetic test (HFE Gene Test)**

HEREDITARY HEMOCHROMATOSIS
\_\_DNA Mutation Analysis (UOM )
Hereditary hemochromatosis (HH) is an autosomal recessive
disorder of iron metabolism that results in iron overload
and potential organ failure. It is one of the most common
genetic disorders in individuals of European-Caucasian
ancestry, with an estimated carrier frequency of 10%. HH is
caused by mutations in the HFE gene. Most individuals with
HH (60%-90%) are homozygous for the C282Y mutation. A
smaller percentage of affected individuals are either
compound heterozygous for the C282Y and H63D mutations
(3%-8%), or homozygous for the H63D mutation (approximately
1%).
\============================== ==

If you test positive,
think of maintainin maximum Total Testosterone at no more than 550ng/dL
phlebotomies if Hgb raises over certain level, say 17.

Minimize foods with iron.

.

>
> \============================== =====================
> **Hemachromotosis Genetic test (HFE Gene Test)**
>
> HEREDITARY HEMOCHROMATOSIS
> \_\_DNA Mutation Analysis (UOM )
> Hereditary hemochromatosis (HH) is an autosomal recessive
> disorder of iron metabolism that results in iron overload
> and potential organ failure. It is one of the most common
> genetic disorders in individuals of European-Caucasian
> ancestry, with an estimated carrier frequency of 10%. HH is
> caused by mutations in the HFE gene. Most individuals with
> HH (60%-90%) are homozygous for the C282Y mutation. A
> smaller percentage of affected individuals are either
> compound heterozygous for the C282Y and H63D mutations
> (3%-8%), or homozygous for the H63D mutation (approximately
> 1%).
> \============================== ==
>
> If you test positive,
> think of maintainin maximum Total Testosterone at no more than 550ng/dL
> phlebotomies if Hgb raises over certain level, say 17.
>
> Minimize foods with iron.
>
> .

Greetings from Ramsey and thank you JanSz for your thorough research and biohacking of my labs, appreciate it very much, Rick

### nicld — 2015-01-21T16:16:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-153531>

Don’t know if this will help or not but for years and years and years I had horrible gut issues. Was diagnosed with IBS-C maybe 12 years ago then with celiac. I was sometimes taking 2 capfuls of mirilax 2X a day just to stay going. Had GI’s done and colonoscopies done and they never really found anything. About 2 years ago (just before I found this place), I was realized I had SIBO and got a round of antibiotics from my GI doc and that knocked it down. Since then I have been eating Epi-paleo, lots of fermented veggies and home brewed kombucha and other stuff to work on the gut. But most important I got my circadian cycle way better. Now I am pretty darn regular and no more C and I am happy.

Everyone has their own N=1 but just wanted to say that I think gut issues can be fixed.

### Rick — 2015-01-21T19:27:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/male-62-yo-with-30-year-history-of-ibs-and-chronic-lower-right-quad-pain.10034/post-153542>

Yes it helps very much nicld, thank you! We are quite similar as I now I believe I have SIBO also and this explains the 30 yr history of IBS but I wrongly described it as IBS-d until the last two years, but all along it was paradoxical-d or really what amounts to simultaneous d and c with chronic abdominal pain and feelings of incomplete emptying, along with the classic gas, bloating, distention and GERD. So what I've learned recently is that gut motility is all brain based so I have some physical exercises to stimulate the vagus to talk to the gut enteric system and I will still get the lactulose test for hydrogen and methane. I have scripts for this test plus labs for an iron panel, neutrophilic hypersegmentation index for folic acid deficiency, a full iron panel, plus 25-OH D3 so my plan is to run those labs later to see if ferritin has come down but yes I will run the SIBO test right away...since I now think this has been around for a very long time off and on, I will likely need rifaximin and neomycin as I bet I have hydrogen-producing bacteria and methane-producing prokaryotes. And I must also do the brain exercises; gargling, lightly stimulating the gag reflex w/a tongue blade, loud humming and singing. After the first round of meds, do the breath test and if the gases aren't down, repeat the Abx. When the overgrowth is gone < 3ppm of methane, < 20 ppm of hydrogen, or combined level < 15 ppm, then add some kind of prokinetic, like Iberogast, and/or huperzine A, or galantamine so the SIBO does not replapse...I learned all this recently from Drs. Siebecker, Sanberg-Lewis, and Kharrazian on Sean Croxton's digestion sessions.

Thanks for your support nicld, appreciate it very much and yes like you I also need to work on light cycles again. I was doing really well with light in 2013 and part of 14 but got out of the habit as I thought it wasn't doing much for my health but I was wrong wasn't I?

So I think I will stay all-vegetables for awhile yet and like I said, see if that brings down the iron labs; then I can go back to epi-Paleo too w/ more seafood, lots more veg and just less iron-rich meats and less often. Glad you are feeling so much better now too, chronic GI problems suck don't they?

TTYL and take care,

Rick

Hi everyone, so I have been eating tons of seafood prepared with bacon fat and cooked veg with 1lb KG butter/week and it has been suggested that any weight loss efforts will be blocked by the imbalance of firmicutes to bacteroidetes that was discovered on Dr. Tim's referral to do the GI Effects 2200. The solution for this, because there is no such thing as bacteroidetes probiotics that one can take, is to go on an all vegetable diet which I tried for several weeks but now I'm learning this F/B ratio is very slow to change and it may take 3-6 months to reverse and some say to avoid animal fats because those feed the firmicutes...Jack or anyone out there, do u have some definitive info on this conundrum because I want to go back to the LR diet and I've decided I need to do it all with seafood but how can I get keto and burn bellyfat if animal fats promote the firmicutes in spite of large amounts of non-starchy veggie fibers to grow the bacteroidetes - all coconut or palm fats, cooked veg at every meal and 80% fat with no more than 20% total carbs and protein??

Help, any troubleshooting ideas, because it appears the gut flora are blocking my efforts but the fix for that is the antithesis of the LR diet, what to do to reverse this situation, Jack, anyone plz??

Thanks to all,

Rick

R

PS - low redox still, poor adrenal output, weight stuck at 190 now, eating all fish protein, very low carb and very high sat fat (bacon grease & KG butter), no liquid oils except mct or c8 with mostly cooked green veg, sm veg salads at lunch and paying attention to light with red bulbs, blublockers, and sunrise exposure while grounding, been soaking in local quarry for 2 weeks now, early to bed early to rise...

# Thread: bldy voltage meter grounding help
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bldy-voltage-meter-grounding-help.10036/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kovita — 2014-03-06T10:27:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bldy-voltage-meter-grounding-help.10036/post-113736>

guys, after the yesterday's webinar I am angry, schocked and ready to take steps. I did some preliminary research this morning and it seems like I have no company around to do the measurements for me. body voltage meter seems to be the best straightforward approach in my situation. I live in Vienna (Austria) in city center in the second floor of a typical townhouse from turn on 19/20.century. i am not skilled to use the fuse and the administrator of the building is not able to confirm the water tubing is made from metal in it's whole lenght. My house is surrounded by pavement and concrete. Some windows face patio, where we have big panting pots filled with clay (photo attached). Could I use it as a 0v refference?

### Martin — 2014-03-06T15:25:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bldy-voltage-meter-grounding-help.10036/post-113766>

kovita, I looked at your picture and I have doubts as to whether you can use the planter to establish an ground/earth connection to be able to accurately check any voltage going through your body. I know there is dirt in the planters, and the planters and patio seem to be made of concrete, but my doubt is whether the voltage could travel through the concrete easily to give you and accurate reading. I am comparing it, of course, with actual earth. I have heard that concrete can be okay for grounding with bare feet, so there may be a chance. You would have to do a side-by-side test to see. I suppose a very long wire with a metal rod attached to one end could be run all the way to actual soil would by okay, but I don't know if it's practical in your area.

# Thread: Fish consumption aids in heart health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-consumption-aids-in-heart-health.10037/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-03-06T13:04:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-consumption-aids-in-heart-health.10037/post-113750>

Quite a nice article. This turns out to be a five-year international study and funded by the NIH. The sample isn't large, but it is also clearly stated:

> ... a larger clinical trial would be necessary to confirm those results.

The following seems significant, in one way or the other, and worth highlighting even if it turns out not to be the fish (or not only that):

> It included 175 middle-aged Japanese men and 113 middle-aged Caucasian Americans. Japanese-American men in the study were found to have levels of coronary artery calcification -- the accumulation of calcified plaques in coronary arteries -- proved to be as high or even higher than Caucasian American men, revealing the lower rate of the calcification among Japanese men is not genetically based.

[http://www.post-gazette.com/news/he...-health-Pitt-study-finds/stories/201403050054](http://www.post-gazette.com/news/health/2014/03/05/Fish-consumption-aids-in-heart-health-Pitt-study-finds/stories/201403050054)

# Thread: Losing Weight on the Leptin RX -- Not happening
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-on-the-leptin-rx-not-happening.10041/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 31
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Pamela — 2014-03-06T16:04:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-on-the-leptin-rx-not-happening.10041/post-113769>

Hi All,

I thought I'd write something here just to get some feedback from the group. I've been on the Leptin RX for 2 weeks (this is my 2nd time doing it, did it a few years ago after doing HCG also but have gained back 20 lbs since then). Initially I lost 2 lbs and thought I was on the way to success with losing about 25 lbs that I need to lose. Now it has come back. I'm doing the prescription how Dr. K says to do it.

Breakfast within 30 mins, usually a saturated fat of either beef or pork (grass fed or pastured), little vegetables.

Lunch about 4 hours later is light, with some sort of meat and little vegetables

Dinner is always a 4-5 oz piece of salmon with some vegetables.

I've made some coconut cream candies that I have after lunch or dinner as well as some dark chocolate. I have had some macadamia nuts with a meal now and then too.

No fruit. No nightshades. I have Hashimotos, take NDT.

I feel frustrated that no weight is coming off and I don't really want to go to fish for ever meal.

What has your experience been with the reset as far as time and success?

### Shijin13 — 2014-03-06T16:13:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-on-the-leptin-rx-not-happening.10041/post-113770>

FWIW - I was on the LR for almost 6mths before I actually began to see changes in weight/body composition. The LR isn't about loosing weight, its about fixing your hormones and your circadian cycles. Once those are fixed, you'll see weight loss.

Some people who aren't as out of sync will loose weight on the LR. Some take longer. Go read my Success story My N=1.
I've been at this for 3yrs, and I've been following the Post Leptin Rx. I was doing really well, following circadian eating, eating seasonally, etc. This fall/winter my thyroid went off the rails.... I gained 30lbs in 3mths with no change in diet, or principles. So I'm back to the LR again to re-train my brain. This journey isn't a direct path forward, sometimes you'll feel like you'll one step forward, two steps backwards but its all forward progress....

### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-06T17:16:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-on-the-leptin-rx-not-happening.10041/post-113784>

2 weeks? If the needle does not move by 6 weeks you know your environment is the problem.

### Pamela — 2014-03-06T17:21:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-on-the-leptin-rx-not-happening.10041/post-113786>

Yes, I know it is just 2 weeks but I thought I'd see something change, even slightly. I will just keep doing the program and stick with it.

### nicld — 2014-03-06T21:12:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-on-the-leptin-rx-not-happening.10041/post-113816>

If you have no other hormonal issues then maybe but if you have a bunch of other stuff off, forget about it. When just doing CT and LR did not get the scale moving down, it made me start looking at other stuff and boy and I am a mess.

Jack says diet is only about 10% of the equation.

### nicld — 2014-03-06T21:13:14+0200

So I'm back to the LR again to re-train my brain. This journey isn't a direct path forward, sometimes you'll feel like you'll one step forward, two steps backwards but its all forward progress....

### caroline — 2014-03-07T01:25:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-on-the-leptin-rx-not-happening.10041/post-113858>

I second or third that ...2 weeks????

How mush veg are you having? nuts? coconut cream candies???

My body comp changed and then the weight followed - quite a bit later.... I still had to work at it tho - and continue to do so.

I eat very simply and and really think about it - well not so much now. calories do matter a little if you need to lose lbs.
If I ate bacon and eggs everyday - I would blow up like a balloon...... I stay away from all paleo treats too and fruit mostly .... maybe a tiny bit in season.

My body is still changing and it has been almost three years ....this is a life time journey. I am craving free and not hungry all the time - those are huge wins for me.

I completely understand that you want the weight gone .....but Gretchen and Nicole have told you the cold hard reality ...other things are up in your body.

### Pamela — 2014-03-07T03:55:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-on-the-leptin-rx-not-happening.10041/post-113900>

Let me clarify. I was on the reset back in 2011 for about eight weeks after I finished HCG. I had started to CT for a while also. I think my problem is that I don't give it long enough because after about a month I didn't see any real changes with CT so stop doing that.

I have been off all gluten for at least two years in addition to all grains and do a pretty strict Paleo and at the Paleo diet. I do have Hashimoto's so I stay away from nightshades and fruit.

Caroline, when you say if you ate bacon and eggs everyday you would blow up can you tell me what you do eat? I do have a fairly large appetite but that's what the morning is good for and I do usually eat pork chops or some sort of red meat at breakfast.

I have done tests where I eat no coconut cream candies or any chocolate for several days and I don't really see much change. Even having zero fruit for weeks doesn't make a difference.

Like I said, I do have Hashimotos but I don't have a lot of symptoms. My main one is overweight and I have been for most of my life. It really is the main thing I want to fix.

Tonight I re listened to the epi-paleo webinar and got some good information. I won't give up
Also I have listened to and read ALOT and JK speaks the most truth ... Even if sometimes I have a hard time understanding it all.

Thanks for reading!
Pam

### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-07T05:54:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-weight-on-the-leptin-rx-not-happening.10041/post-113915>

The road to success is a fairly long journey. We all live in blue-lit/microwaved society, so people generally expect to see success pretty quickly. When they do not see the expected results, they quit pretty soon. However, successful people know that great success is achieved by working diligently, day in and day out irrespective of the results, because they understand that long-term success requires hard work, believe in yourself, have faith in God, total commitment towards your dreams and goals. It is a test of one's character, how strong you are internally to keep doing things, even though you do not see the results straight away. Sometimes we need to take three steps backward before we leap forward. It is counterintuitive, but this is how the environment's power densities affect the biologic antenna's inside of you that monitor blue light and nnEMF around you. This is why you can never get well in the same environment you go ill within.

### CTforlife — 2015-02-03T22:23:16+0200

> [Pamela said:](/goto/post?id=114038)
>
> I do have to start the CT up again, it is a hard one for me to confront. I'm going to start with the face dunking, I can do that. I think my gut is pretty good. No adverse bloating, gas, etc. I have decided not to weigh every day which is what I was doing and then trying to figure out what I ate the day or 2 before and adjust. Now, as long as I know I'm following the RX then I know that is the best thing I'm doing. Off to continue my reading.

I started pretty low on the "totem" pole. If you want I'll link my first post and you can see how long I struggled. I persevered like water does to cut the Grand Canyon. I didn't see noticeable results for a while, a while, but I wasn't doing any of the details. Now I'm more detail orientated. I view the more details you get right, the faster the flow of water to cut the Grand Canyon.

Cold thermogenesis works better when you're coupled and cycled to your local circadian rhythm. I would focus on a high sea food diet leptin rx and add cold thermogenesis AFTER you begin to liberate body heat at night naturally. This means the pillars of the house or the foundation of the house will support the roof. Cold thermogenesis is the gas peddle no doubt. It's the "hurry up" button, but just how you can't build a house without a foundation, cold thermogenesis won't be your saving grace until other things are in place.
I'm going to say you need to eat a lot of shrimp or take a kelp supplement. I guarantee your estrogen is sky high so we need to drop it and add iodine to get your fire lit. I highly recommend a kelp or sea vegetable source of iodine so you can really load up. The dha and sulfated Cholesterol will naturally accumulate in your tissues, after you follow some basic steps.
I love cinnamon and suggest it for reasons I don't feel like explaining. Look up what the ancients thought about cinnamon if you really want to know. I suggest it.
Coming from a kid who had no hope, get back on that horse and ride. You can't see where you are going until you get up on that next ridge, then you see farther, but not until you get up on that ridge. You may have to travel in darkness to reach the sun at the top.

If you are into using your brain for things other than your government education would teach you, I would essentially change the way you think and that one step will change your life. Don't look for results, believe they will come and you deserve them. Sounds stupid until you understand what your brain does. As jack said, it works on light. There happens to be a lot wrong with our current theories on light, but I'll give you a hint and tell you to use your head.

# Thread: Otago researchers gain new insights into ancient Pacific settlers’ diet
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/otago-researchers-gain-new-insights-into-ancient-pacific-settlers%E2%80%99-diet.10046/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-03-06T19:50:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/otago-researchers-gain-new-insights-into-ancient-pacific-settlers%E2%80%99-diet.10046/post-113805>

It sounds like a pretty good diet …

> Researchers from New Zealand’s University of Otago studying 3000-year-old skeletons from the oldest known cemetery in the Pacific Islands are casting new light on the diet and lives of the enigmatic Lapita people, the likely ancestors of Polynesians.
>
> Their results—obtained from analysing stable isotope ratios of three elements in the bone collagen of 49 adults buried at the Teouma archaeological site on Vanuatu’s Efate Island—suggest that its early Lapita settlers ate reef fish, marine turtles, fruit bats, free-range pigs and chickens, rather than primarily relying on growing crops for human food and animal fodder. ...

I don't know what fruit bat tastes like, but I'd go with a diet like that!

[http://www.otago.ac.nz/news/news/otago066271.html](http://www.otago.ac.nz/news/news/otago066271.html)

[http://www.plosone.org/article/info:doi/10.1371/journal.pone.0090376](http://www.plosone.org/article/info%3Adoi%2F10.1371%2Fjournal.pone.0090376)

### nonchalant — 2014-03-06T20:22:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/otago-researchers-gain-new-insights-into-ancient-pacific-settlers%E2%80%99-diet.10046/post-113812>

Mike, they taste like chicken...

### kovita — 2014-03-06T21:18:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/otago-researchers-gain-new-insights-into-ancient-pacific-settlers%E2%80%99-diet.10046/post-113823>

I lived with some fruit bats in the very neighbourhood while residing in Southeast Asia. They must taste great, they look so yummy!

# Thread: MsYo's Optimal Journal

### MsYo — 2014-03-07T00:56:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/msyos-optimal-journal.10050/post-113852>

Hi Everyone!

My name is Yolanda (MsYo) and this is my journey to optimal. I've been a member/follower of this forum for a while. I have made great progress with my health and I am on my way to Optimal.

Here is some "context" for my journey......

46 years old 5’7” 215lbs Divorced No children
Gastric Bypass April 2002 – Highest Weight 352lbs (Open RNY)
Fibroids – 2 myomectomies Hysterectomy suggested but refused 2012
Post Gastric Bypass – Used exercise/fitness as therapy from 2002. I ran 2010 Chicago Marathon without training and that probably was the beginning of the decline of my health. Within 1 month after completing the marathon I lost my job. I survived being unemployed for 7 months before I found employment. I work in the Global Logistics/Supply Chain industry. I don’t enjoy my current role at all. I am planning to do a career/employer change in the very near future. I hate my current role. It is a source of stress.

Current health issues are adrenal fatigue,circadian rhythm mismatches, EMF, hormones, and weight. I live in Chicago originally from the South. Past issues with Fibroids seem to no longer be a problem since discovering this forum and dosing with Lugol’s in 2012. I need to confirm with an updated ultrasound but my OB/GYN isn't my favorite since our hysterectomy discussion.
I used HCG off and on from 2011-2013. I didn’t follow the protocol (without cheating) but I could manage to lose weight when nothing else seemed to work. My HCG abuse probably contributed to some of my issues with adrenal fatigue.

Family History
Mother died at age 77 from Breast Cancer. Maternal history of breast cancer. Dad still alive at age 87. Suffers from Rheumatoid Arthritis, kidney ailments, and other aging ailments. Still has good mental capacity. Congestive heart failure and high blood pressure is a family pattern. 6 siblings alive (all much older) with existing health issues (heart, diabetes, arthritis, etc.)

Workout
I love working out (heavy lifting) and bodybuilding. I have recently started adding moderate cardio to my workout routine. The workouts are helping with my body composition and resetting my circadian clock.

Past Labs/Thread
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/2013-lab-results-and-link-to-2011-2012-labs.8588/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/2013-lab-results-and-link-to-2011-2012-labs.8588/)

### Jack Kruse — 2014-03-07T07:26:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/msyos-optimal-journal.10050/post-113934>

Most in paleo believe that reducing elements are made in the PPP while burning glucose/fructose. If anything using the PPP would be most critical in Winter/pregnancy time so where does the glucose come from to support the pathway in these times when they are not present?

Free association time.

During these times it does not burn glucose....it uses the glycerol backbones of FFA for its source of carbon........that is why it is a slow recycling pathway compared to TCA and ATP-CP system. See EMF 4. It is designed to work as a mammal sleeps connected to Earth underground for 4-6 months in cold when it is not cross fitting or power lifting . See, our biochemistry is not catching up with our modern culture........even thought our brain can create way more possibilities......technology and out beliefs is the latest and most deadly mismatch.

This brief post underpins my concerns with GI surgery of any sort (RNY) and if you look in the bariatric literature you will see some disturbing longer time findings in neuro functioning. 40% complication rate with respect to mental functioning long term. I have actually briefly mentioned this here and there and not gone in depth about it because it requires understanding this pathway (PPP) and the ATP recycling issue with protein folding to truly get it (Ling alert)......anyone who is medical profession has been looking at these bariatric sx outcomes of what we are doing and trying to make sense of it. Some trends have disturbed us.......and no one seems to have a good answer. I think I might.

I already figured out that certain HSPs (stimulated by cold) up regulate the main enzyme in the PPP, so the tie in makes more and more sense. I'm really comfortable with Winter metabolism for bariatric Sx patients actually.

I was looking at some recent studies and found glutamate excitotoxicity throws the PPP off in the brain's mitochondria..............now this should be no surprise. Ketosis = water chemistry = better PPP This is all tied to the lactate/pyruvate ratio and water...........that will all come with time on the blog.

I just finished another round of interiews. I'm remaining optimistic on the outcome. I will know within the week the results.

I also just did labs for the 2nd quarter. I'm curious to see what my results are. I've had a few issues with my hormones/weight the past 8 weeks. My period was at 23 days for 2 months and then it was 45+ days before my period started. Maybe I'm moving into another phase prior to menopause. I stopped taking pregnenolone (last labs it was high) and I wonder if that made a difference. I'm slated to do Alcat/Lyme/Pharmasan neuro labs soon. I plan to get those done before I change jobs.

I think adjusting my mental disposition has helped tremendously. I have been expecting something "magic" to happen with a supplement or protocol but the magic happened when I adjusted my mental outlook. I started to feel better almost immediately. I have moments when I feel like I could "slip" back to the doom and gloom/resentment state I fought for so long. Working out has helped me feel better. I know I must be careful with the quantity of cardio (I'm using a coach to get my body in competition shape) and I make sure my sleep is as close to optimal as possible. I still haven't figured out my full EMF strategy as I think that is my biggest problem to deal with. I am prioritizing a magnetico purchase soon.

# Thread: The Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (CFS)/Myalgic Encephalomyelitis (ME) Thread
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-chronic-fatigue-syndrome-cfs-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-me-thread.10052/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2014-03-07T03:01:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-chronic-fatigue-syndrome-cfs-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-me-thread.10052/post-113881>

There seems to be a few people on this forum who have CFS/ME. A quick search of the forums has revealed some posts but I thought it might be good to have a thread of useful resources and Jack Kruse inspired thoughts/truth bombs.

I will kick off this as I found some amazingly honest, funny and intelligent videos by [Howard Bloom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Howard_Bloom)

Bloom was diagnoses with CFS since 1988 and worked with musicians such as Michael Jackson and Prince but has since conquered his CFS after 15 years being trapped in a bedroom. His is now able to do between 360 and 520 pushups without stopping, He walks 3.5 miles a day and gives lectures in Moscow, Amsterdam, Paris, Kuala Lumpur, Seoul, and Chengdu

He recorded a number of video interviews about his CFS
[http://www.healtheo360.com/stories/chapter-1-the-origins-of-howard-bloom/#.UxkFWH8aySN](http://www.healtheo360.com/stories/chapter-1-the-origins-of-howard-bloom/#.UxkFWH8aySN)

Also the [Phoenix Rising forum](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php) has much useful information

There is also a new film called ["Canary in the Coalmine](http://www.canaryinacoalminefilm.com/)" that is looking for support/funding.

I have also used the Poll feature of the new forum to quantify (privately) people's improvements or not.

### SeaHorse — 2014-03-07T04:32:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-chronic-fatigue-syndrome-cfs-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-me-thread.10052/post-113907>

The methylation thread by Fredd and Rich on the phoenixrising forum is a huge source of information and research.

### PaulG — 2014-03-07T11:13:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-chronic-fatigue-syndrome-cfs-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-me-thread.10052/post-113960>

This is Bloom's document on how he overcame CFS

### SeaHorse — 2014-03-08T15:09:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-chronic-fatigue-syndrome-cfs-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-me-thread.10052/post-114145>

Interesting...particularly in the light of Dr. K's blogs. Some of his suggestions in 1997 were way ahead of the curve and some of them made me cringe (try to sleep by listening to radio with headphones on, using cyanocobalamin instead of methylb12)...what a blessing that here we are working with concepts that hold such profound healing potential...light, magnetism, water....Bloom did an amazing job to find his way through what was available and thinking for himself. Thanks for posting that.

Have you looked at the thread on "bains derivatifs".....a version of CT that has an interesting history. People in the CFS world seem to know about CT too.

### Tanya — 2014-03-08T15:44:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-chronic-fatigue-syndrome-cfs-myalgic-encephalomyelitis-me-thread.10052/post-114148>

> [PaulG said:](/goto/post?id=113960)
>
> This is Bloom's document on how he overcame CFS

Thank you, Paul for posting this. However, it did not meet my expectations. Pick up any book about the CFS and you will find a lot of similar and much more complete information about it. I also have the same reaction as SeaHorse about some of the things that he was doing. Finally, he also said that he DID NOT have 2 of the most important symptoms of CFS - brain fog and muscle and joint pain. Makes a huge difference, as far as I am concerned....

### Martin — 2014-11-21T15:47:12+0200

Some Dr. Kruse quotes that I've collected...
_(emphasis mine)_

"The more omega 6 's in your Cell Membranes (CM) the more GABA you need to allow the brain to work. The more DHA in CM and inner mitochondrial membrane (MINOS) the less GABA you need.
